Sinkholes by Caitlyn, Meredith and Margi Southeast Minnesota is the sinkhole capital of the U.S.A. These strange holes in the ground are all over the place. Some have been here for as long as we can remember and some seem to pop up overnight. But we wanted to know, how do these sinkholes form? What did we do? We looked at three different sinkholes and measured their size with a tape measure. Then we stood in the center and stacked some tubes together to measure their height. The last thing we did was sketch what each sinkhole looked like. We also looked around the sinkholes for other features, like standing water and any special kinds of rocks. What did we find out? In the bottom of the first sinkhole, we found a little bit of limestone and a shell fossil. The hole wasn't very deep or wide, but we thought the limestone might be a clue to how the hole formed. The second hole was wider and deeper than the first and there was a lot of wood debris at the bottom. It reminded us of a kitchen sink drain, except this drain led underground. The last sinkhole was huge! It was so deep that we couldn't safely climb into it to measure the depth. We saw a lot of limestone shelves and another drain at the bottom. We explored some more and found that the drain led to an underground cave. From all these sinkholes we learned that limestone and water play a big part in making sinkholes. We figured that limestone layers must dissolve over time and then collapse to form a sinkhole. - You can get to the bottom of sinkholes too. Make your own model. You'll need a medium jar, sugar cubes, graham crackers, soil and water. From bottom to the top, stack layers of sugar cubes, graham crackers and a little soil. The sugar cubes represent the Earth's limestone. The graham crackers are soil, and the dirt is topsoil. Add drops of water to the topsoil, and then watch the limestone slowly crumble. The water will act like rain and will filter to the bottom. The sugar limestone layers will crumble and form a mini sinkhole. - Do you have sinkholes where you live? Why do some sinkholes have trees growing out of them and some don't? Have you passed sinkholes before and never paid attention to them? How far apart are they from each other? - In the old days, farmers filled sinkholes in their fields with garbage, old furniture and cars, but it began polluting their water supply. Sinkholes act like a drain. Rain flows through the Earth's layers, filtering pollution from the sinkhole down in to underground rivers that flow into our drinking water. How else is our water supply affected by pollution? How fast does the supply get contaminated? How would you test this? - Use this earth science investigation as a science fair project idea for your elementary or middle school science fair! Then tell us about it! Skip to main content More Search Options A member of our team will call you back within one business day. Taking your pulse is a way to measure your heart rate. When you take your pulse, you are feeling the force of blood as it's pumped from your heart into your body. You may be asked to take your pulse regularly. Or you may just need to take it when you exercise or when you feel something is wrong. With your first 2 fingers, press lightly on the inside of your wrist, just below the base of the thumb. You should not be pressing on a bone. The beats you feel are your pulse. If you can't find your pulse, try moving your fingers slightly to a new spot. Count the beats you feel in your wrist as you watch the second hand on a clock. You may be told to count the beats for 6 seconds, then multiply that number by 10. Or you may be told to count for a full minute. The number you get is your pulse measurement. It is measured in beats per minute (bpm). A normal pulse is between 60 and 100 beats per minute. The beats should be regular (evenly spaced). Write down your pulse each time you take it. You may be asked to bring your results with you each time you visit the doctor. Newton's law of universal gravitation (UG), added to the three Galilean laws of motion and some other presumptions, was published in 1687. Newton's theory successfully modeled interactions among objects of a size we can see, a range of phenomena now called the "classical realm". Coulomb's law modeled electric attraction. Maxwell's electromagnetic field theory unified electricity and magnetism, while optics emerged from this field. I don't think you can eat those either. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/07/4866AC.cha = = = Title: How to Give a Low-Cost but Thoughtful Wedding Gift Introduction: Weddings can be an expensive affair, both for the couple tying the knot and the guests attending. While it's customary to give a wedding gift, you don't necessarily have to break the bank to show your love and support. This tutorial will guide you through giving a low-cost yet thoughtful wedding gift that will leave a lasting impression without leaving you penniless. Step 1: Set a Budget Key Tip: Decide on a budget beforehand and stick to it. This will help prevent impulse buying or overspending. Aim for a range between $25 to $75, depending on your financial situation and relationship with the couple. Step 2: Consider the Couple's Interests and Needs Guideline: Reflect on what you know about the couple - their shared hobbies, personal tastes, and lifestyle. For instance, if they enjoy cooking together, consider kitchen gadgets; if they are avid travelers, think about travel accessories. By catering to their interests, your gift becomes more meaningful. Step 3: Browse Online Registries Explanation: Many couples create online registries listing items they need or would like to receive as gifts. Check these lists for affordable options within your set budget. Purchasing from the registry ensures that your gift is something the couple wants and needs while helping them avoid duplicates. Step 4: Opt for Personalized Items Benefit: Personalization adds sentimental value to any gift, making it extra special despite its modest cost. Examples include engraved jewelry, monogrammed towels, or custom-made art pieces. These small touches make your present stand out among others. Step 5: Pool Resources with Other Guests Advantage: If several friends or family members plan to attend the wedding, pooling resources allows you to purchase a higher-value item collectively. Discuss potential gift ideas based on the couple's preferences and agree on an amount everyone is comfortable contributing. Step 6: Create Something Handmade Reason: Handmade gifts demonstrate time, effort, and creativity - qualities that often surpass monetary worth. From crocheted blankets to painted artwork or homemade food baskets, handmade presents offer a unique touch that store-bought alternatives may lack. Step 7: Donate to Charities in Their Name Effect: Making a charitable contribution in honor of the couple shows thoughtfulness and generosity. Choose organizations close to their hearts, such as animal welfare groups, environmental conservation initiatives, or medical research foundations. Remember to inform the couple about your donation so they can appreciate this act of kindness. Step 8: Offer Your Skills or Services Impact: Sharing your talents or expertise can prove incredibly valuable to newlyweds. Depending on your skills, you could offer photography sessions, haircuts, gardening services, pet sitting, or home repairs as a gift. Be sure to discuss timelines and expectations with the couple before committing. Conclusion: Giving a low-cost wedding gift doesn't equate to being stingy or unthoughtful. With careful planning, consideration, and creativity, you can provide a memorable and appreciated present that reflects your affection towards the happy couple without straining your finances. For a few isotopes (an atom with the same amount of protons but a different amount of neutrons) such fission can release many neutrons. If those neutrons then hit other atoms, they will make the other atoms split. This can happen again and again. This is called a nuclear chain-reaction, and it can release huge amounts of energy very quickly. The amount of energy released by a nuclear chain reaction is measured in kilotons. One kiloton is the same as the energy of one thousand tons of TNT (trinitrotoluene). This means that a male will be color blind if the single X he inherits from his mother contains the color blind version of the "color blind" gene. A female will inherit color blindness only if she inherits two X chromosomes containing defective (mutant) color gene alleles. In other words, a female needs to inherit "color blind" genes from both parents in order to be color blind. And they thought that this was the moment to try again. Mercedes-Benz () is a brand of cars, trucks, buses and coaches from the Daimler AG company of Germany. The company was once known as Daimler-Benz and is still sometimes called just "Mercedes." Mercedes-Benz is the world's oldest car maker and the cars they make cost a lot of money. The symbol of Mercedes-Benz is very famous. It is a three-pointed star inside of a circle and was designed by one of the men who started the company, Gottlieb Daimler. The three points of the star stand for land, air and sea because Daimler's engines were used not only in cars and trucks but in airplanes and boats. The symbol was first used in 1909. Let's look out here and see if we can find what it was. Title: Understanding Ultrametric Fields in a Fun Way Hello young mathematicians! Today, we will learn about something called "ultrametric fields." Don't worry - it sounds complicated, but I promise it's not too bad once we break it down together! Imagine you have a big bag of jelly beans, and you want to compare them based on their colors. You decide that two jelly beans are "close" if they share the same color. In this case, your bag has an "ultra-metric," which means that distances between jelly beans follow some special rules: 1. The distance between different colored jelly beans is always the same (e.g., 1 inch apart). 2. The distance between identical jelly beans is zero because they're practically indistinguishable (e.g., 0 inches apart). 3. No matter how many times you measure distances between jelly beans using these rules, you won't need a ruler longer than 1 inch - everything fits nicely into this system! Now let's connect this to math: An "ultrametric field" consists of numbers with a similar concept of closeness, following three important rules like our jelly bean example. These ideas come up when working with unique kinds of numbers beyond regular whole or decimal ones. In fact, there's even a theorem regarding infinite sums (series) in these fields: Theorem: In a complete ultrametric field, the infinite series a + a + a + ... converges if and only if lim a = 0. Don't fret over the fancy words here! Just think of it as saying: If you keep adding smaller and smaller jelly beans (where each jelly bean is smaller than the last), eventually, those added jelly beans become so tiny that they don't make much difference anymore. So, you can consider the series as having reached its final value, just like how our pile of jelly beans reaches a certain height after adding enough small jelly beans! Remember, understanding new mathematical concepts takes time, practice, and patience. Keep exploring, asking questions, and enjoying the sweet world of mathematics! Title: How to Enter a New Line on Reddit while Using an iPhone or iPad Reddit is a popular platform where people from all over the world share content, engage in discussions, and build communities around shared interests. When using the Reddit app on your iOS device (iPhone or iPad), you might want to format your text with new lines to make it more readable or visually appealing. This tutorial will guide you through entering a new line on Reddit when using an iPhone or iPad. Step 1: Open the Reddit App Locate the Reddit app on your home screen or within the app library and tap on its icon to open it. The Reddit app has a white background with a red Snoo logo, which represents Reddit's alien mascot. Step 2: Navigate to a Subreddit or Your Post To create a new post or comment on an existing one, navigate to any subreddit by tapping on the 'Home' button at the bottom left corner of the screen, then selecting a subreddit from the list that appears. Alternatively, if you have already found a thread you would like to reply to, simply enter that particular thread. Key Tip: If you wish to create a new post instead, tap on the pencil-and-paper icon located at the top right corner of the Home tab after opening the Reddit app. Choose the relevant community you want to post in, fill out the title and body sections accordingly, and proceed to formatting your text as needed. Step 3: Type Your Text Tap on the comment box under a post or inside a thread to begin typing your response. You can also start writing a fresh post by following Step 2 above. Place your cursor where you desire to insert a new line. Step 4: Double Space to Create a Single New Line When composing your message or post, double space between two sentences or paragraphs to add a single new line. However, this method may cause issues with displaying the new line properly once posted due to inconsistencies across various platforms and devices. To ensure cross-platform compatibility, move on to the next step for creating multiple new lines. Step 5: Use Two Spaces Followed by Return Key for Multiple New Lines After completing a sentence or paragraph, type two spaces followed by hitting the return key. This will produce a consistent new line effect regardless of the platform or device used to view your post. It resembles the classic manual typesetting technique, ensuring better control over formatting. Guideline: Always proofread your comments or posts before submitting them. Make sure there are no errors, typos, or missing information, especially if you have spent time perfecting the layout. Remember that Reddit users value both quality and presentation! Step 6: Tap 'Post' or 'Reply' Button Once satisfied with your newly formatted text containing well-structured new lines, hit the blue 'Post' button at the upper right corner of the screen to submit your contribution to the selected thread or subreddit. For replies, just tap the blue arrowhead icon beside the username to send off your neatly arranged thoughts into cyberspace. In conclusion, mastering the art of adding new lines on Reddit while using an iPhone or iPad requires some practice but provides significant benefits in terms of improving legibility and visual appeal. By following these steps consistently, you can enhance user engagement with your contributions and establish yourself as a thoughtful participant in the thriving Reddit community. [not what Seth said!; 7 sec feeding] Title: How to Add Related wikiHow Articles Introduction: As a member of the wikiHow community, you may have noticed that some articles include a section called "Related wikiHows" at the end. This feature allows users to discover other helpful content related to the topic they're currently reading about. If you would like to contribute by adding related wikiHows to an article, this tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step. Step 1: Familiarize yourself with the wikiHow editing interface. Before diving into adding related wikiHows, ensure you are comfortable using the wikiHow editor. Log in to your account (or create one if necessary), navigate to any article, and click on "Edit." You can practice switching between VisualEditor and Source Editor modes to see which one works best for you. For this tutorial, we recommend using the VisualEditor as it simplifies linking to other articles. Key Tip: It is essential to understand basic formatting rules such as headings, paragraphs, bold text, italics, etc., before proceeding. Review these concepts if needed. Step 2: Identify appropriate related wikiHows. To determine relevant articles, think carefully about the current topic and what other information might be useful or interesting to those who read it. The ideal related wikiHow should share similar themes but offer unique insights or cover distinct aspects of the broader subject matter. Avoid suggesting identical or nearly identical topics since this redundancy does not enhance user experience. Guideline: Ideally, aim for 3-5 related wikiHows per article. Too few may limit discovery opportunities; too many could overwhelm users. Step 3: Open the Relate Article Tool. Once you have identified suitable candidates for related wikiHows, open the Relate Article Tool. To do so, edit the page in VisualEditor mode and position your cursor where you want to insert the list (usually right after the last subheading). Click on the "Insert" button above the editor window, then select "More..." from the dropdown menu. Scroll down until you find "Related wikiHows," and click on it. Step 4: Search for and add related wikiHows. The Relate Article Tool will display a search bar where you can enter keywords related to the current article. Type in a keyword or phrase and press Enter. Available options will appear below the search bar. Check the boxes next to the titles representing the most fitting choices and click "Apply changes." I'd be happy to help create an educational piece based on the given snippet that would be appropriate for grade-school students. Given that the original post involves abstract mathematical concepts like "sets," "real numbers," and "least upper bounds (i.e., suprema)," we will need to find a more concrete and relatable context. With that in mind, let's consider a situation involving collecting coins. --- **Topic:** Adding a Fixed Number to a Set of Numbers Imagine you have a jar filled with different amounts of money written down on slips of paper. Each slip contains exactly one real number, representing the amount of money you could collect. To make things interesting, suppose there is a maximum limit to the total amount of money in your jar - perhaps you promised not to collect more than $100 from your friends. So, even though each individual slip may represent any positive or negative dollar value, when added together, all the values should sum up to no more than $100. Let's define two new jars called "c + Jar" and "c Jar." These jars depend on another fixed number, c, which represents a constant quantity you decide to add or multiply to every slip inside the original jar. For example, imagine our original jar looks something like this: > Original Jar: {$-2, $5, $1, $7, $0, $3, $-6} If we choose c = $2, then adding "$2" to each slip gives us the following results: > c + Jar: {$0, $4, $3, $9, $2, $5, $-4} Similarly, if instead we multiply each slip by "$2", we get: > c Jar: {-$4, $10, $2, $14, $0, $6, -$12} Now, think about finding the largest possible value that can appear in these new jars. This turns out to be simply the original largest value plus the extra constant c that we added or multiplied along the way. In other words: > Largest Value in c + Jar = Largest Value in Original Jar + c and > Absolute Value of Largest Value in c Jar = Absolute Value of Largest Value in Original Jar |c| The reason why this happens is due to properties similar to those described in the original web page snippet. The language becomes easier to understand when using jars and coin collections rather than sets and supremum. However, under the hood, the underlying principles remain quite similar! Title: Cleaning Oil Paints Off a Brush Without Turpentine Introduction: Removing oil paints from brushes without using turpentine may seem challenging, but there are several effective alternatives available that can help you maintain your tools properly while keeping your workspace safe and fume-free. This tutorial will guide you through a simple, thorough process using everyday household items, ensuring the longevity of your paintbrushes and preserving their original shape and texture. **Materials Needed:** 1. Old newspapers or rags 2. Paper towels or clean cloths 3. Dish soap (preferably grease-cutting) 4. Vegetable oil or linseed oil 5. An old toothbrush or brush comb 6. A jar or container with lid (optional - for storing soapy water mixture) 7. Distilled white vinegar (optional - as an alternative cleaning solution) 8. Rubbing alcohol (optional - as an additional disinfectant) Step 1: Remove Excess Paint --------------------------- Before starting the cleaning process, wipe away any excess oil paint from the brush onto a piece of scrap paper, cardboard, or disposable rag. Be sure to do this over a waste bin to minimize mess. Removing excess paint reduces the likelihood of clogs forming within the bristles, making subsequent steps easier. Key tip: Avoid pressing too hard when wiping; instead, gently stroke the brush against the surface to preserve its shape. Step 2: Lather With Soap ----------------------- Next, create a lather by mixing dish soap with warm water in a bowl or directly on the palm of your hand. Submerge the brush into the soapy solution and swirl it around vigorously, working up suds along the length of the bristles. The goal is to dissolve as much leftover paint as possible during this stage. Guideline: Use a grease-cutting dish soap specifically designed to break down stubborn oils, enhancing the cleaning efficiency. Lisa and Raymond's mom said we're eating out tonight. How to do calculations between moles, atoms or molecules, and grams of a substance One of the main problems that beginning chemistry students have is in doing conversions between grams, moles, and molecules (or atoms). Usually, a question will be asked of you in the following form: How many moles are in 22 grams of copper metal? If you're confused by this problem, don't worry. Most people are when they start doing this kind of problem. To make life easier for you, I put together a "road map" which tells you exactly what you need to do to convert between atoms (or molecules), grams, and moles. You should read this picture the same as you would read a subway map. For example, if you want to go from Example: How many moles are in 22 grams of copper metal? In all problems like this, you need to go through four steps to find a solution. The Four Steps to Solving Mole Problems: Step 1: Figure out how many parts your calculation will have by using the diagram Looking at the diagram above, we can see that we are going between grams and moles, which is a one-step conversion. Furthermore, we can see that we need to use the atomic mass of copper as our conversion factor. Step 2: Make a T-chart, and put whatever information the problem gave you in the top left. After that, put the units of whatever you were given in the bottom right of the T, and the units of what you want to find in the top right. In this case, the problem gave you "22 grams of copper" as the starting information. Because this is what you were given, put "22 grams of copper" in the top left of the T. Since "grams of copper" is the unit of what you were given, put this in the bottom right of the T. Since you want to find out how many moles of copper are going to be made, put "moles of copper" as your unit in the top right. When you've done this, your calculation should look like this: Step 3: Put the conversion factors into the T-chart in front of the units on the right. As we saw from the "map", the conversion factor between grams and moles is the atomic mass of copper. Because we measure atomic mass in grams, you need to put the atomic mass in front of the unit "grams of copper". What do you put in front of moles? Whenever you do a calculation of this kind, you need to put "1" in front of moles, like you see here: Step 4: Cancel out the units from the top left and bottom right, then find the answer by multiplying all the stuff on the top together and dividing it by the stuff on the bottom. In this case, you'd multiply 22 by one and divide the result by 63.5. Your answer, 0.35 moles of copper: And that's how you do a one-step problem of this kind! Solving Two-Step Mole Calculation Problems: What happens if we need to solve a problem that requires we not just go from one box in the next in our diagram, but across the entire diagram? Well, it means that we need to do two steps in our calculation. Let's see that "map" again to see what I mean: If we were asked to convert 22 grams of copper to atoms of copper, we'd have to go from one end of the map to the other. Instead of doing a simple one step calculation, we'd need to do a two-step calculation, with the first step going from grams to moles and the second step going from moles to atoms. How can we solve this kind of problem? Well, we start off by doing the same thing that we did in our last example: We had to convert grams to moles before, and we can see from the map that we have to convert grams to moles now, too. To refresh your memory, here's the calculation from last time: In the next step, we do the same thing over again, except that we need to add another T to the T-chart. When you do this, take the units of the thing at the new top left and put them on the bottom right (in this case, moles). Then take the units of what you want (in this case, atoms) and put it in the top right. Finally, put in your conversion factors, which from the chart above is Avogadro's number, or 6.02E23. Since this number refers to the number of atoms in a mole of a substance, we put this in front of "atoms of copper". Again, put the number "1" in front of moles, because we're saying that there are 6.02E23 atoms in ONE mole of an element. When we add all these terms in, we can cross out the units that cancel out, as shown. To get the answer, multiply all the numbers on the top together and divide by the numbers on the bottom. Your answer should then be set up like this: And that's how you do mole problems! Comments, questions, or gripes? This comes from the inner cores of early small proto-planets. The iron found in iron meteorites was one of the earliest sources of usable iron, before humans invented smelting. That signalled the beginning of the iron age. Iron meteorites are easily found, because native iron is rare. 'Cause I was editing with your mom. You are in a spacecraft in outer space. You start hearing all your alert signals going off. Suddenly you notice an unknown object is sucking you in. Guess what? You just got too close to a black hole! How Can You See Something That s Invisible? I didn t see the black hole, you say? Well that s exactly my point. A black hole is black and so is the universe. Any light that hits the black hole will be absorbed instead of reflected. The reason we can t see black holes with just plain light is because the black hole "sucks" all of the light in, so there is nothing to reflect from the black hole. The two main sources of energy astronomers use to detect black holes are gamma rays and x-rays. A gamma ray is a little different from an x-ray because it doesn t shine as far, but it is a little bit more powerful. We use these rays because they can send better images of black holes than regular light can. The scientists make the picture colorful by using a computer. The color in the computers make it so the scientist can see the black hole better, so the scientist can study more about its properties. If the picture was just black and white, you would pretty much see the same thing over and over. If they are different colors you will see a lot of different things. How Black Holes Are Created Try to imagine a star ten to twenty times bigger than our Sun. This is how big a star has to be in order for it to eventually become a black hole. Dead stars in space make black holes. The star dies after creating an enormous explosion, which scientists call a supernova. It creates this massive explosion when it runs out of nuclear gas. Think of a gas fireplace in your house. If it burns out, it means it can no longer produce the fuel needed to produce the fire. Well, it s the same thing for a star except a star will shrink to a fraction of its normal size. As the star gets smaller in size it also gets heavier. A smaller star becomes a white dwarf, while a bigger star becomes a neutron star, or a black hole. Soon the bits and pieces of the star will combine to form the matter known as a black hole. Any planet that passes the black hole will be sucked in because of the black holes incredible gravitational force. Once you see this horizon it will seem that it is moving slowly, but once you get closer you will see that it is moving very fast. In fact, it is moving at a speed of about 186,000 miles per hour. That s the speed of light! The horizon is light bouncing off the gas particles near the black hole. Astronomers think there might be millions of black holes, but we just can t see them. A black hole s gravitational pull is so strong that not even light can escape it. For example pretend, a speck of dust is a planet and a vacuum is a black hole. The vacuum can suck the dirt from a piece of carpet with ease. A black hole can suck a planet from its orbit with ease just like the vacume and dirt. In order to escape the gravitational pull of a black hole, you would have to travel faster than the speed of light. Einstein, through his theory of relativity, says that nothing can go faster than the speed of light, so nothing can escape a black hole. Proving that Black Holes Exist Astronomers have been able to prove that black holes exist only by observing the effect of a star close to the black hole. A good example of a star being affected by a black hole is the star Cygnus X-1. When astronomers look through optical telescopes, they see a huge blue star. (An optical telescope uses light mirrors to magnify stars so scientists can see them.) This star revolves around something invisible (which they think is the black hole) every 56 earth days. Since this invisible star is so massive and invisible, scientist think it is a black hole. Also the way that Cygnus X-1 moves makes astronomers believe that the invisible star is at least ten times larger than our sun. Getting Sucked into a Black Hole Astronomers have often wondered what happens when something gets sucked into a black hole. Some astronomers believe that when a you travel through a black hole you go through a tunnel called a worm hole where you would be "changed into energy" according to Einstein. Then you come out of a white hole. If a white hole exists, scientists think they are a small body in space that sends out lots of energy. Other astronomers believe a worm hole is a portal to other universes. They say that matter changes going through a black hole and creates its own universe. There are also astronomers that think that black holes will always be a mystery. No one can record what goes on inside or outside of a black hole. We can try and try but once you go into a black hole you won t come out. A small number of black holes have been detected, but we haven t learned any additional information on them. Black holes have many mysteries that may never be solved. <http://www.cosmology.berkeley.edu/Education/BHfaq.html> Last visited: December, 2001. Berger, Melvin. Checking a New York State nursing license is an important process that ensures the credibility and qualifications of nurses practicing within the state. This guide will walk you through the steps required to check a New York State nursing license using the online verification system provided by the New York State Education Department's Office of the Professions (NYSED). Step 1: Visit the NYSED Online Verification System website Open your web browser and navigate to the following URL: <https://www.op.nysed.gov/opsearches.htm>. This page contains various search options; select "Profession" from the drop-down menu. From the list of professions, choose "Nursing." Click the "Continue" button to proceed. Key tip: Make sure you are visiting the official NYSED website to avoid potential security risks or misinformation from unofficial sources. Step 2: Read and accept Disclaimer & Terms of Use Carefully read the disclaimer and terms of use presented on the next screen. By clicking the "I Accept" button, you acknowledge that you have understood and agreed to these conditions. Step 3: Select the type of nurse license You will now be prompted to specify the type of nursing license you wish to verify. The available options include Registered Professional Nurse (RN), Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN), and Nurse Practitioner (NP). Choose the appropriate option based on the license you want to check. Guideline: Double-check the spelling of the name when entering it into the search fields. An incorrectly entered name may result in no matches found during the search. Step 4: Enter the last name and first initial Type the last name of the individual whose nursing license you would like to verify in the designated field. Then enter their first initial in the adjacent field. If unsure about the correct spelling or format of the first name, try searching with only the last name. However, this might return multiple results if there are several professionals with similar names. Step 5: Specify licensure status and city (optional) Select the desired licensure status from the drop-down menu - Active, Expired, Surrendered, Suspended, Revoked, or Other. Leaving this set to 'All Statuses' can provide more comprehensive information but may also increase the number of irrelevant results. Optionally, you can further narrow down the search by specifying a city where the nurse practices or previously practiced. Step 6: Initiate the search Click the "Search" button to begin the query process. The system will then display all matching records based on your input criteria. Step 7: Review the search results Examine the search results carefully and locate the entry corresponding to the nurse whose license you intended to verify. Note relevant details such as full name, registration number, license issue date, expiration date, and current status. Step 8: Access additional information (if needed) If necessary, click on the Registration Number link associated with the specific record to access more extensive data regarding disciplinary actions taken against the nurse or any other pertinent details related to their professional background. By following these eight steps, you should successfully verify a New York State nursing license using the online verification system provided by NYSED. Always ensure that the licensed healthcare professionals caring for you or your loved ones possess valid credentials reflecting up-to-date training and competency levels. The synthesis explains how the ideas of Charles Darwin fit with the discoveries of Gregor Mendel, who found out how we inherit our genes. The modern synthesis brought Darwin's idea up to date. It bridged the gap between different types of biologists: geneticists, naturalists, and palaeontologists. oh, it looks like a hat, doesn't it? Anyway Jacinta's climbin' out, and she falls down right on top of the policeman. A proton is part of an atom. They are found in the nucleus of an atom along with neutrons. The periodic table groups atoms according to how many protons they have. A single atom of hydrogen (the lightest kind of atom) is made up of an electron moving around a proton. Most of the mass of this atom is in the proton, which is almost 2000 times heavier than the electron. Protons and neutrons make up the nucleus of every other kind of atom. In any one element, the number of protons is always the same. An atom's atomic number is equal to the number of protons in that atom. That was an attempt at saying tape recorder. Book Club Books A Smart Start Reader Bats by Scholastic 0-590-96960-9 All Aboard Reading Bats Creatures of the Night by Joyce Milton 0-448-40193-2 Bats A First Nature-Fact Book 1-56156-253-0 Bats by Gail Gibbons 0-439-14787-5 Teacher Resource Books about BATS Bats Scholastic Books Grades 1-3 by Robin Bernard 0-590-10617-1 Bats Evan-Moor EMC 535 JoEllen Moore 1-55799-385-8 Bats A Science Discovery Book by Annalisa McMorrow mm2090 (Monday Morning PO Box 1680 Palo Alto, CA 94302 http://www.mondaymorningbooks.com) Another guided reading book activity Stellaluna by Janell Cannon 0-590-48379-X Children will answer the following questions. After reading several bat books we will revisit these questions. The children can record yes/no or true/false. 1. A bat is a kind of furry bird. 2. All bats can fly. 3. Flying foxes eat rabbits. 4. Most bats are blind. 5. Vampire bats suck lots of blood from animals. 6. Little Brown Bats hibernate for the winter. 7. Bats are nocturnal animals. 8. Bats hang by their tails. 9. A bat's wings are covered with feathers. 10. Baby bats hatch from eggs. 11. Bats are very clean animals. 12. Mother bats feed their babies milk. 13. All bats use echolocation to find food. 14. Mother bats find their babies by sight. 15. Some bats catch fish. 16. Bats build nests for their babies. 17. The smallest bat is the size of a bumblebee. 18. People would be better off without bats. Using the book club format I will teach my class about bats (see the book club group section in The Teacher's Guide to Four Blocks, pages 58-61). THIS LESSON is totally based on the example given in the guide using different books. I divide the kids into 4 equal groups. I follow the guide's suggestion of letting the children preview the books for 5 minutes on a book. After the 5 minutes is up, I switch the books. I have found that instead of having the kids move, it is quicker to have the books move. After the kids have seen all 4 books, I have them write their first, second, and third choice on an index card. They are reminded that they can select the other books to read during ssr time next week. The guided reading time today is getting a bit familiar with the bat books then signing up for a book to read. The teacher needs to plan who will be in which group, how to divide the book into three days of reading; get a KWL chart ready for each group; and post the name of the book with the names of the children in a group. Each group has an assigned area. Each group has a marker to record on the KWL chart. Each group has a KWL chart. For the first 10 minutes the groups record as much as they can in the first two columns: What do they Know? and What do they Wonder? The teacher circulates encouraging. The second twenty minutes the teacher explains that the groups have 20 minutes to read the pages in the first third of the book. I use paper clips to clip down the last page they read to the back cover so they know exactly where to stop. The students record what they are learning on their group's KWL chart. The last 10 minutes the groups gather together and share what they are learning as a group. I list "bat" vocabulary on chart paper. I also will list more questions with them to help focus their learning the next day. Repeat day two reading the second third of the books. Repeat day three reading the last third of the books. This is the section I do a little different from the teacher's guide. Important Poems: Children write their own Important Poems based on The Important Book by Margaret Wise Brown. I use this technique often. After we have finished a unit, then I have them write an important poem to show what they remember. An important poem example: The most important thing about a pencil is you write with it. It has lead and an eraser. You write spelling words with it. You write notes to Junie B. with it. You get a new one for 25 cents from the office. But the most important thing about a pencil is you write with it. Basically, the kids pick one fact for the beginning and the ending sentence (same fact) then they list facts in the between. I compare it to an oreo cookie. I give each kid a cookie and the figure out the top and bottom are the same The way The Teacher's Guide to Four Blocks works just as well. I just try to have a variety. I begin today's guided reading with a graphic organizer called a Venn Diagram. I label one circle bats, the other birds, and the middle overlap both. I read Stellaluna as a read-aloud sometime earlier in the unit. The kids hear and enjoy the language and the story. Then as they read with partners today, they can look for facts about birds and facts about bats. I give them a venn diagram of their own to jot notes on. I do not expect super duper independent facts yet. It is just a place to jot down notes. Title: How to Respectfully Read Your Tween Daughter's Diary Introduction: As parents, we want to be involved in our children's lives as much as possible. However, when it comes to reading their diary, it can be a gray area. This article aims to provide guidance on how to approach this situation respectfully and thoughtfully, keeping your tween daughter's privacy and trust in mind while also ensuring her safety. Please note that this should only be done in exceptional circumstances where there are serious concerns about your child's well-being. Step 1: Reflect on Your Reasons Before deciding to read your tween daughter's diary, take some time to reflect on why you feel the need to do so. Are there significant changes in her behavior or mood? Have there been indications of self-harm, bullying, or other harmful activities? If yes, then proceed carefully. Remember, invading her privacy without proper cause could damage your relationship. Step 2: Establish Trust Building and maintaining trust is crucial during these formative years. Before considering reading her diary, consider talking openly with your daughter first. Express your worries calmly and non-judgmentally. Let her know that you're there to support her no matter what she's going through. Step 3: Seek Professional Advice (if Necessary) If your concerns persist after open communication, consult a professional such as a school counselor, pediatrician, or therapist. They may offer alternative ways to address the issue at hand without resorting to reading her private journal. Step 4: Understand Privacy Boundaries Recognize that everyone deserves privacy, even tweens. Reading someone's personal thoughts without permission crosses boundaries and can harm relationships. Therefore, this step should only be taken if there are severe concerns about your child's welfare. Step 5: Choose an Appropriate Time and Place Choose a quiet, private moment when she isn't likely to come across you unexpectedly. Make sure you have enough uninterrupted time to read thoroughly yet quickly since discovering that you've read her diary might upset her. Step 6: Read With Care Approach the task delicately. Skim through the pages instead of reading every single word. Look out for red flags like references to self-harm, depression, dangerous behaviors, etc. Avoid getting sidetracked by trivial matters or normal adolescent anxieties. Step 7: Respond Thoughtfully After reading, process the information objectively. Don't jump to conclusions based on one entry; people often write things they don't mean in their diaries. Consider seeking advice from a trusted friend, family member, or professional before taking any action. Step 8: Address Concerns Directly If you find something alarming, plan how to discuss it with your daughter. Be empathetic, listen more than you speak, and reassure her of your love and support. It's essential to maintain open lines of communication moving forward. Key Tips and Guidelines: - Always prioritize building and preserving trust. - Communicate openly and honestly whenever possible. - Consult professionals if needed. - Respect privacy but act when necessary. - Keep reactions measured and supportive. Conclusion: Reading your tween daughter's diary should never be taken lightly. Use this guide cautiously, remembering always to put your child's emotional health and trust above all else. This is a take apart car that we would like you to play with. Sis is going to throw it to you. An example of positive feedback is the hormone that causes childbirth (when babies are born.) The hormone that causes this is oxytocin. This hormone is made by the pituitary gland. When the baby starts coming out, it stretches the muscle in the cervix (the bottom of the uterus). Nerves in the cervix send a message to the pituitary. This message makes the pituitary release more oxytocin. The oxytocin then causes the muscles of the uterus to contract, or squeeze. This causes more stretching in the cervix. This stretching then tells the pituitary to make even more oxytocin. So levels of oxytocin keep rising until the squeezing or contractions of the uterus force the baby out. [more MOT and Row about the activity] When it's raining what do we use? She did not like to leave any white spots. She also liked to cut her toast into small pieces and eat them one by one. One morning, Sara was making her toast when she heard a loud noise outside. She ran to the window and saw a big truck with a deep hole in the back. The truck was full of dirt and rocks. Sara wondered what the truck was doing. She asked her mom, who was in the kitchen. "Mom, what is that truck for?" Sara said. "That truck is for digging a new pool in our backyard, honey," Mom said. "Remember, we talked about it yesterday? We are going to have a lot of fun in the summer." Sara nodded. She remembered that Mom and Dad had told her that they were going to build a pool for her and her brother. She was very excited. She wanted to swim and splash and play with her toys in the water. Sara went back to her toast. She finished spreading the jam and cutting the pieces. She was about to take a bite when she heard another loud noise. She looked out the window again and saw the truck dumping the dirt and rocks into the hole. She gasped. She saw something shiny and red in the dirt. It looked like her favorite toy car. The one that Dad had given her for her birthday. "Mom, Mom, look!" Sara cried. "My car, my car! It's in the dirt!" Mom came to the window and saw the car too. She frowned. She remembered that Sara had left it outside yesterday and had forgotten to bring it in. "Oh, Sara, I'm sorry," Mom said. "That was your car. It must have been in the grass and the truck picked it up. We can't get it back now. It's buried under the dirt." Sara felt a big lump in her throat. She felt tears in her eyes. She loved her car. She did not want to lose it. She did not care about the pool anymore. She only wanted her car. She dropped her toast on the plate and ran to her room. She threw herself on her bed and sobbed. She wished she had taken better care of her car. She wished she had not left it outside. She wished the truck had not taken it away. She hugged her pillow and covered her face. She did not want to see anything. She did not want to hear anything. She did not want to eat anything. She only wanted her car. Hey I do that [arrange the men]. Do you think that's a bush Linda? In a high voice, still playing Monty Python Title: How to Create a Perfect Bun Introduction: A bun is a classic hairstyle that never goes out of fashion. It's elegant, versatile, and can be worn for various occasions - from casual days to formal events. This tutorial will guide you through creating a perfect bun step by step. Materials Needed: 1. Hairbrush 2. Hair ties (preferably non-slip) 3. Bobby pins 4. U-pins (optional) 5. Rat tail comb (optional) 6. Hair spray (optional) Step 1: Brushing Your Hair Start by brushing your hair thoroughly using a hairbrush. This removes any knots or tangles, making it easier to manage when styling. It also ensures a smooth finish for your bun. Key Tip: Always brush gently to avoid damaging your hair or causing breakage. Step 2: Gathering Your Hair Gather all your hair towards the crown of your head as if you were going to tie a high ponytail. Try to make this as neat as possible; use your fingers or a rat tail comb to ensure there are no loose strands. Guideline: The tighter you gather your hair at this stage, the sleeker your final bun will look. However, if you prefer a more relaxed style, don't worry about gathering every last hair. Step 3: Securing with a Hair Tie Once gathered, secure your hair with a non-slip hair tie. You might need to wrap it around twice depending on the thickness of your hair. Make sure it isn't too tight but also not too loose. Key Tip: Choosing a good quality, non-slip hair tie is crucial. Slippery ties may cause your bun to sag or fall apart throughout the day. Step 4: Twisting Your Ponytail Take your entire ponytail and twist it clockwise until it starts coiling into itself. Keep twisting until it forms a rope-like shape. Step 5: Wrapping the Twist Continue wrapping this twisted rope tightly around its base (where you secured your ponytail). Once it has been wrapped once, continue winding it around itself until you reach the end of your hair. Step 6: Pinning the Bun Now that your hair is formed into a bun shape, begin securing it with bobby pins. Insert these diagonally into the outer layer of the bun, ensuring they penetrate both the bun and the underneath section of your hair. Use enough pins so that your bun feels secure and doesn't move easily. Additional Tips: For added stability, consider using U-pins instead of regular bobby pins. These have two 'arms', which provide extra grip. Also, crisscrossing your pins can help hold the bun more firmly in place. Step 7: Smoothing Stray Hairs Check for any stray hairs or flyaways. If necessary, lightly spritz some hairspray onto them before smoothing down with your hands or a brush. Final Thoughts: Practice makes perfect! Don't be discouraged if your first few attempts aren't flawless. With patience and practice, achieving a beautiful, professional-looking bun becomes second nature. Enjoy experimenting with different variations of this timeless hairstyle! If I had ta go to one place and find all those animals where would it be? Gather Your Best Photos Into One Album on iPhone ============================================== Do you have countless photos stored on your iPhone and want to organize them in one place? Creating an album is a great way to gather your best memories together. This tutorial will guide you through the process of creating an album on your iPhone using the built-in Photos app. By following these steps, you'll be able to quickly access all your favorite photos in one convenient location. **Step 1: Open the Photos App** ------------------------------- Locate the Photos icon (a multicolored flower) on your home screen or in the app library, then tap to open the app. The Photos app allows you to view, edit, and manage all photos saved on your device. **Step 2: Navigate to the Albums Tab** ------------------------------------ At the bottom of the screen, find and select the 'Albums' tab. Here, you can see existing albums and create new ones. ### Key Tips & Guidelines * If you don't see any albums yet, try scrolling down; Apple creates some default collections like Favorites, Selfies, Screenshots, etc. **Step 3: Tap the + Button at the Top Left Corner** -------------------------------------------------- Tapping this button opens a menu with several options, including 'New Album'. Selecting 'New Album' enables you to create a custom album tailored to your preferences. **Step 4: Name Your New Album** ------------------------------ Enter a descriptive name that represents the content of the album. For instance, if you plan to collect pictures from a recent trip, consider naming it after the destination. A clear title makes finding specific albums much easier later. ### Key Tips & Guidelines * Keep names short but informative. Avoid excessive characters or special symbols as they may cause issues when syncing across devices. **Step 5: Add Photos to Your Album** ------------------------------------- Now it's time to fill up your newly created album! To do so, navigate back to the 'Photos' tab at the bottom center of the screen. Scroll through your collection until you find a photo you wish to add. Once located, tap and hold the image thumbnail until a popup appears. Select 'Add to Album', which brings up another window showing available albums. Locate and tap on the album you just created. Repeat this process for every picture you want to include. ### Key Tips & Guidelines * You can also bulk select multiple photos by tapping and holding one photo, followed by tapping other photos while keeping your finger pressed. Then choose 'Add to Album' and proceed accordingly. * Consider organizing your photos based on themes such as people, places, events, or even emotions. It would make retrieval quicker and more enjoyable. **Step 6: View Your Created Album** ---------------------------------- After adding photos, head back to the 'Albums' tab where your newly crafted album should appear alphabetically among others. Go ahead, give it a click! Enjoy revisiting those precious moments neatly compiled into one beautiful memory hub. ### Key Tips & Guidelines * Regularly updating and managing your albums ensures their relevance and usability over time. Make it a habit to review and curate your albums periodically. * Don't forget about sharing capabilities. With iCloud Photo Sharing, you can invite family and friends to view selected albums directly within the Photos app. Title: Coin Stacking a Nickel Spiral Introduction: Coin stacking is an art form that involves balancing coins on top of one another to create intricate structures. One popular coin stacking design is the nickel spiral. This tutorial will guide you through the process of creating your own nickel spiral with clear instructions, helpful tips, and guidelines. With patience and practice, you can master this captivating skill. Materials needed: * 40-50 nickels (preferably new and without wear) * A flat, stable surface * Tweezers or small pliers (optional) Step 1: Understanding Balance Points The 1968 Democratic National Convention had large protests and riots outside the convention. [presumably SIB but no way to be certain] Title: How to Dress Like Tennessee Kid Cooper Introduction: Tennessee Kid Cooper is a fictional character known for his distinctive Western-inspired wardrobe. This tutorial will guide you through creating an authentic Tennessee Kid Cooper outfit, perfect for costume parties or cosplay events. By following these steps, you'll learn about the essential elements that make up this iconic look while also gaining insights into classic Western fashion. Step 1: Choose the Right Shirt Key Tip: Opt for a light-colored, long-sleeved shirt made from breathable fabric such as cotton or linen. Explanation: A staple piece in Tennessee Kid Cooper's attire is his signature white shirt. The long sleeves add a traditional touch and can be rolled up if needed. Choosing a light color reflects sunlight, keeping you cool under harsh stage lights or during outdoor activities. Breathability ensures comfort throughout your day in this outfit. Step 2: Select Appropriate Pants Key Guideline: Go for high-waisted, straight-leg trousers in a dark hue like brown or black. Explanation: High-waisted pants were popular during the Wild West era when function met form. Straight legs provide ease of movement without restricting your stride. Dark colors hide dirt better and complement the rest of the outfit. Step 3: Invest in Quality Boots Key Tip: Look for well-made leather boots with a slight heel and pointed toes. Explanation: Authentic cowboy boots are crucial to achieving the Tennessee Kid Cooper aesthetic. Leather offers durability and style, while the slight heel provides stability when riding horses (or simply standing tall). Pointed toes elongate the appearance of your legs and enhance the overall silhouette. Step 4: Add a Vest or Suspenders Key Guideline: Either choose a vest or suspenders - but avoid both together. Explanation: Both vests and suspenders serve similar purposes by holding up your pants and adding another layer to your ensemble. However, using them simultaneously may appear overly busy. If choosing a vest, select one that matches your pants. For suspenders, opt for plain leather styles rather than patterned ones to maintain elegance. Step 5: Don't Forget Accessories Key Tips: Essential accessories include a wide-brimmed hat, bandana, belt, and gun holster. Explanation: Each accessory plays its part in completing the Tennessee Kid Cooper image. A broad-brimmed hat shields you from sun or rain and adds flair. Bandanas offer versatility; they can protect your neck, face, or even act as a makeshift sling. Belts keep your pants secure and allow room for additional items like ammunition pouches or knife sheaths. Lastly, a realistic yet safe gun holster finishes off the rugged outlaw persona. Conclusion: By carefully selecting each component of your outfit based on our guidelines, you'll successfully replicate Tennessee Kid Cooper's dress sense. Remember, attention to detail counts! Whether attending a themed event or just wanting to channel some old west charm, dressing like this legendary figure promises a unique twist to any occasion. Title: How to Care for Your Pet Rock Family Introduction: While caring for a pet rock family might seem like a simple task, there are many aspects to consider to ensure their health and happiness. This guide will provide you with comprehensive information on setting up the perfect rock habitat, cleaning and maintaining it, as well as understanding your rocks' behavioral patterns. Section 1: Setting Up the Perfect Habitat Step 1: Choose an Appropriate Container Select a glass or plastic container with enough space for all of your pet rocks to comfortably reside. A fish tank or large storage bin would be ideal. The size depends on the number of rocks; allow at least 5 gallons (19 liters) per three rocks. Key Tip: Avoid using metal containers, which can react negatively with the minerals found in some types of rocks. Step 2: Create a Substrate Layer Add a layer of substrate material such as gravel, sand, or small pebbles to create a natural environment for your pet rocks. Make sure the particles aren't too big that they could potentially harm smaller members of your rock family. Guideline: Use about two inches (5 cm) of substrate material for adequate coverage. Step 3: Decorate the Habitat Incorporate elements from nature into the habitat, like driftwood, stones, or artificial plants. These additions offer hiding spots and help mimic the rocks' original environments. Step 4: Provide Proper Lighting Though not necessary for survival, having indirect sunlight or artificial light sources can bring out the colors and textures of your pet rocks more vibrantly. However, avoid direct exposure to sunlight to prevent overheating. Section 2: Cleaning and Maintaining the Habitat Step 1: Regularly Check Water Levels If you decide to include water features in your rock habitat, check daily to maintain appropriate levels and replace evaporated water regularly. Step 2: Weekly Spot-Cleaning Remove any visible debris, uneaten food, or algae buildup from surfaces within the habitat. Use a soft brush or sponge to gently scrub these areas without causing damage to your pet rocks. Step 3: Monthly Deep-Cleaning Empty the entire habitat and clean all components thoroughly using warm water and mild dish soap. Rinse everything completely before refilling the habitat with fresh substrate and decorations. Key Tip: Never use chemical cleaners inside the habitat, as residue can harm your pet rocks. Section 3: Understanding Behavior Patterns Rock families generally don't exhibit active behaviors compared to other pets. Nonetheless, observing them closely may reveal subtle changes indicative of their wellbeing. Lookout for signs of discoloration, cracks, or chips, which could indicate potential issues related to temperature fluctuations, improper handling, or aggressive interactions among the rocks. If noticed, adjust conditions accordingly or seek advice from a geologist or experienced rock care professional. Conclusion: Taking care of your pet rock family is relatively low maintenance but requires attention to detail to keep them healthy and happy. By following this extensive guide, you'll have created a comfortable living space where your unique collection of rocks can thrive for years to come! Hey Sarah he's always going like this. He was so glad that he got to record it all. Copyright 2007 Dorling Kindersley Light has different wavelengths, which we see as colors. The range of wavelengths we see is called the visible spectrum. We separate the colors of the spectrum by DISPERSION. Light waves are just one type of electromagnetic wave. They belong to an electromagnetic spectrum that includes radio waves, X-rays, and gamma rays. The visible spectrum is the only part the human eye can see. To our eyes, the colors in the visible spectrum range from violet at one end to red at the other. The light-sensitive cells in the human eye react to just three types of light: red, green, and blue wavelengths. These are the three primary light colors. If all three types of wavelength enter the eye with equal strength, we see white light. When just red and green light are present, we see the mixture as yellow. Different wavelengths of light blend to produce millions of shades of color. The human eye is able to pick out over 10 million of them-some of which can be shown by a COLOR TREE. The amount of color we see depends on how much light there is. In dim light, we see no colors at all, only shades of gray. When white light shines through a specially shaped piece of glass called a prism, it is separated into its different wavelengths by dispersion. The wavelengths show up as a range of colors called a spectrum. English scientist Isaac Newton first used a prism to disperse sunlight in the late 1600s. Rainbows appear when there are water droplets in the atmosphere and bright sunshine at the same time. The droplets act like tiny prisms, refracting and reflecting the sunlight, and dispersing it into the colors of the spectrum. To see a rainbow, you have to be standing at a particular angle to the water droplets and the Sun. A color tree is one way of grading or classifying colors. Using a color tree, it is possible to describe and then match a particular shade of color (of paint or fabric, for example). This guide will take you through each stage of this process, providing in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines. By following these steps, you'll be well on your way to achieving a successful and sustainable sales future. Step 1: Connect (Building Rapport) ---------------------------------- ### Explanation The first C in 4CCC represents connecting with potential clients by building rapport and trust. It involves understanding their needs, pain points, and goals. The primary objective at this stage is to create a positive impression and establish common ground between you and the prospect. ### Key Tips & Guidelines * Research your prospects before reaching out to them. Understand their industry, company size, role, and any recent news or updates related to their business. * Personalize your initial communication based on what you have learned during research. Address their specific challenges and demonstrate genuine interest in helping them solve problems. * Use active listening skills throughout conversations to ensure you fully understand their perspective. Paraphrase and summarize their statements to confirm comprehension. * Show empathy towards their situation and validate their emotions. Avoid interrupting them and maintain eye contact when meeting in person or via video conferencing. * Establish credibility by sharing relevant experiences, case studies, testimonials, or endorsements from satisfied customers within their industry. Counting Music with the Pie Game: A Comprehensive Tutorial ========================================================= Introduction ------------ The Pie Game is an educational tool used by musicians and music teachers to help students learn rhythmic patterns and subdivisions. The game involves dividing a pie into various slices representing different note values, which can then be combined and rearranged to create complex rhythms. This tutorial will provide you with a detailed guide on how to count music using the Pie Game, including key tips and guidelines for mastering this technique. Understanding Note Values ------------------------ Before diving into the Pie Game, it's essential to understand basic note values and their corresponding durations. Here are some common note values: 1. **Whole Note (semibreve):** Lasts for four beats in 4/4 time signature 2. **Half Note (minim):** Lasts for two beats in 4/4 time signature 3. **Quarter Note (crotchet):** Lasts for one beat in 4/4 time signature 4. **Eighth Note (quaver):** Lasts for half a beat in 4/4 time signature 5. **Sixteenth Note (semiquaver):** Lasts for a quarter of a beat in 4/4 time signature After I put your plate in it. In one of her earliest publications, 'Reform or Revolution?' (1900) Luxemburg accepted Marx's argument that capitalism promoted exploitation and was at odds with humanity's natural, fraternal instincts. She also agreed that evolutionary socialism was impossible: only revolution could create real change. Volumes of 3D Shapes - A Fun Activity with Toilet Paper Rolls! Hey kids! Today we are going to learn about volumes of 3D shapes in a fun way using toilet paper rolls. You must have seen these tubes lying around at home. Let's imagine them as thin hollow cylinders. Now, let's explore how we can find out the amount of space inside these tubes or any other similar shaped objects like cans or pipes. Imagine cutting the tube vertically and then laying it flat on a table. What do you see? It looks like a long rectangle, right? The height of this rectangle would be the length of our roll (let's call it h) while its width will depend on the thickness of the roll (we'll label it r). So, the area of this rectangular strip gives us the surface area of the curved side of the cylinder. But what about the volume? Volume refers to the total space occupied by the object. In the case of our toilet paper roll, there are two parts to calculate - one being the inner empty part (hole) and another outer part (the rolled up paper itself). Interestingly, both follow the same formula because they're essentially identical shapes just varying slightly in size! Now here comes the exciting part! We don't actually need to measure the hole in the middle since when calculating the whole roll's volume, it gets included automatically. That means all we require is the roll's length (h), thickness (r), and the constant value Pi (approximately equal to 3.14). Here's the magical formula: V= *r *h This stands for Volume equals Pi multiplied by radius squared times height. Let's say your toilet paper roll has a length of 15 cm, a diameter of 7cm; first, calculate the radius (diameter divided by two): Radius= Diameter/2 = 7/2 = 3.5 cm Then multiply it with itself (radius square), then by the height (length), and finally by pi: V 3.14*3.5 *15 769.65 cubic centimeters So, your toilet paper roll occupies approximately 769.65 cubic centimeters of space! Wasn't that cool? Remember, this concept isn't limited to toilet paper rolls but applies to various real-life items too. Next time you drink soda from a can or play with a toy pipe, try estimating their volumes! This story is Bill's Late For School. Do you like to go to the beach? Adjusting spacing in Microsoft Word can greatly enhance the readability and appearance of your documents. This tutorial will guide you through the process of adjusting spacing in Microsoft Word for both PC and Mac users. **For Windows Users:** 1. Open Microsoft Word: Launch Microsoft Word on your computer by clicking its icon from the Start menu or desktop shortcut. 2. Create or open a document: If you want to create a new document, click on 'New' under the File tab. To work with an existing one, choose 'Open', navigate to where the file is saved, select it and hit 'Open'. 3. Navigate to Paragraph Settings: Place your cursor within the text whose spacing you wish to modify. Then go to the Home tab > Paragraph group > dialog box launcher (small arrow at bottom right corner). Alternatively, press Ctrl+Shift+P. *Why this step?* By doing so, you access the paragraph formatting options that allow precise control over line and page breaks, indentation, and spacing before and after paragraphs. 4. Under Indents and Spacing section, find Line Spacing drop-down list: Here, you have several predefined choices like Single, 1.5 lines, Double etc., as well as the option to set exact line space value in the At field below. *Key tip*: Aim for single-spaced lines most of the time because they are easier to read. However, double-space between paragraphs if it makes sense contextually, such as when writing academic papers. 5. Set points for Before and After fields: These settings determine the blank spaces above and beneath each paragraph respectively. Enter your preferred measurements in these boxes using the up/down arrows or typing directly. 6. Customize other settings if needed: You may also alter indention values if required via Left, Right, First line, and Special tabs. Click OK once done. 7. Verify changes: Observe the altered spacings visually throughout the entire text. Make sure there aren't any inconsistencies. 8. Save your work: Remember to save your modifications either manually via File > Save or automatically through AutoRecover feature. **For Mac Users:** 1. Fire up Microsoft Word: Locate and tap the Word app icon, usually found in Applications folder or Dock. 2. Access your doc: Either generate a fresh doc (File > New) or retrieve an old one (File > Open), then proceed accordingly. 3. Reach Paragraph Options: Highlight the target text, move to the Format menu, select Paragraph, or simply utilize Shift + Command + M keyboard combo. 4. Modify Line Spacing & Space Before/After: Within the opened window, look for the 'Line spacing' category containing familiar presets alongside customizable inputs for leading ('Before' and 'After') - equivalent to adding vertical spaces around selected paragraph(s). 5. Tweak further settings: Don't forget about additional controls regarding alignment, indentation, and tabulation available inside the same panel. 6. Confirm amendments: Check thoroughly whether all corrections appear correctly across the whole content. 7. Keep updated records: Never neglect preserving improvements along the way, accomplished through cmd+S command or choosing Save As... from File menu. Remember, consistency is crucial while dealing with document layouts including spacing. Always review final pieces meticulously prior sharing them with others! You know what you can do with that? I do a lot of things with you. Title: How to Detect Pregnancy in Dogs Introduction: Determining if your dog is pregnant is an important responsibility of pet ownership. Understanding the signs and symptoms can help ensure proper prenatal care and timely veterinary intervention when necessary. This guide will provide a comprehensive overview of detecting pregnancy in dogs, from recognizing early signs to confirming the pregnancy with veterinary assistance. Step 1: Know Your Dog's Heat Cycle Before you can suspect pregnancy, it's crucial to understand your dog's heat cycle. Most female dogs experience their first heat (estrus) around six months old, although this can vary depending on breed and size. A typical heat cycle lasts about three weeks, during which time she may attract male dogs and could become pregnant. Keep track of these cycles to better predict potential pregnancies. Key Tip: Spaying your dog before her first heat significantly reduces the risk of mammary tumors later in life. Consult with your vet about spaying options. Step 2: Observe Changes in Behavior In the initial stages of pregnancy, some dogs might show behavioral changes such as increased affection, nesting behaviors, or even aggression. While these signs aren't definitive proof of pregnancy, they can serve as early indicators worth noting. Guideline: Remember that many factors can cause shifts in a dog's demeanor, so consider other possible causes like illness or stress before assuming pregnancy. Step 3: Look Out For Physical Signs As pregnancy progresses, physical changes become more apparent. These include: - Enlarged nipples: Around 25 days post-breeding, nipples start swelling due to increased blood flow and milk production preparation. - Morning sickness: Just like humans, dogs sometimes experience morning sickness characterized by loss of appetite, listlessness, and vomiting. However, unlike humans who usually have bouts of morning sickness within the first few weeks, dogs tend to exhibit symptoms between day 40 and day 45. - Weight gain: Expect noticeable weight gain after the fourth week of gestation, averaging approximately 1% to 2% of body weight per day. By the end of the second trimester, expect a total increase of about 20%. Step 4: Visit The Vet By now, you should be fairly certain whether your dog is indeed pregnant. To confirm, schedule an appointment with your vet around 28 days into the suspected pregnancy. They will perform a manual abdominal palpitation to check for fetuses. However, this method isn't always accurate, especially in larger litters where individual puppies are harder to distinguish. Key Tip: Ultrasounds offer a higher level of accuracy than manual examination, typically providing results as soon as twenty-one days after conception. Ask your vet about scheduling one! Step 5: X-ray Confirmation Towards the end of the pregnancy, typically around day forty-five, x-rays become the most reliable way to verify pregnancy and estimate litter size. At this stage, skeletons of the developing puppies are visible, giving clear evidence of pregnancy. Guidelines: Refrain from using radiation-based diagnostic tools earlier in the pregnancy as they pose risks to unborn puppies. Also, discuss any concerns regarding exposure with your vet. Conclusion: While detecting pregnancy in dogs involves careful observation and medical confirmation, understanding the process allows for responsible pet parenting. Regular veterinary visits remain vital throughout the entire pregnancy to monitor health and development, ensuring both mother and pups receive optimal care leading up to delivery. Title: How to Call People on Your iPod Touch Without Needing Another Phone The iPod Touch is a popular device developed by Apple Inc., known for its media player capabilities, internet connectivity, and various apps available through the App Store. Although the iPod Touch does not have built-in cellular capabilities like an iPhone, you can still make calls using third-party applications and services. This tutorial will guide you through setting up your iPod Touch for calling without requiring another phone. ### Things You'll Need 1. An active Wi-Fi connection 2. A valid email address (for creating accounts) 3. An iTunes account with a payment method or sufficient store credit (for purchasing and downloading apps) 4. The following recommended free Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) apps from the App Store: * WhatsApp Messenger * Skype * Google Duo 5. Optional: Headphones with a built-in microphone 6. Optional: Bluetooth headset or speaker **Step 1:** Set Up Your Wi-Fi Connection ---------------------------------------- Before proceeding further, ensure that your iPod Touch has access to a stable Wi-Fi network. To do this, navigate to **Settings > Wi-Fi**. Enable Wi-Fi by tapping the switch next to 'Wi-Fi'. Select your preferred network from the list below. If necessary, enter your password and tap 'Join'. Having a reliable internet connection allows high-quality voice and video calls through VoIP apps. ![Wi-Fi Settings](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/ab/Use-Your-iPod-Touch-as-a-Phone-Step-1-Version-3.jpg/aid789019-v4- See if she wants to go in the water too? What you'll need: - rimmed baking sheet/cookie sheet - Matchbox cars or something of the like - salt (table salt is fine!) - sandwich bag - Fill a baking sheet up with a thin layer of water. Place in freezer and let it freeze completely. - Put ice cube in a bag. - I started by filling an ice tray with her and freezing it. This way she could see that ice is just frozen water. - Give a bag with 1 cube of ice. Let them roll it around. Instruct them to rub it, blow on it, etc. Ask questions like, "What is happening to the ice? Why do you think it's melting?" Make sure you explain after that it was the heat from the air and their hands that helps it melt. - Pull out tray of frozen water. Let them roll and skid cars across it. Point out how slippery it is, asking what would happen if there was ice on our roads outside? If there isn't heat to melt the ice, our cars would slip. - I then made comments like, "I wonder if we can put something on the ice to help it melt. What do you think?" - If desired, try other items in the house like cornstarch, flour, cocoa powder. Let them pour salt on the ice. Make observations together about what happens when the salt is put on top of the ice. - Let them roll the cars. You should find some traction, as well as the ice melting. Explain that we also put salt on the roads to help melt the ice and keep cars from slipping. Point it out next time you see it! Historians believe that Lincoln practiced these problems in 1824 when he was 15 years old. Note the verse in the lower left corner: Questions for Illustrations 1 & 2 In Illustration 2, Lincoln assigned himself the problem of multiplying 342,435 by 342. Attempt to solve this problem. Once you have your answer, you can check it just like Abraham checked his. He used a technique called the "Rules of Nines." Follow the directions below. 1. First draw a sizeable X on your paper and put an "A" on the right side of the X, a "B" on the left side, a "C" on top, and a "D" on bottom. 2. What do you get when you add together all the numerals in the multiplicand? Add the numerals in 342,435 or: 3 + 4 + 2 + 4 + 3 + 5. 3. Add together the numerals that make up the multiplier of 342 (3 + 4 + 2). 4. Finally, add up the numerals in your product (the answer you got when you multiplied 342,435 by 342. That's ___ + ___ + ___ + ___ + ___ + ___ + ___ + ____ + ___. 5. What was the number you got when you added up the numerals in the multiplicand? (Your answer for #1) Divide this number by 9. Put your remainder in the big "X" below, right next to the letter A. 6. What did you get when you added up the numerals in the multiplier? (Your answer for #2) Divide that number by 9 and put the remainder in the "X" by the letter B. 7. Now do the same thing with the number you got once you added the numerals in the product. Put the remainder in the top of the "X", next to the letter C. 8. Here are the last three steps. Multiply together the number you put next to A with the number you put next to B. Divide by 9 one last time and put your remainder next to D, in the bottom of the "X." 9. Is the number you put in the top of the "X" (near the C) the same as the number in the bottom of the "X" (near D)? Then your multiplication is correct!
