Title: How to Change the Wallpaper on Your iPhone Introduction: Changing the wallpaper on your iPhone is an easy way to personalize your device and make it truly yours. Whether you want to set a photo of your loved ones as your background or use a beautiful landscape image, this tutorial will guide you through the process with clear and concise steps. Let's get started! Key Tips & Guidelines: 1. Make sure your iPhone is updated to the latest iOS version to ensure all features are available. 2. Choose high-resolution images for optimal display quality. 3. Be aware that using live photos or videos as wallpapers may consume more battery life than static images. 4. Set different wallpapers for Home Screen and Lock Screen to distinguish between them visually. 5. Preview the changes before applying to confirm they meet your expectations. 6. Remember where you sourced the image from (camera roll, downloaded files, etc.) to avoid confusion when searching for new wallpapers. Step 1: Unlock Your iPhone --------------------------- Press the side button or use Face ID / Touch ID to unlock your iPhone. This allows access to the home screen and settings menu. ### Step 2: Open Settings App Locate and tap on the gray gear icon labeled 'Settings.' The settings app contains various customization options for your device. ### Step 3: Scroll Down and Tap 'Wallpaper' In the settings menu, scroll down until you find the option labeled 'Wallpaper.' It has an icon resembling two overlapping squares. ### Step 4: Select 'Choose a New Wallpaper' Tap on 'Choose a New Wallpaper' to proceed to the selection screen. Here, you can choose from preloaded Apple images or pick one from your own camera roll. ### Step 5: Pick Image Source On the top bar, there are two tabs - 'Dynamic,' which includes animated wallpapers provided by Apple, and 'Stills,' containing still images. Alternatively, select 'All Photos' under 'Photos' tab to view your entire Camera Roll. ### Step 6: Browse Through Images Navigate through the gallery and select the image you wish to use as your wallpaper. You can zoom in or out by pinching the screen. ### Step 7: Apply Perspective Zoom (Optional) For some images, especially those taken with Portrait Mode, you might see an additional toggle called 'Perspective Zoom'. Enabling this feature adds a parallax effect, giving a sense of depth to the background when moving your phone around slightly. ### Step 8: Choose Where To Set The Wallpaper After selecting an image, you'll be prompted to decide whether to change only the lock screen, home screen, or both. Make your choice based on your preference. ### Step 9: Adjust Display Position Drag the image left or right, up or down to position it correctly within the frame. A white rectangle indicates the active area visible behind app icons. ### Step 10: Press 'Set' Button Once satisfied with the preview, press 'Set' located at the bottom right corner. Then choose either 'Set Both' if you like the same wallpaper on both screens or 'Set Lock Screen'/'Set Home Screen' depending upon your earlier decision. Congratulations! You have successfully changed your iPhone's wallpaper. Enjoy your newly personalized device. Angles are studied in geometry, where an angle where edges meet is often called a vertex. For example, the three sides of a triangle are its "edges" and two of the edges meet at each "vertex". Similarly, two of the six sides (or "faces") of a cube meet at each of its twelve edges, and three edges meet at each of its eight corners (or vertices, which is the plural version of "vertex"). [MOT sits next to Chi, lights cigarette] Title: How to Master the Dropshot Technique in Call of Duty Introduction: Dropshotting is a popular advanced technique used by players in Call of Duty games to gain an advantage over opponents. It involves quickly switching between standing, crouching or proning (lying down) positions while firing your weapon. This can make you a harder target to hit, especially when combined with good movement and aiming skills. However, mastering dropshotting requires practice and patience. Here's our step-by-step guide on how to do it effectively. Step 1: Understand the Basics Before starting, understand that dropshotting does not guarantee a win every time; it's merely another tool in your arsenal. The goal is to disrupt your enemy's aim, making them more likely to miss their shots, giving you an opportunity to land yours. Step 2: Choose the Right Weapon Not all weapons are suitable for dropshotting. Submachine guns (SMGs), assault rifles (ARs), and light machine guns (LMGs) with quick ADS (Aim Down Sight) times work best. Shotguns and sniper rifles aren't ideal as they require longer aiming times. Key Tip: Use attachments like Stock and Quickdraw to reduce ADS time further. Step 3: Perfect Your Movement Practice moving around maps smoothly - running, sliding, jumping, and changing stances. Learn how each stance affects your view and mobility. Familiarize yourself with different map layouts so you know where cover is available if needed. Step 4: Bind a Button for Quick Stance Switching In PC versions of Call of Duty games, bind a button to switch rapidly between stand, crouch, and prone modes. On consoles, this might be trickier due to limited buttons, but some controllers allow customizations. Step 5: Start Practicing Slowly Don't rush into intense combat situations right away. Find a quiet area on the map and start practicing transitioning between stances while shooting at dummy targets or empty spaces. Try to maintain accuracy during these transitions. Step 6: Combine Dropshotting with Other Movements Once comfortable with basic dropshotting, incorporate other movements such as jumpshots (jumping while shooting) and slideshots (sliding while shooting). These additions increase unpredictability, making it even harder for enemies to hit you. Step 7: Engage in Combat Now that you've practiced enough, engage in real combats. Remember, dropshotting should complement your overall strategy, not replace it. Keep an eye on your surroundings, listen for footsteps, and always consider whether dropping shot will provide any benefits in the current situation. Step 8: Adapt According to Situation Sometimes, staying stationary to shoot may not be the best option. If you find yourself getting flanked often or struggling against certain types of opponents, adjust your approach accordingly. You might need to rely less on dropshotting and focus more on evasive maneuvers or improving your aim. Step 9: Analyze Your Performance After each match, analyze your performance. Identify areas where you could improve, whether it's reaction time, accuracy, or understanding when to drop shot. Use this feedback to refine your technique. Conclusion: Mastering dropshotting takes time and consistent effort. Don't get discouraged if results don't show immediately. With regular practice and application of these steps, you'll soon see improvements in your gameplay. Happy gaming! Honey you had two bites on your plate. Title: How to Have a Successful Hotel in Animal Jam Introduction: Animal Jam is an online playground where kids can safely learn about animals and their habitats while playing games and having fun with friends. One popular activity in Animal Jama is setting up shops or hotels to sell rare items, furniture, den decorations, and other virtual goods to fellow players. This tutorial will focus specifically on creating a successful hotel business within Animal Jam. Step 1: Choosing Your Theme and Location Key Tip: A themed hotel attracts more customers than a generic one. Decide what kind of animal you want your hotel to cater to (e.g., penguins, tigers) or choose a specific theme like underwater, winter wonderland, or jungle lodge. Once you have decided on a theme, select a location that matches it closely. For instance, if you are going with an underwater theme, place your hotel near the ocean area in Animal Jam. Step 2: Designing Your Den Guideline: The design of your den should reflect both your chosen theme and the purpose of being a hotel. Make sure there's ample space for guests to move around comfortably and place enough beds so they can rest when needed. Consider using custom greetings to welcome visitors and create a friendly atmosphere. Also, ensure proper lighting by adding lights or bright-colored objects throughout the den. Step 3: Acquiring Items to Sell Key Tips: * Focus on acquiring high-demand items such as seasonal rares, hard-to-find pets, and exclusive clothing pieces. * Participate in daily spikes and events hosted by Animal Jam to increase your chances of obtaining these desirable items. * Trade wisely - always research the value of an item before accepting any trades offered to you. Step 4: Pricing Strategy Guidelines: Determine fair prices based on current market values. You may charge slightly higher prices for extremely rare or sought-after items but avoid overpricing, which could deter potential customers. Offering discounts or bundles might also entice buyers to purchase multiple items at once. Step 5: Marketing Your Hotel Key Tips: * Use social media platforms outside of Animal Jam to promote your hotel and its offerings. * Word-of-mouth advertising among friends and community members can be highly effective. * Host giveaways or contests to generate interest in your hotel. Step 6: Providing Excellent Customer Service Guidelines: Treat every customer respectfully and courteously, answering questions patiently and addressing concerns promptly. Going above and beyond for your customers can lead to positive reviews and repeat business. Step 7: Managing Inventory Key Tips: Keep track of your inventory levels regularly to prevent selling out of popular items too quickly. Restock frequently and consider diversifying your offerings to appeal to a wider range of customers. Step 8: Stay Updated With Animal Jam News and Events Guideline: Regularly check official Animal Jam blogs, social media accounts, and newsletters for updates on new releases, sales, and promotions. Being aware of these changes allows you to adjust your strategies accordingly and take advantage of opportunities as they arise. Conclusion: Running a successful hotel in Animal Jam requires careful planning, strategic decision-making, and consistent effort. By following this comprehensive guide, you'll be well on your way to establishing a thriving business within the game. Happy jamming! Gravitational wave astronomy is the study of the Universe in the gravitational wave spectrum. So far, all astronomy that has been done has used the electromagnetic spectrum. Gravitational waves are ripples in spacetime emitted by very dense objects changing shape, which include white dwarves, neutron stars and black holes. Because no one has been able to detect gravitational waves directly, the impact of Gravitational Wave Astronomy has been very limited. Since most bonds require energy to form, they also give off energy when they are broken. But before most bonds break, the molecule has to be heated. Then the atoms start to move, and when they move too much, the bond breaks. Molecules that require less energy to break than they give off when broken are called fuels. For example, a candle will just sit there and nothing happens. But when you use a match to light it, it will burn for a long time. The match brings the energy to break the first bonds, which release enough energy to break the bonds below them, until the candle has burned down. No because your nose could get covered. You went to another house this morning did ya? Title: How to Bowl a Swinging Yorker A swinging yorker is a challenging but highly rewarding delivery in cricket. It involves bowling the ball at the batsman's toes with significant lateral movement, making it difficult for them to score or even keep the ball out. This wikihow will guide you through the process of mastering this skill. **Step 1: Understand the Basics** Before starting, understand that a yorker is delivered very close to the ground aiming for the base of the stumps (the 'toes'), while a swinging delivery moves sideways after pitching due to rotation imparted by your wrist action. A swinging yorker combines both these elements. *Key Tip*: Practice individual components - accuracy for a yorker, swing for an inswinger/outswinger - before attempting a swinging yorker. **Step 2: Grip the Ball Properly** Hold the ball firmly but not too tightly using your first two fingers and thumb. The seam should be horizontal if you are right-handed (vertical otherwise). Your third and fourth fingers provide balance and control over speed. *Key Guideline*: Ensure there's enough space between your index and middle finger to allow free spinning of the ball. **Step 3: Run-Up and Approach** Maintain a smooth rhythm during your run-up which usually varies from 5-8 strides depending on personal preference. As you approach the crease, ensure your body weight shifts onto your dominant leg. *Key Tip*: Keep your eyes focused on the target area throughout the approach. **Step 4: Action at the Crease** At the point of release, your bowling arm should be almost fully extended, pointing towards the batsman. Your wrist should rotate counterclockwise (for right-handers) causing the back of your hand to face the batsman. *Key Guideline*: Rotation of the wrist determines whether the ball swings inward ('inswing') or outward ('outswing'). For a swinging yorker, you want either type of swing. **Step 5: Follow Through** After releasing the ball, continue moving forward naturally without stopping abruptly. Bring your non-bowling arm down and across your chest, and let your bowling arm come to rest near your hip. *Key Tip*: A proper follow-through reduces strain on your shoulder and increases consistency. **Step 6: Analyze and Correct** Analyze the result of your delivery carefully. If the ball didn't swing sufficiently or went beyond the toe line, adjust your grip, wrist position, or stride length accordingly. *Key Guideline*: Patience and persistent practice are crucial in perfecting a swinging yorker. Remember, every bowler has their unique style and technique; therefore, adapt these steps according to what feels comfortable and effective for you. With consistent effort, you can certainly add a swinging yoker to your repertoire. Happy Bowling! I was curious if she'd remember what it was called from the book. Title: How to Find Out If Someone Is Bankrupt Introduction: Knowing whether someone is bankrupt can be important for various reasons such as business decisions, financial planning, or personal relationships. While you may want to respect people's privacy, there are legal ways to access this information under certain circumstances. This guide will walk you through the process of finding out if someone is bankrupt in the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, Australia, and New Zealand. Keep in mind that laws and regulations vary by country, so always consult local resources and seek professional advice when necessary. Step 1: Determine Your Reason for Needing the Information Before proceeding, ensure your reason falls within legally acceptable grounds. These typically include protecting yourself from fraudulent activities, evaluating potential business partners, or making informed lending decisions. Misuse of this information could lead to legal consequences. Step 2: Gather Basic Details About the Person To begin your search, collect essential details about the person in question, including their full name, last known address, and any possible aliases. The more accurate and complete this information, the easier it will be to find relevant records. Step 3: Check Public Records Databases (United States) In the US, bankruptcy filings are considered public records and can often be found online through databases like PACER (Public Access to Court Electronic Records). However, using these services usually requires registration and payment. You might also consider searching free websites that compile publicly available data; however, they may not provide comprehensive results. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Remember that PACER charges fees per page viewed, so keep track of your usage to avoid unexpected costs. - Always verify the accuracy of the information obtained from third-party databases since they might contain errors or outdated data. Step 4: Contact Local Courts Directly (Canada, UK, Australia, and New Zealand) Bankruptcy records in countries outside the US aren't as readily accessible online due to stricter privacy laws. Instead, try contacting local courts directly where the individual has lived or conducted businesses. Provide them with the gathered details and ask if they can disclose any bankruptcy filings related to the person. They may require written requests or proof justifying your need for the information. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Be prepared to wait several days or even weeks for a response, depending on the court's processing time and resources. - Familiarize yourself with specific privacy laws and requirements in the respective country before submitting your request. Step 5: Consult Professional Services If your efforts have been unsuccessful, consider hiring professionals who specialize in background checks or debtor tracing. Reputable companies possess extensive knowledge of local laws and resources, increasing the likelihood of obtaining accurate information. Fees for these services vary but generally range between $20-$100 per report. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Research different providers thoroughly, read reviews, and compare prices before choosing one. - Ensure the company adheres to strict confidentiality policies and follows ethical practices during its investigations. Conclusion: Finding out if someone is bankrupt involves understanding legal limitations, gathering necessary information, and utilizing appropriate resources. By following the steps outlined above, you increase your chances of discovering valuable insights while respecting individuals' privacy rights. Title: How to Clean Yourself: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Maintaining personal hygiene is essential for overall health, well-being, and social acceptance. This guide will provide you with an in-depth understanding of various cleaning techniques, enabling you to maintain optimal cleanliness throughout your day. By following these steps and incorporating them into your daily routine, you can effectively eliminate germs, bacteria, and unpleasant odors while promoting self-confidence and positive first impressions. I. Bathing 1. Prepare the bath or shower: Fill the bathtub with warm water (not too hot) and add soothing bath salts or bubbles if desired. Alternatively, adjust the showerhead to a comfortable height and temperature. This is called the very hungry caterpillar. Ready, Set, Read Growing Independence and Fluency Rationale: For children to be able to read adequately, they need to be able to read fluently and skillfully. A reader that is fluent can recognize words automatically, rapidly and accurately. Not only do fluent readers learn to read quickly and smoothly but also with expression. The goal of this lesson is to help students develop reading fluency using timed reading. Materials: stopwatches, reading logs, frog reading sheet with Velcro strips to the frog can hop to lily pad to lily pad, the book If You Give a Mouse a Cookie by Laura Numeroff. 1. I will introduce what it means to be a fluent reader and how important it is to read with ease. It is very important that you read accurately because fluent readers can better understand the text. We are going to use some procedures that will help you read easier. To help increase your speed and accuracy, we will do quick reads. 2. I am going to read two sentences. I want you to listen to both of the sentences and hear the differences in them. Read one sentence by sounding out every phoneme and the other one by reading it quickly and smoothly. Ask which one was choppy and which was smooth. Read two more sentences but this time make one dull and monotone and read the other with expression. Ask which one they better understood the text. Explain that by reading with expression and emotion reading can be fun and can be easier to understand. 3. Our first activity will be to read If You Give a Mouse a Cookie. We will be practicing increasing your reading speed. We will be reading this book quite a few times to increase your fluency along with reading faster. Book Talk: A little mouse wants a cookie. While eating this cookie he wants a glass of milk. He keeps wanting more and more. Read to find out what all the little mouse gets into! 4. I will set the timer for one minute to begin the one-minute reading. When I say stop I want you to write down how many pages you read. Do not rush and write down words just to finish. Remember you are reading for accuracy and fluency. Model what a one minute reading looks like. I will read for one minute as quickly as possible reading as many pages as I can. I will set the timer for one minute and I will stop when I hear the timer go off. Then I will record my performance in my reading log (one minute) (six pages). Lastly, I will move my frog on the six page marker. 5. Pass out a copy of If You Give a Mouse a Cookie to each student. Have the students do a quick read of the book. Have students cross check when rereading the sentence to see if it makes sense. When the buzzer goes off have students record their time and number of pages read. 6. Next, group the students into pairs and have them do quick read with their partner. Give each pair a stopwatch and have one person be the timer and the other the reader. After a minute of reading switch roles. Remind the students that they are reading for fluency and accuracy. 7. To assess every student, I would take up their reading logs to see where they are in their speed readings. Ready, Set, Read by Beth Tyler http://www.auburn.edu/academic/education/reading_genie/openings/tylergf.html Return to Projects Index No we don't break the magic wand. Lila and Max were best friends. They liked to play in the park with their toys. One day, they saw a new girl with a big doll. The doll had long hair, a pretty dress and shiny shoes. Lila and Max looked at the doll and felt envious. "Wow, look at her doll!" Lila said. "It is so big and nice. I wish I had a doll like that." "Me too," Max said. "Maybe we can ask her to play with us and share her doll." They walked over to the new girl and said hello. The new girl smiled and said her name was Mia. She held her doll close to her waist. "Hi, Mia. We are Lila and Max. Can we play with you and your doll?" Lila asked. "Sure, you can play with me. But you can't touch my doll. She is mine and I love her very much. She is the best doll in the world," Mia said. Lila and Max felt sad. They wanted to play with the doll, but Mia did not want to share. They thought Mia was not very nice. They looked at each other and had an idea. "Okay, Mia. We will play with you. But we have a game for you. It is called 'seem'. Do you know how to play?" Max asked. "No, I don't. How do you play?" Mia asked. "It is easy. You just have to say what things seem like. For example, I can say 'the sky seems blue' or 'the grass seems green'. Do you understand?" Max explained. "I think so. It sounds fun. Let's play," Mia said. "Okay, you go first. What does your doll seem like?" Lila asked. Mia looked at her doll and smiled. She said, "My doll seems like a princess. She has beautiful hair, a lovely dress and sparkly shoes. She is the most wonderful doll ever." Lila and Max nodded. They waited for Mia to look away. Then they quickly grabbed the doll and ran away. They laughed and said, "Your doll seems like ours now. Bye, Mia!" Mia was shocked and angry. She shouted and cried. She wanted her doll back. She did not like the game of 'seem'. She thought Lila and Max were very mean. She wished she had shared her doll with them. No I want' a Graham cracker now. oh, you already ate half of it? -Click on the left or right arrows to shift the gray bars left or right. Notice that when the gray stripes are on the top of the black bars they appear lighter and when they are between the black bars they appear darker. -Center the gray stripes so they are half-on and half-off the black bars. You can do this either with the arrows or by clicking the gray circular button. Notice that the two sets of gray stripes appear the same. -Click and hold the striped button to make the black bars disappear. Notice that the two sets of gray stripes appear the same. The gray stripes on both sides are the same shade. When you change the position of the stripes, they seem to get lighter and darker. This illusion is not fully understood, but it seems to have something to do with the way your eye determines Your eye focuses an image of the striped pattern on your retina, a layer of light-sensitive cells at the back of your eye. Nerve cells in the retina begin processing the light and dark information in two different ways. Some nerve cells in the retina take a look at the big picture, receiving information from a large area of the retina. These cells blend light from several stripes and react as if the light were mixed together. When there are white stripes on either side of the gray stripes, you see pale gray - a mixture of white and gray. When the gray stripes are surrounded by black stripes, you see dark gray - a mixture of black and Even though some nerve cells blend the light from the stripes together, other nerve cells receive information from a small area of the retina. These cells note the contrast between the gray stripes, the white stripes, and the black stripes, allowing you to see the striped pattern. There has been controversy surrounding when the correct time to start therapy should be after a person discovers that he or she has HIV. Recently, the answer has been that earlier treatment is recommended. This is because, first, effective therapy can prevent non-AIDS-related deaths. Second, therapy can prevent harm to a person's immune system. Third, therapy can help prevent transmission of HIV to others, and can therefore reduce HIV prevalence overall. Although there are some negative side effects of antiretroviral medications, the benefits of therapy usually outweigh the negative effects. I want 'a BS in a race. Weather happens in this layer. Most clouds are found in the troposphere. The next layer up is the stratosphere. Click on image for full size Original artwork by Windows to the Universe staff (Randy Russell). The troposphere is the lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere. The troposphere starts at ground level. The top of the troposphere is about 11 km up (that's 7 miles or 36,000 feet). The layer above the troposphere is called the stratosphere. Most of the air (about 3/4ths) in our atmosphere is in the troposphere. Almost all weather happens in the troposphere. Most clouds are found in this lowest layer, too. The jet stream is near the top of the troposphere. This "river of air" zooms along at 400 km/hr (250 mph)! The troposphere is warmest near the ground. Sunlight heats the ground (or oceans!). The ground heats the air that is closest to it... at the bottom of the troposphere. The higher up you go in the troposphere, the colder it gets. That's why there is often snow in tall mountains, even in the summer. The temperature at the top of the troposphere is around -55 C (-64 F)! Brrrrrrrrr! Air also gets 'thinner' as you go higher up. That's why mountain climbers sometimes need bottled oxygen to breathe. The "border" between the troposphere and the stratosphere above it has a special name. It is called the tropopause. The height of the tropopause actually depends on whether it is day or night, summer or winter, or whether you are near the equator or one of the poles. At the equator, the tropopause is about 20 km (12 miles or 65,000 feet) above sea level. In winter near the poles the tropopause is much lower. It is about 7 km (4 miles or 23,000 feet) high. Lesson 12 Section 2 HOW TO ROUND OFF A DECIMAL HOW TO EXPRESS THE QUOTIENT |4.||How do we round off, or approximate, a decimal to a required number of decimal digits?| Look at the digit to the right of the required number. If it is a 5 or greater, add 1 to previous digit. If it is less than 5, leave the previous digit unchanged. In either case, drop all the remaining digits. Example 1. Round off this decimal 7.253896 to three decimal digits. (The wavy equal sign means "is approximately equal to.") To round off to three decimal digits, we must look at the digit in the fourth place. The digit in the fourth place is 8 (greater than 5). Therefore, we add 1 to the previous digit 3. Example 2. Round off 7.253896 to two decimal digits. The digit in the third decimal place is 3 (less than 5). Therefore, we leave the digit in the second place (5) unchanged. Example 3. Round off 7.253896 to the nearest tenth. To round off to the nearest tenth, means to keep one decimal digit. (To round off to the nearest hundredth would mean to keep two digits; to the nearest thousandth, three; and so on. Lesson 3, Question 5.) Now, the digit in the second decimal place is 5. Therefore add 1 to the previous digit 2. Example 4. Round off $6.497014 Answer. This is money. Therefore we must round off to two decimal digits: The digit in the third decimal place is 7 (greater than 5). Therefore, when we add 1 to 9 of 6.49, we get 6.50 To round off whole numbers, see Lesson 2. |5.||How do we express the quotient as a decimal?| |When there are no more digits in the dividend:| |1)||Place a decimal point and bring it up to the quotient.| |2)||Add a 0 onto the remainder, and continue dividing.| |3)||Add a 0 onto each remainder until the division is exact. If the division will never be exact, round off the quotient to the required number of decimal digits.| "4 goes into 32 eight (8) times. "4 goes into 7 one (1) time with 3 left over. There are no more digits in the dividend. Therefore, according to the rule: Place a decimal point, bring it up to the quotient, and add a 0 onto the remainder 3: Continue dividing. Place a 0 after each remainder: The quotient of 327 4, then, may be expressed in three different ways: Example 6. Compare the following: |a)||If it takes 4 yards of material to cover a sofa, how many sofas could you cover with 25 yards?| |b)||If 4 people go to lunch and the bill is $25, how much does each one pay?| a) We must divide by 4: "4 goes into 25 six (6) times with 1 left over." This means that you could cover 6 sofas, and 1 yard of material will remain. |b)||Again, we must divide by 4, but here we must express the quotient as a decimal as money and 6 R 1 makes no sense.| Each person will pay $6.25. Solution. "6 goes into 12 two (2) times. 6 goes into 7 one (1) time with 1 left over." Place a decimal point, and add a 0 onto the remainder 1: Now the same remainder 4 will be repeated. Therefore this division will never be exact. To round off to three decimal digits, then, we must know the digit in the fourth place -- 6. Therefore, we add 1 to the previous digit; we get 21.167 To round off, we must know one more decimal digit than is required. |6.||How do we proceed when the divdend is less than the divisor?| |5 8| |Proceed as in the previous case.| Example 8. 5 8 The dividend 5 is less than the divisor 8. Since 8 is the divisor, write it outside the division box: "8 goes into 5 zero (0)." Place the decimal point, and add on a 0. Next, "8 goes into 50 six (6) times (48) with 2 left over." "8 goes into 20 two (2) times (16) with 4 left over." "8 goes into 40 five (5) times exactly." We will see an application of the dividend less than the divisor when we come to changing a fraction to a decimal (Lesson 24). For, |=||5 8.| The horizontal line separating 5 and 8 is the division bar. Example 9. The following division will not be exact. Round it off to three decimal digits: 1 11 Solution. Place 11 outside the box and begin: "11 goes into 1 zero (0)." Place the decimal point, and add on a 0. Now 11 does not go into 10. Write 0 in the quotient -- -- and add another 0 onto the dividend. "11 goes into 100 nine (9) times, with 1 left over." Write 9 in the quotient, and add a 0 onto the remainder 1. Write 0 in the quotient, and add another 0 to the dividend. Again, This division will never be exact. We will keep getting 090909. To round it off to three digits, we have calculated the digit in the fourth place. It's 9. Therefore, we will add 1 to the previous digit: You sometime see: 1 11 = 0.090909... The three dots, called ellipsis, mean: "The decimal for 1 11 will never be exact. However, we can approximate it to as many decimal digits as we please by following the pattern 090909." Example 10. Patricia pays $66 each year for magazine subscriptions. How much does that cost her each week? Solution. We must divide $66 by the number of weeks in a year, 52. Press Since this is money, we must round it off to the nearest cent. It costs Patricia approximately $1.27 each week. Example 11. A plane flew between New York and Los Angeles, a distance of 2,477 miles, in 4.25 hours. What was its speed in miles per hour? Solution. Miles "per" hour indicates Miles hours (Lesson 11). Press On the screen see This is approximately 582.8 miles per hour. Example 12. With a calculator, Janet found that 978 5 = 195.6. What actually is the remainder upon dividing 978 by 5? |195.6 5||=||195 5 + .6 5||(Lesson 9)| |=||975 + 3.||(Lesson 10)| 3 is the remainder. This example illustrates the meaning of a decimal quotient. It consists of a whole number, 195, plus a decimal fraction, .6. (Lesson 3.) The digit to the right of the decimal point is not the remainder. It is that decimal fraction of the divisor. (Lesson 27.) Please "turn" the page and do some Problems. Dictators often rewrite an existing constitution or put in place a completely new one. This makes their power constitutional (which then cannot be disputed). Dictators then maintain their rule with state terrorism, which normally involves a secret police, death squads, random or night curfew, indefinite arrest without trial and a network of torture centers and concentration camps. Some dictatorships create a fictional (or non existent) internal (inside their country) enemy which they claim to be at war with to justify (give reason for) their use of much military violence against their people. There was a change in plant resources, with the reduced forests and extended grassland. This favoured specialist grass feeders over generalist eaters. This change in the dietary supply was one of the factors which forced gomphotheres toward extinction in the late Pleistocene in South America. The remaining population probably succumbed to climatic change and/or human predation around the time of the Holocene margin. Title: How to Change Windshield Wipers Changing your windshield wipers is an essential car maintenance task that you can do yourself. It's simple, quick, and cost-effective. This guide will provide a comprehensive, step-by-step tutorial on changing windshield wipers for both front and rear wipers. **Things You'll Need:** 1. New wiper blades (make sure they fit your vehicle by checking the owner's manual or using an online vehicle compatibility checker) 2. Gloves (optional, but recommended for protection against dirt and harsh weather conditions) 3. A clean microfiber cloth 4. Screwdriver (if necessary, depending on the type of wiper arm) ### **Step 1: Determine the Size and Type of Your Current Wiper Blades** Before purchasing new wiper blades, measure the length of your current blades (in inches) and take note of their types (conventional, beam, hybrid, etc.). Most vehicles have different sizes for driver and passenger sides. Visit an auto parts store or their website to find compatible replacements based on your vehicle make, model, and year. ### **Step 2: Gather Necessary Materials and Protect Your Vehicle** Collect all required materials as listed above. Put on gloves if available, then lift the wiper arms away from the windshield to prevent scratches while working. If possible, cover the windshield with a clean microfiber cloth to protect it from dirt and debris during blade removal. ### **Step 3: Remove Old Wiper Blades** Locate the joint where the wiper blade connects to the wiper arm. There should be a small release tab or lever on one side of this joint. Press or pull this tab while simultaneously pulling downward on the wiper blade. The old blade should slide off the metal arm once released. Be careful not to let go of the wiper arm, as it may snap back onto the windshield due to its spring tension. ### **Step 4: Install New Wiper Blades** Take your new wiper blade out of its packaging. Align the hooked or J-shaped end of the new blade with the curved underside of the wiper arm. Slide the blade onto the arm until you hear a click, indicating that it has locked into place. Make sure the blade is securely attached and cannot slide off easily. Repeat these steps for the other side. ### **Step 5: Test the New Wiper Blades** Lower the wiper arms back onto the windshield gently. Turn on your vehicle's ignition and test the wipers by activating them briefly at a slow speed. Check for even contact along the entire length of the windshield and ensure proper operation without any vibration or skipping. ### **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Always replace both wiper blades at the same time, even if only one appears worn out. This ensures balanced visibility and prolongs the life of the new blades. * Changing windshield wipers regularly (every six months to a year) is crucial for maintaining optimal performance and preventing streaking, smearing, or scratching the glass. * When storing your vehicle for extended periods, consider lifting the wiper arms off the windshield to avoid potential damage from snow buildup or ice formation. * Never attempt to forcefully remove stuck wiper blades; instead, consult a professional mechanic if you encounter difficulty during replacement. Title: The Best Way to Reheat Fried Rice: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Fried rice is a popular dish enjoyed by many due to its unique taste and texture. However, leftover fried rice can be challenging to reheat while preserving its original flavor and consistency. This article will provide you with an in-depth guide on the best way to reheat fried rice, ensuring that your leftovers are as delicious as when first prepared. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Use low heat settings to avoid overcooking or burning the rice. 2. Add moisture (either water or oil) to prevent drying out. 3. Stir frequently during the reheating process. 4. Monitor the temperature consistently to ensure even heating. 5. Taste test before serving to adjust seasonings if necessary. Step 1: Choose Your Cookware Select a pan or skillet with a flat bottom surface, which ensures uniform heat distribution. Non-stick pans work well since they require minimal oil and make cleaning easier. Avoid using pottery or glass cookware because their poor heat conduction may lead to unevenly heated food. Step 2: Prepare Leftover Fried Rice Take out your refrigerated fried rice and let it sit at room temperature for about 10-15 minutes. Allowing it to warm up slightly makes the grains less dense, helping them separate more efficiently during reheating. Never leave cooked rice at room temperature for extended periods, as this encourages bacterial growth. Step 3: Add Moisture Add one tablespoon of water per cup of cold rice directly into the pan. If you prefer, substitute water with vegetable oil or sesame oil for extra richness. Gently mix the added liquid throughout the rice with a spatula or wooden spoon until every grain is coated. Step 4: Cover and Heat Place the covered pan on medium-low heat. Allow the rice to gently steam for approximately five minutes without lifting the lid. Steaming infuses the chilled grains with warmth and rejuvenates their fluffy structure. Remember, lower temperatures help maintain the integrity of the ingredients and reduce the risk of scorching the exterior layers. Step 5: Uncover and Stir After steaming, remove the cover from the pan and stir the contents thoroughly. Break apart any clumps formed during storage and distribute the heat evenly among all the grains. Be gentle; vigorous mixing could damage the delicate texture of the dish. Step 6: Increase Heat and Saut Turn the heat up to medium and continue saut ing the rice mixture for another two to three minutes. Regular stirring prevents sticking and browning while encouraging further evaporation of excess moisture. Step 7: Season Adjustment Towards the end of the reheating process, check the flavor balance. Depending upon the age of the stored rice, some loss of seasoning might have occurred. Feel free to add soy sauce, salt, pepper, or other spices according to personal preference. Conclusion: Reheating fried rice doesn't need to result in dry, unappetizing leftovers. By following these steps meticulously, you can restore your fried rice close to its freshly made state. Always remember to monitor the cooking progress closely, adding small amounts of moisture whenever needed, and never neglecting the importance of proper seasoning adjustments. Happy cooking! Title: How to Confidently Speak in Public Without Fear Public speaking is an essential skill for many aspects of life, including work, school, and social situations. However, fear of public speaking can be overwhelming and prevent you from sharing your ideas effectively. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on confidently speaking in public without fear. By following these steps, you'll learn techniques that will help you overcome anxiety, engage your audience, and deliver powerful speeches. ### Step 1: Understand the Causes of Public Speaking Anxiety Fear of public speaking often stems from several factors such as lack of preparation, self-doubt, past negative experiences, or even physiological responses like increased heart rate and sweating. Recognizing what triggers your anxiety allows you to address those issues specifically and develop coping strategies. Key tip: Remember that everyone experiences some level of nervousness before giving a speech; it's natural and doesn't necessarily reflect your abilities. ### Step 2: Prepare Thoroughly Proper preparation reduces anxiety by building confidence in your material. Research your topic extensively, create an outline, practice delivering your speech out loud multiple times, and become familiar with any equipment or visual aids you plan to use. Guideline: Allocate sufficient time for rehearsals, ideally starting weeks ahead of the actual presentation date. ### Step 3: Visualize Success Visualization exercises can significantly improve mental toughness and performance under pressure. Imagine yourself delivering a successful speech, engaging your audience, and receiving positive feedback. Repeat this process regularly leading up to your presentation. Key tip: Make your visualizations vivid and realistic, focusing on details like body language, tone of voice, and facial expressions. ### Step 4: Practice Relaxation Techniques Learning relaxation techniques can help manage physical symptoms of anxiety during public speaking. Deep breathing exercises, progressive muscle relaxation, and mindfulness meditation are all effective methods for reducing stress levels. Guideline: Begin practicing relaxation techniques well before your presentation so they become second nature when needed. ### Step 5: Connect With Your Audience Establishing rapport with your listeners makes public speaking less intimidating and more enjoyable. Greet individuals as they enter the room, make eye contact throughout your speech, and invite questions or comments at appropriate moments. Key tip: Use storytelling and humor to create relatable moments that connect with your audience emotionally. ### Step 6: Focus On Your Message Rather than worrying about potential mistakes or judgment from others, concentrate on conveying your message clearly and passionately. When you genuinely believe in what you're saying, your enthusiasm becomes contagious and helps captivate your audience. Guideline: Avoid using filler words (such as "um" and "like") which may detract from your overall credibility and impact. ### Step 7: Seek Feedback and Improve Continuously After delivering your speech, solicit constructive criticism from trusted friends, colleagues, or mentors. Consider videotaping your presentations to analyze your own performance objectively. Use this feedback to identify areas for improvement and refine your skills over time. Key tip: Maintain a growth mindset - understand that every opportunity to speak publicly is a chance to learn and grow as a speaker. By consistently applying these steps, you'll gradually build confidence in your public speaking abilities and reduce anxiety associated with presenting. Overcoming the fear of public speaking opens doors to new opportunities and enables you to share your thoughts more effectively with others. Do you mean Celia or my sister Moira? Trees can vary a lot. A plant that is similar to a tree, but generally smaller, and may have multiple trunks, or have branches that arise near the ground, is called a "shrub", or a "bush". Since these are common English words there is no precise differentiation between shrubs and trees. Given their small size, bonsai plants would not technically be "trees", but are indeed "trees". Do not confuse the use of tree for a species of plant, with the size or shape of individual specimens. A spruce seedling does not fit the definition of a tree, but all spruces are trees. Men often lose some of their hair as they grow older. This is known as "baldness". Doctors call it "male pattern baldness" because hairs often fall out in similar places. It often begins by hair falling out first from the front of the head, and then from the top of the head. After a while, all that may be left is a some hair running above the ears and around the lower back of the head. Even though it is unusual for women to go bald, many women suffer from thinning hair over the top of their head as they grow old. Beethoven wanted to become famous as a pianist and composer, so he started to get to know important, aristocratic people. Some of these people had already heard him in Bonn when they had traveled there, so his name was becoming known in Vienna. It also helped that he could say he was the pupil of the famous Joseph Haydn. There were a lot of aristocratic people in Vienna who liked music, and many had their own private orchestras. Some of them started to give Beethoven places to live when the Elector of Bonn stopped sending him money in 1794. Beethoven started to perform in private houses, and he became known for his improvisations. In 1795 he performed one of his piano concertos at a concert. Say the number when I ] get this out [noises]. The moisture gathered in clouds eventually falls to the ground as liquid rain or drizzle, or as solid, frozen SNOW or HAIL. Any kind of falling moisture is called precipitation. Rain forms when tiny droplets of water floating in clouds collide to form bigger drops. If the drops get large and heavy enough, the air can no longer support them, and they fall as rain. Rain also forms when falling snowflakes melt high in the air. The bright arch of a rainbow appears in the sky when the Sun shines through falling raindrops. Inside each raindrop, the sunlight is refracted (bent and split) into the seven colours that make it up - red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet. Moisture rises from the Earth's surface and falls back in a never-ending cycle driven by the Sun's energy. As the Sun heats the surface of lakes, oceans, and icefields, moisture evaporates (turns into water vapour), rises into the air, and gathers in clouds. At cooler temperatures, the water vapour condenses (turns into a liquid) and falls back to Earth as rain, snow, or hail. Hailstones are balls of ice that grow from ice crystals. They form inside storm clouds that tower up to 10 km (6 miles) above the ground. The temperature at the base of a storm cloud is warmer than at the top. This causes powerful vertical air currents. Water droplets in the cloud freeze and are whirled up and down. A fresh layer of ice forms around a hailstone each time it is tossed up to the frozen cloud top. Eventually, it gets so heavy it falls to the ground. Snow forms in clouds high in the atmosphere, in temperatures of -20 to -40 C (-4 to -40 F), as water vapour condenses to form ice crystals. The crystals collide and combine to form larger snowflakes, until they get too heavy to float, and drift to the ground. Sleet is a mixture of snow and rain, or partly melted snow. A snow crystal is a single crystal of ice. A snowflake can be one snow crystal, or several stuck together. All snow crystals have a hexagonal (six-sided) structure. This is formed because the tiny water molecules inside ice line up in a regular hexagonal shape called a lattice. You want' 'o tilapia on the cabinet door. Some techniques may work better than others depending on the cause of your congestion, so try them out one by one until you find relief. Title: How to Attend a High School Dance Without a Date Introduction: High school dances can be an exciting yet daunting experience, especially when you don't have a date. However, attending a high school dance without a date is completely normal and nothing to be ashamed of. This guide will help you navigate through the process with confidence and ease. Step 1: Decide Whether You Want to Go Before anything else, decide if you truly want to attend the dance alone or wait for another opportunity where you might have a date. Consider your reasons for wanting to go; are they based on enjoyment, socializing, or just experiencing the event? If yes, then proceed! Key Tips: - Don't feel pressured into going simply because others are. - Understand that it's perfectly fine to enjoy events like this by yourself. Step 2: Find Out Details About the Dance Gather all necessary information about the dance such as its theme, location, dress code, ticket price, etc. This will help you prepare accordingly and make the most out of the night. Key Guidelines: - Note down important dates (like ticket sales start & end). - Ask friends about their plans so you know who else from your circle will be there. Step 3: Choose Your Outfit Pick something comfortable yet stylish that adheres to the dance's dress code. Remember, you should wear what makes YOU feel good, not what you think others would approve of. Key Points: - Comfort is crucial since you'll likely be dancing and moving around quite a bit. - Accessorize appropriately according to the dance's theme. Step 4: Arrange Transportation Make sure you have reliable transportation arranged beforehand. If possible, try coordinating rides with friends heading to the same destination. Important Notes: - Always prioritize safety over convenience. - Have backup options ready in case your primary mode falls through. Step 5: Prepare Conversation Starters Having some conversation starters up your sleeve can come in handy when meeting new people at the dance. Avoid generic topics and aim for more unique ones related to shared experiences or interests. Sample Questions: - What kind of music do you usually listen to? - Which classes do you enjoy most in school? - Did you watch the latest blockbuster movie? What did you think? Step 6: Enjoy the Dance Once everything is set, relax and enjoy the evening. Engage in activities that appeal to you - whether it's hitting the dance floor, taking photos at the photo booth, or chatting with fellow attendees. Crucial Reminders: - Stay positive and open-minded throughout the night. - Keep checking in with yourself to ensure you're having fun and feeling comfortable. Conclusion: Remember, attending a high school dance sans date isn't something to dread but rather an opportunity to create memorable experiences. With the right preparation and attitude, you're bound to have a fantastic time! " "You've always been a Londoner?" "Practically, always. But I was tired of London before all this happened--dying to get out of it. Title: How to Find Your House on Google Earth Google Earth is a powerful tool that allows users to explore satellite imagery of their homes and neighborhoods from anywhere in the world. This detailed guide will walk you through the process of finding your house on Google Earth, with clear explanations and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1:** Download and Install Google Earth Before starting, ensure that you have downloaded and installed the latest version of Google Earth on your computer or mobile device. You can find the application by searching for "Google Earth" in your preferred web browser and following the prompts to download and install it. Having the most recent version ensures optimal performance and access to new features. **Step 2:** Launch Google Earth Once installed, open Google Earth. Familiarize yourself with its layout; at the top left are search tools (magnifying glass icon), navigation controls (compass rose icon), layers panel (stacked squares icon), and settings (gear icon). The main window displays the globe and any saved places as thumbnails below. **Step 3:** Search for Your Address To locate your home, click the magnifying glass icon or press `Ctrl + F` (Windows) / `Cmd + F` (Mac) to reveal the search bar at the top right corner. Type in your full address, including street number, street name, city, state, and postal code. Double-check spelling and formatting to avoid errors. Press Enter or click the magnifying glass button next to the search box. Key tip: If your exact address does not appear, try entering just your street name and town. Zoom out using the "-" button until you see recognizable landmarks near your location. Then manually navigate to your house. **Step 4:** Navigate Around Your Area After pressing enter, Google Earth should automatically fly you to the specified location. Use the mouse wheel to zoom in and out, or hold down the middle mouse button and move your cursor around to pan across the map. To rotate the view, hold down both the right mouse button and drag your cursor. Guideline: Make sure to check if there's more than one result matching your query. Scroll through them and select the correct one based on nearby landmarks such as parks, schools, or major roads. **Step 5:** Identify Your House Now that you're close enough, look for distinguishable features like roof shape, driveway, garage, pool, etc., which may help confirm whether this is indeed your property. Satellite imagery varies in age, so don't be alarmed if things look slightly different now compared to when the photo was taken. Key Tip: Switch between Map, Satellite, and Terrain views under the 'View' menu to get better context and make identifying your house easier. **Step 6:** Save Your Location If you want easy future access to your house's location, consider saving it as a bookmark. Click on the star icon located above the search bar. A popup will ask you to name the place. After naming it, hit 'Save.' Now, whenever you open Google Earth, you can quickly return to your house by clicking on the bookmark. Congratulations! By following these steps, you've successfully found your house on Google Earth. Enjoy exploring other locations and taking advantage of all the features Google Earth has to offer. Too bad it doesn't taste like anything. Computers can become obsolete quickly, depending on what programs the user runs. Very often, they are thrown away within two or three years, because some newer programs require a more powerful computer. This makes the problem worse, so computer recycling happens a lot. Many projects try to send working computers to developing nations so they can be re-used and will not become waste as quickly, as most people do not need to run new programs. Some computer parts, such as hard drives, can break easily. When these parts end up in the landfill, they can put poisonous chemicals like lead into the ground-water. Hard drives can also contain secret information like credit card numbers. If the hard drive is not erased before being thrown away, an identity thief can get the information from the hard drive, even if the drive doesn't work, and use it to steal money from the previous owner's bank account. Title: Get Closer with Nature: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Connecting with nature is essential for our mental, emotional, and physical well-being. This extensive guide will walk you through various steps and techniques to help you get closer to nature, fostering mindfulness, relaxation, and appreciation for the world around you. By following these steps, you can build a meaningful relationship with nature and enjoy its numerous benefits. Step 1: Research Local Natural Spaces Key Tip: Familiarize yourself with nearby parks, trails, gardens, or wildlife reserves that offer opportunities for exploration and connection with nature. Use online resources, maps, or local guides to identify suitable locations. This knowledge will enable you to plan regular visits and gradually develop your bond with nature. Step 2: Prepare Necessary Gear Key Guideline: Equip yourself with appropriate clothing and accessories based on weather conditions and activities. Consider comfortable shoes, layered clothing, hats, sunglasses, sunscreen, water bottles, and insect repellent as necessary. Proper preparation ensures comfort during your outdoor experiences, enhancing overall satisfaction and engagement. Step 3: Learn about Native Flora & Fauna Key Tip: Acquire basic information regarding native plants, trees, birds, mammals, reptiles, amphibians, and insects common in your region. Consult field guides, attend workshops, join local naturalist groups, or engage in citizen science projects to expand your understanding. Knowledgeable observers are more likely to appreciate their surroundings and notice subtle changes over time. Step 4: Practice Mindful Observation Key Technique: Engage all senses while exploring natural spaces - observe sights, sounds, smells, textures, and even tastes. Focus on small details such as leaves' veins, animal tracks, or cloud formations. Regular practice cultivates present-moment awareness, reducing stress and increasing gratitude for nature's beauty. Step 5: Document Your Experiences Key Tool: Keep a journal, sketchbook, camera, or voice recorder handy to document observations, thoughts, emotions, and reflections during your encounters with nature. Artistic expression and written records serve as valuable reminders of memorable moments, encouraging continued interaction with the outdoors. Step 6: Participate in Conservation Efforts Key Action: Contribute positively to environmental conservation by volunteering for cleanups, habitat restoration projects, or educational programs organized by local organizations. Such involvement strengthens personal commitment to preserving natural areas and fosters connections within communities passionate about protecting Earth's biodiversity. Step 7: Cultivate Patience and Stillness Key Attitude: Develop patience and stillness when engaging with nature, resisting the urge to rush through experiences. Allow sufficient time for observation, reflection, and immersion in surrounding landscapes. Practicing stillness encourages introspection, deepens relationships with nature, and promotes inner peace. Step 8: Share Your Passion Key Communication: Inspire others by sharing stories, photographs, artwork, or insights gained from your interactions with nature. Encouraging friends, family members, or colleagues to explore nearby green spaces may create new advocates for environmental protection and enrich both human lives and ecosystem health. Conclusion: By consistently applying these steps and techniques, individuals can foster deeper connections with nature, leading to improved well-being, increased appreciation for Earth's wonders, and enhanced motivation to protect vital habitats. Embrace the journey towards becoming an informed, engaged, and compassionate steward committed to cherishing and safeguarding our planet's precious natural heritage. how will she know what I'm saying? Circle Fractions Frog Craft Shapes, Scissor Skills, Colors, Fractions, and Counting Practice This craft can be used to teach a variety of skills. You don't have to use all the thoughts for lessons... just choose the ones you're working on with your child. Materials you'll need: scissors, glue, printer, paper, CD ROM - the large circle will fit on a CD rom if you'd like to make this a CD Rom craft Thoughts for Lessons: - This craft has lots of nice large circles to cut out -- good scissor - You can precut the template into separate pieces (roughly) to make it a bit easier for beginning crafters to cut out the circles. - The mouth is a half circle. - Use this opportunity to show the children the difference between WHOLE circles and HALF circles. - Ask the children how many half circles (mouths) it would take to make a whole circle. - Instead of using the template mouth, have the children trace a cup or margarine container lid onto red construction paper. - Cut out the circle. - Have the children fold it in HALF and cut it. - This demonstrates that they get two halves (mouths) from one - verbally instruct the children on how to assemble the shapes using the colors of the shapes. (ie: Take the white circle and glue it on the green circle. Take the black circle and glue it on the white circle. Take the red mouth and glue it on the - arrange the circles into piles of very large, large, medium and small - show the children two circles and ask -- is this one bigger, smaller or the same size as that one. - Assemble the eyes (stack a large green, medium white and small black circle). Show the children how this is done OR verbally tell them the steps (practice following verbal direction). - Glue the eyes onto the top of the head - Talk about the 1/2 circle mouth - Glue the mouth onto the head. - OPTIONAL: Tape to a drinking straw to make a puppet OR tape a small piece of coarse sandpaper to the back to make a felt board - Close the template window after printing to return to this screen. - Set page margins to zero if you have trouble fitting the template on one page (FILE, PAGE SETUP or FILE, PRINTER SETUP in most browsers). Emergent Literacy Design By: Sarah Leslie Smith Rationale: This lesson will help children identify the letter R. The sound /r/. They will recognize the phoneme. The representation is the visual growling like a tiger and showing your claws and the sound will be the actual growling like a tiger. Students will be able to pick the /r/ sound out of words when they are spoken or heard. Students will also recognize the word in writing and be able to write words with the letter R. Materials: 1 sheet of primary paper per child. 1 pencil per child. Notecards with words, CARD, PAPER, RAGE, PARTNER, RAGTOP. Board to write words from number 9 on to show students where the letters come in. Book, "Pat's Jam" 1. Say: There are many sounds that make up our language. What language do we have? That's right, English! Today we are going to look at one specific sound. That sound is made by the letter R. It sounds like a tiger growling. Everyone practice making their tiger "RRRRR". 2. Now let's pretend like we are tigers in the jungle or tigers on the football field and say ""RRRR""!! That was scary! We say RRR by closing our mouth and almost putting our teeth together with rounded lips. Our tongue curves around. 3. I am going to say a word then I will find the R in the word. I will listen for the tiger RRR. Ccccc-aaaa-rrrrr-dddd. Card. Where was it? It was carrrrd. I found it! 4. Now you try. I will say this word and you say it and look for the RRR. PPPP-aaa-pppp-eeee-rrrr. Where was the RRR? Say it again slowly to yourselves. It's at the end! We found it! RRR! Now growl like a tiger! Show me your fangs and your claws! 5. Now we will learn to write the letter R. Take out primary paper and pencil. Draw a straight line with a cup around the top and a leg coming out of the cup. There you have an RRR. RRR like a tiger! Now practice writing that letter 10 times! 6. Now write the word RAGE on your paper. Underline the RR in that word. Do the same with Partner and Ragtop. Have these words written so children can copy them. 7. Ask students if they hear R in rip or slip? What about car or map? Watch my mouth when I say the word CAR. Look for the tiger RRR. 8. Practice reading from some pages from the book "Pat's Jam". Read the page with the word "rat". Have them find the RR word. Read the page with the word "car". Have them find the R. 9. Show them how to decide is R in a word. Show them Rap. Help them decide if it is Rap or Tap. Show them deliberately how you move your mouth to find the sound. Do the same with Run. Run or Fun? Ripe. Ripe or Pipe? 10. Assessment. Give children for them to practice. Assess as you move through the lesson. Take notes on children that seem eager and children that aren't speaking up. You can learn a lot throughout the lesson by watching the children. Let children the worksheet. When they finish have them individually read the words and show you the RRR by making a tiger RRR . GRADES 1 AND 2 1.It's Impossible-Have children sit in a circle and one by one finish the sentence, "It's impossible to..." Tell kids that there are no wrong answers. Kids who don't answer within 10 seconds lose their turns. After everyone has had a chance to answer, talk about some of the creative impossibilities. Ask: What is something that's impossible for God to 2. Is It Impossible?-Say: Some things look impossible but they really aren't. Show kids a 3X5 card and ask: Do you believe I can fit my hand through this card? Why or why not? Well, I can fit my hand through this card. Would any of you like to figure out how? Let children try for a solution with extra 3X5 cards. When kids give up, lead kids in the following steps: Fold the card in half lengthwise (see illustration). Draw a series of parallel lines 1/2-inch apart. Cut along the lines through the folded edge, but don't cut through the open edges of the card. Cut along the middle fold leaving the end strips intact. Unfold the card and stick your hand through the hole. 3. It Looks Possible!-Say: Some things look possible but really aren't. Stand up against a wall with your shoulder and right foot touching the wall. Now lift your left foot. (This will be impossible.) Afterward, ask: How did you feel as you tried to do this impossible activity? What are other things that seem impossible for you to do? Can God do those things? Why or why not? 4. Everything Can Be Possible-Read aloud Matthew 19:26. Say: God does things that seem impossible every day. We don't know how some of these things happen, but we believe that God will continue his miracles. This week you'll have a chance to watch your own miracle happen. We're going to plant seeds without using dirt and by next week God will help them grow. Give each child a cup to decorate with crayons. Have children each put a wet paper towel in the bottom of their cup and sprinkle mung beans on the paper towel. Then have them each cover their cup with plastic wrap, secure it with a rubber band and punch small air holes in the plastic wrap. Tell children to keep the towel damp and the seeds will grow into sprouts that the children can eat with 5. Snack-Serve a salad with bean sprouts. Pray: Dear God, help us always have faith in you even when things seem impossible. Thank you for being able to make impossible things possible. Amen. Now Daddy's going to put it right up by your eye. [points to the shape in the book] [picks up the ball at the end of the line; some of the other magnets remain stuck to it, but others fall off and stay on the table] [places the pants on the figure in the book] Entering ICD-9 medical billing codes into Vericle system is an essential task for healthcare providers and medical billers who want to ensure accurate reimbursement from insurance companies. The International Classification of Diseases (ICD) provides standardized diagnostic codes that help insurers identify and pay claims based on specific conditions or procedures. This tutorial will guide you through the process of entering these codes into the Vericle system with clear and actionable steps. Step 1: Understand the Basics of ICD-9 Code Structure Before diving into the Vericle system, familiarize yourself with the structure of ICD-9 codes. Each code consists of three to five digits separated by decimal points. For example, 428.0 represents hypertension without heart disease. Learning this format will make data entry more efficient and accurate. Key tip: Keep up-to-date with any changes or updates to the ICD coding system to avoid errors during data entry. Step 2: Log in to Your Vericle Account Start by launching your web browser and navigating to the Vericle login page. Input your username and password to access your account dashboard. If you don't have an account yet, sign up for one first. Guideline: Make sure your computer meets the minimum requirements set forth by Vericle to prevent technical issues while using their platform. Step 3: Navigate to Patient Records Section From your dashboard, find the patient records section where you can enter new diagnoses or modify existing ones. Typically, there should be a tab labeled 'Patients', 'Encounters', or something similar. Click on it to proceed. Step 4: Select a Patient Choose the appropriate patient record for which you wish to input the ICD-9 code(s). You may need to search for them by name, ID number, or date of birth depending on the options provided. Step 5: Create New Diagnosis Entry Once you've selected the right patient, create a new diagnosis entry within their file. There might be an option like 'Add Diagnosis', 'New Encounter', or simply a blank field for manual input. Fill out all required fields accurately. Step 6: Enter ICD-9 Codes Now comes the main part - entering the actual ICD-9 codes. Type or paste the relevant code(s) into the designated area. Remember to include decimals if applicable. Double-check every digit before moving forward. Key tip: Verify the accuracy of your entered codes against trusted resources such as the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), American Medical Association (AMA), or other authoritative sources. Step 7: Save Changes After confirming the correctness of your inputs, save the changes made to the patient's record. A confirmation message usually appears once successful saving has occurred. Now, the entered ICD-9 codes are integrated into the Vericle system and ready for processing. Step 8: Review Periodic Reports Lastly, periodically review reports generated by the Vericle system to monitor claim submissions, payments, and denials related to the ICD-9 codes you entered. Use these insights to improve future coding practices and optimize revenue cycle management. By following these comprehensive steps, you'll efficiently incorporate ICD-9 codes into the Vericle system, ensuring seamless communication between healthcare providers, patients, and insurance companies. Title: How to Eat a Banana with a Spoon Introduction: While bananas are typically eaten by hand, there may be instances where using utensils is necessary or preferred. This could be due to various reasons such as maintaining cleanliness, practicing table manners, or making it easier to consume for those with motor skill difficulties. Whatever your reason might be, this tutorial will guide you through the process of eating a banana with a spoon. Materials Needed: 1. A ripe banana 2. A suitable spoon (preferably plastic or silicone) 3. A flat surface 4. Optional - a bowl or plate to place the peeled banana Step 1: Choosing Your Banana Select a ripe but firm banana for this task. An overly ripe banana may become mushy and difficult to handle with a spoon. On the other hand, an unripe one would be too hard and not enjoyable to eat. Look for bananas that have yellow skin with small brown spots; these are just right! Step 2: Preparing Your Workspace Find a clean, flat surface like a kitchen countertop or table. If you're concerned about mess, consider placing a napkin, paper towel, or placemat underneath your workspace. Step 3: Cleaning Your Utensil Wash your hands thoroughly before starting the process. Then, rinse your chosen spoon under running water and dry it completely. Using a dirty spoon can introduce bacteria into your food, while wetness can cause the banana to slip off the spoon more easily. Step 4: Peeling the Banana Hold the stem end of the banana firmly between your thumb and index finger. Gently pull downwards to expose about two inches of the fruit. Be careful not to tear the peel entirely from the banana; doing so may make it challenging to keep the banana intact during scooping. Step 5: Positioning the Banana Place the exposed part of the banana onto your chosen plate or bowl. This step isn't mandatory, but having the banana secured in position makes handling it much simpler. Step 6: Cutting the First Scoop Insert the tip of the spoon into the top edge of the exposed banana flesh at approximately a 45-degree angle. Slice downward slowly until the spoon reaches the bottom of the banana piece. The cut should resemble a shallow 'V'. Step 7: Lifting the First Bite Slide the spoon beneath the sliced section, gently lifting it away from the remaining banana. You now hold your first bite! Step 8: Repeating the Process Continue steps six and seven along the length of the banana, ensuring equal spacing between cuts for even sized bites. Remember to maintain the same cutting angle to prevent tearing the banana. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Use a sturdy yet lightweight spoon made of materials such as plastic or silicone. Metal spoons are generally too heavy and could squish the banana. - Always wash your hands and clean your utensils before beginning. - Take care when making cuts to avoid tearing the banana peel or fruit. - Enjoy every bite slowly, savoring the taste and texture of the banana. Conclusion: With patience and practice, consuming a banana with a spoon needn't be daunting. By following our comprehensive guide, you'll soon master this unique way of enjoying nature's sweet treat! Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jane. She was three years old and loved playing outdoors. One day, Jane decided to explore the fields near her home. As she roamed around, Jane found a promise rock. On the rock was written something special. She couldn't read it, so she turned around. Suddenly, Jane noticed a rough-looking man walking towards her. She started to feel scared and hid behind the bush. The man started to talk. He said, "I want you to cover the promise rock. If you don't, I'll take away something you love." Jane was too frightened to move. She thought for a moment and then decided to stand up and face the man. She said, "I will not cover the promise rock. I don't have to do what you say!" The man thought for a moment and then he smiled. He said, "I can see you're brave. I won't make you do anything, but please remember this. Promises should always be kept." With that, the man walked away. Jane was relieved and happily continued her journey, keeping her promise to never forget the promise rock. " The bear wants the old lady and..." Adjusting cabinet drawers is a simple home improvement project that can be done with just a few tools. By following these steps, you'll ensure that your drawers open and close smoothly, reducing wear and tear on both the drawer and the cabinets themselves. **Things You'll Need:** * Screwdriver (either flathead or Phillips, depending on your screws) * A hammer * Shims or thin pieces of cardboard **Step 1: Identify the Problem** The first step in adjusting your cabinet drawers is identifying what needs to be adjusted. Common issues include: - Drawer doesn't fully extend when pulled out. - Drawer sags when opened partway. - Drawer rattles or moves around when closed. - Drawer binds or sticks when opening or closing. Each problem may require different solutions, so it's important to diagnose correctly before proceeding. **Step 2: Gather Necessary Tools** Once you've identified the issue, gather the necessary tools listed above. Make sure they are clean and in good working condition to avoid causing further damage. **Step 3: Empty the Drawer** Before starting any work on the drawer, remove all items from inside. This will make it easier to handle and maneuver while making adjustments. **Step 4: Access the Underside of the Drawer** Most cabinet drawers have an underside panel held by several screws. Remove these screws carefully using your screwdriver. Be careful not to lose them as you'll need them later. **Step 5: Check the Glides** Drawer glides typically come in two varieties: roller glides and ball bearing slides. Both types can become misaligned over time leading to operational problems. Examine the glides closely to see if they're loose, dirty, or damaged. If they appear worn out, consider replacing them entirely rather than trying to readjust. **Step 6: Level the Drawer** If the back of the drawer sits lower than the front, use shims or folded pieces of cardboard under the back edge of the drawer bottom until it's level. Too much lift could cause the drawer to rub against the top of the cabinet opening, so aim for minimal height increase. **Step 7: Align the Drawer** Ensure the sides of the drawer line up evenly with the cabinet opening. Use your hands to gently push or pull the drawer into place if needed. Keep in mind that some slight misalignment might be unavoidable due to manufacturing tolerances. **Step 8: Secure the Drawer** After achieving proper alignment, replace the screws removed earlier to secure the underside panel. Tighten snugly but don't overdo it; allow room for natural movement during operation. **Step 9: Test the Drawer** Slowly pull out and push in the drawer several times to check its action. It should slide freely without binding or sagging. Also, listen for unusual noises which might indicate additional issues. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - Always empty the drawer before beginning adjustments. - Work slowly and methodically to prevent causing new problems. - Regular maintenance checks can help catch potential issues early. - Don't force anything - if something feels wrong, stop and investigate before continuing. - Consider replacing old or heavily worn hardware instead of attempting to repair. By following this guide, you should now have properly aligned and smoothly operating cabinet drawers! let's hide it from pap. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV5/LW/inalw5.cha = = = Did you say it in silent language?
