Once, there was a little girl who loved to go to the playground. She would smile and laugh as she swung on the swing. One day, she happily went to the playground again. However, when she got there, she saw that part of the playground was being repaired. Her smile turned downward and she began to complain. "That's not fair!" she said, pointing to the side that was being fixed. The playground attendant came over to explain that the side was broken and he was fixing it. But the little girl was not happy. She kept complaining and the attendant gave her an idea. He said that if she could build something on the other side that was just as hard to break, maybe he could leave the broken side alone. The girl was excited by the challenge and immediately got to work. She gathered wood, she hammered nails and soon, she had built a sturdy fence. When the attendant saw it, he was astonished. He praised her for her hard work, and declared that the side didn't need to be repaired after all. The little girl was thrilled! She smiled and laughed as she played on both sides of the fence. Is a knife a type of silverware? [S starts in the middle of D's utterance; clicking sound] - slide 1 of 2 Math can be found in lots of places. During autumn you can use falling leaves to create math activities with a fall theme. - slide 2 of 2 Ideas for Math Lesson Using Leaves Collect leaves and use them for sorting activities. Students can sort their leaves by color, leaf type, or size. Encourage them to sort the same leaves in several different ways. Students can work with partners and play a game where one partner sorts the leaves and the other guesses the sorting rule. After students have sorted their leaves several ways, they can choose their favorite and glue them onto a piece of paper and write the sorting rule. For a cute bulletin board display, have them cover their rule with an index card, so that others can guess the rule and check it. A good book to introduce sorting leaves is Fall Leaves Fall! by Zoe Hall. Your students can also use leaves to practice measuring. Give each student five leaves and piece of paper for recording their measurements. Then show them how to measure the leaves, first with a nonstandard unit of measurement such as paper clips or snap cubes and then with a ruler. Have them draw their leaves on their paper and measure each one two ways and record. For more measurement practice put different leaves, pine cones and twigs at your math center along with rulers and cubes so that children can measure them too. Use this lesson idea from Teaching Arithmetic: Lessons for First Grade by Stephanie Sheffield to model subtraction. Give each student a plastic bag and take them on a walk around the school to collect leaves. Tell them to collect about twelve or thirteen leaves. When you get back to class, give the students a large sheet of manila paper and tell them to draw a tree with no leaves on it. Have the student place a certain number of leaves, like 8, on their pictures. Then show them how to gently blow on the leaves and count how many remain on the tree. Ask the children if they added or subtracted when they blew the leaves off the tree. If 5 leaves remain write the subtraction sentence on the board - 8 - ? = 5. Tell the students find out how many leaves blew off the tree. Ask the children if they think the answer will be more or less than 8. Give them a few minutes to work and then discuss their answers and how they got them. Repeat the activity with different numbers. Look here for more great ideas for fall math manipulatives. - Hall, Zoe. Fall Leaves Fall! Scholastic, 2000 - Clarke, Jacqueline. Hands-On Math Around the Year. Scholastic, 2000 - Sheffield, Stephanie. Teaching Arithmetic: Lessons for First Grade. Math Solutions, 2001 MacGyver was an American television series. Some elements, and many isotopes, have what is called an "unstable nucleus". This means the nucleus is either too big to hold itself together or has too many protons or neutrons. When this happens the nucleus has to get rid of the excess mass or particles. It does this through radiation. An atom that does this can be called "radioactive". Unstable atoms continue to be radioactive until they lose enough mass/particles that they become stable. All atoms above atomic number 82 (82 protons, lead) are radioactive. What kind of things did you learn to paint? > Bug World > Bee World > Why do bees hang around flowers? Why do bees hang around flowers? Many plants use nectar as a way of encouraging insects (bees, wasps, butterflies, etc.) to stop at the flower. Honeybees "make their living," so to speak, by gathering nectar from flowers and turning it into honey. In the process of gathering nectar, a worker bee transfers pollen grains from one flower to another and pollinates the flower. This is why the plant made the nectar. Bees also gather the pollen - it is a source of protein for bees. They have little baskets on their hind legs, and they fill the baskets with pollen so they can carry it back to the hive. By mixing honey and pollen together, bees make beebread, one of the foods that bees feed to larva as they are growing. Some bees in the hive produce a food called royal jelly using special glands in their heads. Royal jelly is a special kind of food fed to bee larvae. If you feed nothing but royal jelly to a bee during four critical days while it is growing, you get a queen bee. Sometimes bees gather sticky substances like tree sap. They turn the sap into propolis, which is also called bee glue. Bees use this glue to fix the hive, plug holes and more. Finally, bees at a certain age can make beeswax. They have little glands on their undersides that secrete wax as tiny flakes. The bees grab the wax flakes with their mouths and add them to the hive. Come on you almost messed this up. As well as these real side effects of vaccinations, some people believe that vaccines cause other serious problems like autism, brain damage, or diabetes. There is no evidence for this. Almost all doctors and scientists believe that vaccination does not cause any of these things. [mother starts setting up trees on large flat box top.] "Mr. Julian Hetzel, 43 Beekman Place." Title: How to Call 911: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: In emergency situations, every second counts. Knowing how to call 911 can make a significant difference in receiving timely assistance from first responders. This guide will provide you with an in-depth understanding of when and how to contact 911, along with essential tips and guidelines to ensure effective communication during emergencies. It is important to remember that this information should be used responsibly and only during actual emergencies. Section 1: When to Call 911 Step 1: Identify Life-Threatening Emergency Situations Determine if the situation involves immediate danger or harm to individuals, such as medical crises (heart attacks, strokes), fires, severe accidents, violent crimes, or any other incidents where lives are at risk. Key tip: Trust your instincts - if something feels wrong, don't hesitate to call 911. Step 2: Assess Non-Life Threatening Incidents Consider whether the issue requires immediate attention but does not pose an imminent threat to life, like theft, vandalism, minor injuries, or non-urgent medical concerns. If so, consider reporting these matters through alternative methods, such as local law enforcement agencies' non-emergency numbers or online portals. Guideline: Save 911 lines for true emergencies. Section 2: Preparation Before Making the Call Key Tip: Familiarize yourself with basic information about your location beforehand to facilitate efficient communication during stressful moments. Step 3: Memorize Your Address and Cross Streets Ensure you know your current address and nearby cross streets accurately. This knowledge allows dispatchers to quickly send help by identifying the exact location of the emergency. Step 4: Keep Important Phone Numbers Handy Store critical phone numbers (family members, doctors, neighbors) in your contacts list for easy access during emergencies. Additionally, programming ICE (In Case of Emergency) contacts within your phone enables first responders to swiftly reach loved ones in case of incapacitation. Section 3: Making the Call Step 5: Dial 91 Well some people are just natural doing some things. I just wanna talk on the phone. I don't know if they're in there anymore. Title: Draw Manga on MS Paint Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through creating your own manga drawing using Microsoft Paint (MS Paint). While MS Paint is a basic painting program compared to other software like Adobe Photoshop or Illustrator, it still provides sufficient tools to create simple manga drawings. This tutorial assumes that you have some familiarity with MS Paint, but even if you don't, this guide should help you get started. Step 1: Open MS Paint To start, open MS Paint by searching for it in the Windows search bar or navigating to Accessories >Paint from the Start menu. Once opened, maximize the window for easier access to tools. Step 2: Set up Canvas Size Before starting any artwork, set up an appropriate canvas size. Go to Resolution > Width and Height and enter your preferred dimensions. For manga-style art, consider portrait orientation, as it suits most panels better than landscape. A good default size would be 2000x3000 pixels. Click OK once done. Key Tip: Choose higher resolution settings for more detail and larger print sizes later. Step 3: Sketch Basic Outlines Start sketching rough outlines using the Brush tool under Tools. Beginners might find it helpful to draw construction lines first - these are guides used to position elements accurately within the frame. To do so, enable Gridlines via View>Gridlines. You can also adjust grid spacing via Options>Grid Spacing. Guideline: Common ratios for character proportions include 7 heads tall for adults and around 6 for children. Step 4: Refine Lines Once happy with initial sketches, refine them into clearer shapes and forms. Switch to the Pen tool under Tools; its straightedge feature allows cleaner linework. If needed, zoom in closer (View>Zoom) for precision. Remember to save frequently (File>Save As...) to prevent loss of work due to errors. Step 5: Add Base Colors Fill in base colors where applicable. Use the Fill Tool found below the Color 1 palette. Before filling, ensure there are no gaps between outline strokes which could cause unwanted color leaks. Fix these issues using the Select tool and Eraser. Key Tip: Layering multiple transparent shades over one another creates richer hues without overwhelming brightness. Step 6: Create Shading Gradients Manga often features smooth gradients instead of harsh shadows. Accomplish this effect by selecting areas with the Rectangular Selection tool, then applying different tones gradually using the Airbrush tool. Repeat steps until satisfied with results. Step 7: Apply Detailing & Highlights Add details such as facial features, clothing textures, etc., with the Brush tool. Make sure they match the overall style of your piece. Afterward, add highlights strategically to give life and dimension to characters. Keep highlight placement consistent throughout scenes. Step 8: Final Touches Review your artwork carefully for mistakes or inconsistencies before finalizing. Consider adding backgrounds, speech bubbles, sound effects, or additional visual cues depending on what best fits your narrative. Save again after all changes made. Conclusion: Drawing manga on MS Paint requires patience and practice, especially since it lacks advanced features available in specialized software. However, mastery of basics here translates well when transitioning to professional programs later. Title: How to Fix Broken Fingernails Broken fingernails can be both painful and unsightly. However, with careful attention and the right tools, you can nurse your nails back to health. This article will guide you through the process of fixing broken fingernails, providing detailed steps and helpful tips along the way. **Things You'll Need:** * Nail clippers or scissors * Emery board or nail file * Cuticle oil or cream * Clear base coat polish * Optional: Tea tree oil, lavender essential oil, or other antiseptic agents ## **Step 1: Clean the Damaged Area** First things first - clean the damaged area thoroughly using soap and water. It is crucial to remove any dirt or bacteria that could potentially cause infection. If there are any signs of bleeding, gently dab the wound with a sterile gauze pad until it stops. For added disinfection, consider applying tea tree oil or another antiseptic agent directly onto the affected area. ## **Step 2: Trim Off Ragged Edges** Using either nail clippers or scissors (whichever feels more comfortable), trim off any jagged edges around the break. Be sure not to cut too close to the skin as this may lead to further damage or pain. The goal here is simply to create a smooth edge while minimizing potential snags. ## **Key Tip**: Always ensure your tools are sharp; dull instruments can tear rather than cut, causing additional harm. ## **Step 3: File Down Sharp Corners** After trimming, use an emery board or nail file to soften the remaining corners of the nail. Gently move the file in one direction only (back-and-forth motion might weaken the nail even more) until all rough spots have been smoothed out. ## **Guideline**: When filing, maintain gentle pressure and keep the file flat against the surface of the nail. Applying excessive force or tilting the file at an angle might result in uneven shaping or further breakage. ## **Step 4: Moisturize Your Nails** Healthy nails require moisture, so don't skip this step! Apply cuticle oil or cream around the entire perimeter of the nail bed, focusing particularly on the areas where the skin meets the nail. Regular moisturization promotes healthy growth and reduces the likelihood of future breaks. ## **Key Tips**: * Use natural oils like jojoba, almond, or olive oil if cuticle products aren't available. * Consider wearing gloves during household chores to protect your hands from harsh chemicals and abrasive materials. ## **Step 5: Protect Your Nail with Base Coat** Before painting your nails, apply a clear base coat to shield the vulnerable surface underneath. A quality base coat provides several benefits including preventing staining, improving adhesion of subsequent coats, and adding strength to weakened nails. Allow the base coat to dry completely before proceeding to the next step. Now that you've followed these instructions, congratulations! You've successfully addressed your broken fingernail issue. Remember, consistent care and maintenance play significant roles in maintaining strong, beautiful nails. There are a lot of tutorials and sample code available showing how to implement the SAT collision detection algorithm. But can someone explain, without math or code, what are the general principls behind this technique. The basic idea behind the Separating Axis Theorem (SAT) is that if we can find a gap or separation between two objects then they must not be touching. The corollary is that if we cannot find a gap between them, the objects are touching. But how can we be sure that there is no gap, how can we be sure we've looked sufficiently hard for a gap? Let's think about what happens in the real world, when two objects are very close to each other and we want to check if they are touching or not; We look along the edges of the objects! Imagine the scene in the image above. You have to help your friend in the red car to parallel park into a tight spot. 'Bump parking' is considered rude where I come from, so you want to position yourself where you can see clearly if is getting too close to the white car. You are probably going to position yourself close to 'A' and look along the yellow line. If you look that way and see daylight between the two cars, you know they are not touching. If the cars get VERY close, then looking along the yellow line is the only way to spot the very moment of collision. What we are doing here is looking along the rear edge of the white car. How did we know to look along that particular edge? In the real world we use our intuition. We know that looking along the purple line [long edge of the red car] or the green line [long edge of the white car] will not help in this situation. But a computer doesn't have intuition, so we will ask it to "look" along all edges of both objects. If it finds a gap along even one of those edges then there's no collision. If it finds no gap on any of those edges, then that means the objects have collided. How do we get the computer to 'look along an edge'? We use projections. We find a line (any line) at right angles to the edge we are considering and project both objects onto that edge. This line is called an 'axis' (not necessarily a coordinate axis, like x-axis or y-axis,) A projection is a one dimensional version of the object (much like a shadow is a two dimensional version of a 3D object). So we project all the points (usually just the corners) of the objects on to the axis. The projection converts the 2D points into distances along the axis. Now we can check if there is a gap between the projections. There is a gap if the smallest projection of one is greater than the largest projection of the other. We repeat this process for all the edges, if a gap(separation) is found, that proves that the objects are not colliding. If we check all the edges of both objects and can't find a gap, them we can conclude the objects are touching. For the underlying math, and examples of how to decide which lines you should be projecting on to, read How do I get the axes for SAT collision detection Once upon a time there was a hero. His name was Mike. Mike was very strong, brave and helpful. He was a hero who everyone admired and loved. One day, Mike was going to save a hurt puppy from a tall tree. When he tried to climb the tree, he was very clumsy. He fell down many times! But he didn't give up. Eventually, he made it to the top of the tree and rescued the puppy. Everyone cheered for Mike. He was their hero! Everyone admired Mike for his bravery and courage. They all gave him lots of hugs and praised him. Mike was so happy that he had been able to help the puppy. From then on, everyone called him "Hero Mike". Why don't you try that one first? We haven't taken any time with you lately, have we? That would be very tricky if you could do that. Title: How to Avoid Herpes Herpes is a common sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by the herpes simplex virus (HSV). There are two types of herpes simplex viruses: HSV-1, which usually causes cold sores or fever blisters around the mouth, and HSV-2, which primarily causes genital herpes. While there's no surefire way to avoid herpes completely, you can significantly reduce your risk by following these steps. **Step 1: Understand the Risks and Symptoms** Before engaging in any sexual activity, make sure you understand the risks and symptoms associated with herpes. Knowing what to look out for can help you protect yourself and seek medical attention if necessary. Common herpes symptoms include small, fluid-filled blisters that break open and leave painful sores. These may appear anywhere on the body but are most commonly found near the mouth or genitals. Other symptoms can include tingling, burning, or itching sensations in the area where sores will develop. Key Tip: Keep in mind that many people with herpes show no signs or symptoms at all, making it easy to unknowingly spread the virus. **Step 2: Practice Abstinence or Be Monogamous** The safest way to avoid herpes is to abstain from sexual contact altogether. If this isn't an option for you, consider being in a mutually monogamous relationship with someone who has been tested and confirmed to be free of STIs. This greatly reduces your chances of contracting herpes and other STIs. Guideline: Regular testing is essential when practicing monogamy to ensure both partners remain STI-free. **Step 3: Use Protection During Sexual Activity** Using protection during oral, anal, or vaginal sex can lower your chance of getting herpes. Dental dams can also provide a barrier during oral sex. However, keep in mind that condoms and dental dams aren't foolproof as they only cover part of the skin where lesions might occur. They won't protect against asymptomatic shedding - when the virus is present on the skin without causing visible sores. Still, using protection consistently decreases your overall risk. Key Tip: Latex barriers like condoms offer better protection than lambskin ones, as the latter may have tiny pores large enough for the virus to pass through. **Step 4: Limit Your Number of Sexual Partners** Having fewer sexual partners lowers your likelihood of coming into contact with the herpes virus. Each new partner increases your potential exposure to various STIs, including herpes. By limiting your number of partners, you limit your overall risk. Guideline: Always discuss sexual health history openly and honestly with new partners before becoming intimate. **Step 5: Get Vaccinated** There are vaccines available for HSV-2 and some strains of HSV-1. Although these don't guarantee immunity, they do offer some level of protection. Speak with your healthcare provider about whether getting vaccinated makes sense for you. Key Tip: Even after vaccination, continue taking precautions to minimize your risk of contracting herpes. Remember, while these steps can reduce your risk, they cannot eliminate it entirely due to the nature of the virus. Stay informed, communicate openly with sexual partners, get regular checkups, and maintain a healthy lifestyle to boost your immune system and overall wellbeing. Title: How to Cancel Your AT&T Wireless Service Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of canceling your AT&T Wireless service. We'll cover everything from understanding your contract obligations to ensuring that your account is properly closed. Please note that cancellation fees may apply if you are still under contract. Let's get started! Step 1: Review Your Contract Obligations Before initiating the cancellation process, review your contract terms and conditions. If you're within the minimum term commitment (usually 2 years), you might be subject to early termination fees. Understanding these charges upfront can help avoid surprises later on. Key Tip: Keep an eye out for any promotional offers or discounts that required a service agreement. These could impact your final bill. Step 2: Back Up Important Data Prior to closing your account, make sure all important data such as contacts, photos, messages, etc., are backed up. Once the line is disconnected, you won't have access to this information anymore. Guideline: You can use AT&T's cloud storage service, Google Drive, iCloud, or other third-party applications for backup purposes. Step 3: Pay Off Any Outstanding Balances Ensure there are no outstanding balances on your account before requesting cancellation. Unpaid bills could prevent the successful closure of your account. Step 4: Contact AT&T Customer Care You can reach out to AT&T via phone at 611 from your AT&T device or 800.331.0500 from any phone. Alternatively, chat support is available through the myAT&T app or website. inform them about your intention to cancel the service. Key Tips: - Have your account number handy. - Be prepared to provide reason(s) for cancellation. - Ask about potential cancellation fees. Step 5: Confirm Cancellation Details Double check all details regarding the cancellation with the representative including effective date, remaining balance, and any applicable fees. It's crucial to confirm whether you need to return devices like phones or hotspots. Step 6: Return Equipment (If Necessary) If you were asked to return equipment, do so promptly using the provided shipping label. Failure to return equipment may result in additional charges. Step 7: Check Final Bill After cancellation, wait for your final bill. Verify its accuracy against what was discussed during the call. Report discrepancies immediately. Step 8: Dispose Of SIM Card Properly Once your service has been cancelled, cut or destroy your SIM card to ensure sensitive information isn't misused. Conclusion: Cancelling your AT&T Wireless service involves several steps, but by following this comprehensive guide, you should be able to navigate the process smoothly. Remember, clear communication with customer care representatives and thorough double checking go a long way towards making sure everything goes according to plan. Good luck! Creating a secure login script in PHP and MySQL is an essential skill for any web developer looking to build user-authenticated applications. This tutorial will guide you through creating such a script while highlighting best practices for security and data integrity. **Step 1: Setting up your development environment** Before starting, ensure that you have a local development environment with PHP and MySQL installed. You can download XAMPP or MAMP stacks which include Apache, MySQL, PHP, and other necessary tools. These packages make installation straightforward and allow you to focus on learning rather than setting up servers. **Step 2: Designing the database schema** Our application needs one table called 'users' with three columns - id (primary key), username, and password. We'll be using bcrypt as our hashing algorithm, so no need to store plain text passwords. ```sql CREATE TABLE `users` ( `id` int(11) NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, `username` varchar(50) NOT NULL, `password` varchar(60) NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (`id`), UNIQUE KEY `username` (`username`) ); ``` The unique constraint on the username field ensures that every user has a distinct username. **Step 3: Registering users** Firstly, let's create a registration form where users input their details. Then we validate these inputs server-side before inserting them into the database. *index.php*: ```html <form action="register.php" method="post"> <label>Username:</label><input type="text" name="username"><br> <label>Password:</label><input type="password" name="password"><br> <button type="submit">Register</button> </form> [Mother explains to Courtney how much Sarah loves to move things one by one from room to room] We went up in Forbore with XXX and um... The weight of each object will be between 1 and 40 pounds inclusive and will be a round number. What's the fewest number of weights that you need to be able to balance any of these objects? (If you think you have the answer, see the Answer before you see the Hint. The answer is just the number of weights, not what they are.) Hint: Your weights don't all need to go on the same side of the balance. Solution: You need weights of 1, 3, 9, and 27 pounds (powers of three). If you want to weigh an object of, say 32 pounds, then on one side of the balance you put the object to be weighed along with the 1 and 3 pound weight. On the other side you put the 9 and 27 pound weight. Both sides add up to the same weight and the balance is horizontal, confirming that the object was 32 pounds. If your best answer was 6 weights, using the binary scheme of 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, and 32 pounds, then your solution was able to weigh objects up to 63 pounds. This is more than was required (40 pounds), so you know that your solution is not optimal. Update 9/21/2007: Ken Belcher came up with a clever way to use four weights to weigh items up to 81 pounds. You double the weights above so that you have 2, 6, 18, and 54 pound weights. You then use the above scheme if your test object has an even weight. If it has an odd weight, you test the two even weights around it to show that it's greater than one and less than the other. Since test objects always have weights that are round numbers, you know it must be the odd number between the two even ones. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV1/ER/emier1.cha = = = Not a lot of them brought theirs [laughing]. Sure! So here's an educational piece related to the snippet above that's targeted towards grade-school students. Let's talk about tangent lines to curves. Have you ever seen a straight line that touches a curve at one point and then goes off in its own direction? That's called a "tangent line." It's kind of like when you're trying to draw a picture of a circle, but your pencil slips and leaves the circle for a little bit before coming back to it. The line that your pencil makes while it's not touching the circle is a tangent line. Now, imagine we have a curve given by the equation y = -x^2. This is a parabola that opens downwards, like a hill that tips over backwards. Can we find a tangent line that goes through a specific point, like (0, -9)? Well, let's try! First, we need to figure out where the tangent line should touch the curve. We do that by finding the derivative of y = -x^2, which tells us the slope of the curve at any point. The derivative of -x^2 is -2x. Next, we set the derivative equal to the slope of the tangent line. Since the tangent line has to go through (0, -9), we know that its slope is (-9 - y)/(0 - x) = -9/x. Setting these two slopes equal gives us -2x = -9/x. Solving for x, we get x^2 = 9/2, so x = 3/sqrt(2). These are the x-coordinates of the points where the tangent lines will touch the curve. Finally, we substitute these values into the equation of the curve to find the corresponding y-coordinates, giving us y = -225/4 and y = -7/4. So our two tangent lines will pass through the points (-3/sqrt(2), -225/4) and (3/sqrt(2), -7/4) and also go through (0, -9). But wait! You may notice that there's something strange going on with the first point, (-3/sqrt(2), -225/4). When we plug this value into the derivative, we get a vertical slope, which means that the tangent line doesn't exist! In fact, this part of the curve is very steep and curved, so no tangent line could possibly go through both the curve and the point (0, -9). So unfortunately, even though we found another solution, we still don't have a tangent line that satisfies all the conditions. Just like sometimes we can't build a ramp between two different heights, sometimes we can't find a tangent line that fits exactly where we want it to. Oh well, maybe next time! [Nina points to the picture of two little girls pushing doll carriages. The picture is on the package that the crib and carriage were in.] [turning the pages of the book to show BLA] Title: How to Clean Tiles with Vinegar Introduction: Cleaning tiles with vinegar is an affordable, eco-friendly, and effective method that can be done using household items. This guide will walk you through the steps to clean your tiles thoroughly and safely. Materials Needed: 1. White distilled vinegar 2. Warm water 3. A bucket 4. A mop or sponge 5. Scrub brush (optional) 6. Baking soda (optional) 7. Microfiber cloth or paper towels 8. Rubber gloves (recommended) Step 1: Gather Your Materials Before starting, ensure all materials are within reach. Protective rubber gloves may prevent skin irritation from prolonged contact with vinegar. Step 2: Create Your Cleaning Solution Mix equal parts white distilled vinegar and warm water in a bucket. For instance, if you're cleaning a small area, half a cup of both should suffice; adjust accordingly based on space size. The acidity in vinegar breaks down dirt, grime, and soap scum while sanitizing surfaces naturally. Key Tip: Never use apple cider or other types of vinegar as they contain sugars and colorants that could leave residue or stains on your tiles. Step 3: Clear Debris Sweep or vacuum the floor to remove loose dirt particles which might scratch tile surfaces during mopping. Step 4: Apply the Vinegar Solution Dip your mop into the solution and wring it out so it's damp but not dripping wet. Overly saturated mops can lead to water seeping under the tiles causing damage over time. Mop the floors slowly ensuring every inch gets covered. Let sit for about five minutes to allow the vinegar to break down tougher stains. Step 5: Scrub If Necessary If there are stubborn spots, sprinkle some baking soda onto them before scrubbing gently with a soft bristle brush. Avoid abrasive tools that can harm the tile surface. Afterward, reapply the vinegar solution to rinse off loosened debris. Step 6: Rinse Thoroughly Rinse the mop in clean water then go over the entire area again to remove any remaining vinegar residue. It's crucial to eliminate all traces of vinegar because its acidic nature can etch certain stone tiles when left too long. Key Guideline: Always do a spot test first on inconspicuous areas if unsure whether your tiles can tolerate vinegar. Step 7: Dry Completely Use microfiber cloths or old towels to dry the tiles. Preventing excess moisture reduces the risk of mold growth and further protects your tiles. Plus, drying gives you a chance to check for missed spots. Maintenance Tips: - Regular maintenance prevents buildup making deep cleans less frequent. - Immediately attend to spills, especially those containing sugar or grease, to minimize stain chances. - Consider using entrance mats to trap outside dirt and reduce tracking indoors. Conclusion: With these simple steps, you now know how to clean tiles effectively using vinegar. Enjoy your sparkling clean tiles knowing you used safe, cost-efficient products! Other writing systems do not use letters, but they do (at least in part) represent sounds. For example, many systems represent syllables. In the past such writing systems were used by many cultures, but today they are almost only used by languages people speak in Asia. A "syllabary" is a system of writing that is similar to an alphabet. A syllabary uses one symbol to indicate each syllable of a word, instead of one symbol for each letter of the word. For example, a syllabary would use one symbol to mean the syllable "ga", instead of two letters of the alphabet "g" and "a". I want some of that diaper pins. Title: Understanding Movement with Basic Math Concepts Have you ever wondered how to describe or predict the movement of an object over time? In higher levels of math, we learn more complex ways to do this, but today we will explore some basic concepts that even younger students can understand! Imagine you are watching a toy car move across a straight track. You want to know how far it travels during its journey. To figure this out, you need to measure both the speed (how fast it's going) and the time it spends moving. Let's say the toy car moves according to the following rule: "For every second that passes, the car covers a distance equal to twice the current time." We could write this mathematically as: distance = 2 * time^2 Now, let's calculate how far the toy car travels in the first 2 seconds: * At 1 second, it would have traveled 2 \* 1^2 = 2 units of distance * At 2 seconds, another 2 \* 2^2 = 8 units of distance Adding these together, we find that the toy car covered 10 units of distance in those first 2 seconds! Next, imagine trying to model not just the position of one toy car, but also the motion of a friend's toy car racing alongside yours on a separate track. Both cars follow their own unique rules of movement. By comparing their velocities - which show how quickly each car changes position over time - we can determine which one is faster or slower and when they meet along the tracks. This idea leads us into studying functions, equations, and graphs - all fundamental building blocks of algebraic thinking! Lastly, consider a playground scenario where you see two different paths drawn on the ground with chalk. One path forms part of a circle while the other follows a straight line. Now suppose you wanted to find the region between these two paths that represents your playing space. The key here lies in understanding area conceptually; breaking down complex shapes into smaller pieces, finding areas of known shapes within them (such as triangles or rectangles), then adding up their contributions until you reach the total area encompassed by both paths combined. While these activities may sound advanced, remember that many mathematical ideas build upon earlier knowledge. Familiarity with counting numbers, addition, multiplication, fractions, geometry basics, and early algebra provide strong foundations for tackling more challenging problems later on. So keep practicing and exploring mathematical connections in real-world contexts like these - who knows what fascinating discoveries await? Um yeah so she's seen some of it. Title: How to Clean Ink Stains Out of Carpet Introduction: Ink stains can be quite daunting to remove from carpets due to their deep penetration into the carpet fibers. However, with the right approach and materials, you can effectively clean ink stains without causing any damage to your carpet. This comprehensive guide will take you through the necessary steps, providing detailed explanations and useful tips along the way. By following these instructions carefully, you'll increase your chances of successfully removing the ink stain and restoring your carpet to its former glory. Things You'll Need: - Paper towels or cloth rags - Rubbing alcohol (isopropyl) - Dish soap - Ammonia - Hydrogen peroxide (for white or light-colored carpets only) - Spray bottle - Soft-bristled brush or sponge - Vacuum cleaner Step 1: Blot Excess Ink Start by gently blotting the ink stain using paper towels or clean cloth rags. Avoid rubbing the stain as this may spread the ink further into the carpet fibers. The goal is to absorb as much excess ink as possible before proceeding with cleaning agents. Be patient during this process; it might take some time to lift most of the ink off the surface. Key Tip: Use white paper towels or cloths to prevent dye transfer onto your carpet. Step 2: Test Cleaning Solutions Before applying any cleaning solution, test it on an inconspicuous area of the carpet to ensure that it doesn't cause discoloration or damage. Wait for about 24 hours after testing to make sure there are no adverse reactions. Step 3: Apply Rubbing Alcohol Pour a small amount of rubbing alcohol (isopropyl) onto a clean cloth or rag. Gently blot the ink stain with the dampened cloth, focusing on one section at a time. Remember not to scrub or rub vigorously, as doing so could damage the carpet fibers and potentially spread the ink. Allow the rubbing alcohol to sit on the stain for several minutes to break down the ink. Explanation: Rubbing alcohol acts as a solvent, helping to dissolve the ink particles and making them easier to remove from the carpet fibers. It also has minimal impact on most carpet materials when used correctly. Step 4: Rinse and Blot Rinse the treated area with warm water, then press firmly with dry paper towels or cloths to absorb any remaining moisture. Repeat this process until no more color transfers onto the towel or rag. Key Guideline: Make sure all traces of rubbing alcohol have been removed, as residues can attract dirt and debris over time. Title: How to Divide Polynomials Polynomial division is a fundamental skill in algebra that allows you to solve complex equations by breaking them down into simpler parts. This tutorial will guide you through the process of dividing polynomials with clear explanations, helpful tips, and practical examples. ### Step 1: Understand the Basics of Polynomials Before diving into polynomial division, let's review what polynomials are. A polynomial is an expression consisting of variables (also called indeterminates) and coefficients, involving operations of addition, subtraction, multiplication, and non-negative integer exponents of variables. For example, 4x^2 + 3x - 5 is a polynomial. It has three terms: 4x^2 (the first term), 3x (the middle term), and -5 (the last term or constant). The degree of a polynomial is the highest power of the variable in any given term. Key tip: Make sure you understand the structure and components of polynomials before attempting to divide them. ### Step 2: Set Up the Long Division Format To begin dividing polynomials, set up your equation using the long division format as follows: (dividend) / (divisor) = quotient For example, if you want to divide (2x^2 - 7x + 6) by (x - 3): * (2x^2 - 7x + 6) is the dividend * (x - 3) is the divisor * You want to find out what goes here: (_____) So the setup looks like this: (2x^2 - 7x + 6) / (x - 3) = ? Guideline: Always remember that when performing polynomial long division, the term with the highest degree should be written first both in the dividend and the divisor. ### Step 3: Determine the First Term of the Quotient Multiply the divisor (x - 3) by the first term of the quotient (which we don't know yet). Place this product under the first two terms of the dividend. (2x)(x) = 2x^2 Write it above the line as shown below: ```markdown (2x) ------------------- Okay so we're having a strawberry cake. Make Your Own Rubber Stamp Flashcards Looking for a way to make learning to count more fun? Here's an activity to inspire your child to recognize numbers by having her make homemade flashcards. All you need are some rubber stamps and index cards to get started! Kids love using rubber stamps, and because craft stores carry many fun shapes, your child will be able to personalize her very own flashcards and learn a great study strategy that'll help her in years to come. What You Need: - Rubber stamps - Stamp pad - 10 blank cards from cardstock What You Do: - Cut your cardstock so that you have ten cards. Depending on your stamp size, make sure your card is large enough to hold ten stamps. - Using markers, write the numbers one through ten on your flashcards. - Ask your child to say the number on the card and then to stamp that many pictures. You might have to show her how to do the first one. If you have multiple-colored stamp pads, show her how to wipe the stamp clean before changing to a different color. Have your child count out loud while stamping. This reinforces one-to-one correspondence, when what she says matches the object. - Continue stamping different stamps on all the flashcards. When finished, try out your rubber stamp flashcards. Hold them up and ask her to say the number and count the stamps. If she gets it correct, she may keep the card. Title: How to Obtain the Bee Shield in Borderlands 2 Borderlands 2 is an action role-playing first-person shooter video game developed by Gearbox Software. One of the most sought-after shields in the game is the Bee shield due to its incredible damage output. This tutorial will guide you through obtaining this powerful piece of equipment. **Step 1: Understand the Basics of the Bee Shield** The Bee shield, also known as the "The Hive," is a legendary shield that has a high chance of generating shock elemental novas upon taking damage. Its unique property lies in its absurdly high base bonus capacitor capacity (also referred to as "Capacity"), which allows it to store more energy than any other shield in the game. The stored energy then translates into increased gun damage when depleted, making it highly desirable. Key Tip: Remember, while using the Bee shield, your primary focus should be maintaining its charge so you can benefit from the massive boost in weapon damage. **Step 2: Reach Appropriate Level and Farming Location** To acquire the Bee shield, you need to reach level 50 or higher since legendaries don't scale beyond level 50. Moreover, farming for the Bee shield becomes efficient at Hyperius the Invincible, who is accessible after completing the Captain Scarlett DLC campaign. He drops the shild with a reasonable frequency. Guideline: If you haven't reached level 50 yet or don't have access to the required DLC, consider progressing further in the storyline or purchasing the necessary expansion pack. **Step 3: Prepare Your Character Build** Before challenging Hyperius, ensure your character build supports either dealing sufficient damage quickly or surviving prolonged fights effectively. Skills enhancing critical hit chances, gun damages, health regen, or shield recharge rates are beneficial. Also, carry weapons capable of inflicting substantial amounts of damage swiftly. Key Tip: Aim for quick kills during the fight against Hyperius to minimize potential threats like his add-on enemies - varkids. **Step 4: Engage Hyperius the Invincible** Join a public game or invite friends to help increase your odds of success. Position yourself correctly, avoid his attacks, and deal enough damage within the time limit to trigger his second phase where he splits into two entities. Quickly eliminate both forms before they regenerate their health completely. Remember, defeating Hyperius isn't strictly necessary every single attempt; what matters most is killing him fast enough to make loot dropping worthwhile. Guideline: Learn Hyperius' attack patterns to dodge them efficiently and maximize your DPS windows. **Step 5: Loot Hunting** After defeating Hyperius, move towards his corpse promptly to collect any fallen loot. Use the "Interact" button (default F) to inspect the area if no item appears immediately. Keep repeating steps 4 & 5 until the coveted Bee shield finally drops. Patience is vital here, as RNGesus might take several attempts to bless you with the legendary prize. Key Tip: To speed up the process, try changing areas right after killing Hyperius instead of waiting for the full respawn timer. Returning instantly may cause the boss to reset without having fully spawned back, thus saving some time between runs. In conclusion, acquiring the Bee shield requires determination, preparation, and understanding of mechanics involved. But once equipped, prepare to wreak havoc across Pandora! Could you please stand over here? Turn it over and color some more. Adding credits to your Wii Fit game can be a great way to unlock new exercises, games, and features. This guide will walk you through the process step-by-step, providing detailed explanations and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1: Determine if Your Wii Fit Game Supports Credit Purchases** Not all versions of Wii Fit support credit purchases. The original Wii Fit, as well as Wii Fit Plus, allow users to add additional credits to their account. However, more recent iterations of the game do not have this feature. To check if your version supports credit purchases, navigate to the main menu and look for an option labeled "Shop" or "Store." If this option is present, then your game likely supports credit purchases. **Key Tip:** Make sure your Wii console is connected to the internet before attempting to purchase credits. You will need a stable connection to complete the transaction. **Step 2: Select the Appropriate Payment Method** Wii Fit accepts several different payment methods for purchasing credits, including major credit cards (Visa, MasterCard, American Express), PayPal, and Nintendo eShop gift cards. Choose the method that best suits your needs and budget. **Guideline:** Be aware of any fees associated with your chosen payment method. Some credit card companies may charge foreign transaction fees if you are making a purchase from outside your home country. **Step 3: Navigate to the Shop Menu** From the main menu of Wii Fit, select the "Shop" or "Store" option. This should bring up a list of available items, such as new exercises and games. At the top of the screen, there should be an option to purchase credits using one of the supported payment methods. **Key Tip:** Keep track of your current credit balance so you know when it's time to refill. **Step 4: Enter Your Payment Information** When prompted, enter your payment information according to the format requested by the system. Double-check all entries for accuracy before submitting your order. **Guideline:** Consider setting up parental controls to prevent unauthorized purchases, especially if young children will be accessing the Wii Fit game. **Step 5: Confirm Your Order** Once you have entered your payment information, review the details of your order carefully. Check the total cost, quantity of credits being purchased, and any applicable taxes or fees. When you are satisfied with the information displayed, confirm your order to proceed with the purchase. **Key Tip:** Save a copy of your receipt for future reference. This can help resolve any potential disputes or errors regarding your purchase. **Step 6: Wait for the Transaction to Complete** After confirming your order, wait for the transaction to complete. Depending on your internet connection speed and other factors, this could take anywhere from a few seconds to several minutes. Once the transaction has been processed successfully, you should receive confirmation that the credits have been added to your Wii Fit account. **Guideline:** Avoid interrupting the transaction by closing the application or turning off the Wii console during the processing period. **Step 7: Enjoy New Features and Exercises** With your newly acquired credits, explore the expanded range of options now available within Wii Fit. Try out new exercises, play exciting mini-games, or customize your experience in ways previously unavailable. Regularly checking back for updates ensures that you always have fresh content at your fingertips. **Key Tip:** Remember to warm up properly before engaging in strenuous activities and cool down afterwards to avoid injury. Lily and Ben were twins who liked to play together. They had many toys, but their favorite was a big box. They pretended the box was a spaceship, a castle, a boat, and many other things. One day, their mom said she had to mail some letters. She asked Lily and Ben to help her put the letters in the box. Lily and Ben were happy to help. They took the letters and put them in the box. But then, they had an idea. "Let's mail ourselves!" Lily said. "Yeah, let's go on an adventure!" Ben agreed. They climbed into the box and closed the lid. They giggled and whispered to each other. "Where do you think we will go?" Lily asked. "Maybe to the moon, or the jungle, or the sea!" Ben said. They waited and waited, but nothing happened. They started to feel hot and bored. "Mom, we're ready to go!" Lily shouted. "Mom, open the box!" Ben yelled. But mom did not hear them. She had gone to the kitchen to make some tea. She did not know that Lily and Ben were in the box. Soon, dad came home from work. He saw the box and the letters on the table. "Looks like mom has some mail to send," he said. He picked up the box and carried it to the car. He did not know that Lily and Ben were in the box. Lily and Ben felt the box move. They were scared and confused. "Dad, stop!" Lily cried. "Dad, we're in here!" Ben screamed. But dad did not hear them. He had turned on the radio and was singing along. He did not know that Lily and Ben were in the box. He drove to the post office and parked the car. He got out and took the box to the counter. He did not know that Lily and Ben were in the box. "Hi, I have some mail to send," he said to the lady. "Sure, let me weigh it for you," the lady said. She put the box on the scale. She was shocked. The box was very heavy. "What's in here?" she asked. "Just some letters," dad said. The lady looked at him suspiciously. She shook the box gently. She heard some noises. "Help!" Lily and Ben shouted. The lady opened the box. She was shocked. She saw Lily and Ben in the box. "What are you doing here?" she asked. "We wanted to mail ourselves," Lily and Ben said. The lady looked at dad. He was shocked. He saw Lily and Ben in the box. "How did you get in there?" he asked. "We put ourselves in," Lily and Ben said. Dad was angry. He took Lily and Ben out of the box. He apologized to the lady. He took the letters and the box and went back to the car. He did not know what to say. Lily and Ben were sorry. They realized they had made a big mistake. They did not want to mail themselves anymore. They wanted to go home. "Mom, we're sorry!" Lily and Ben said. "Mom, we love you!" Lily and Ben said. Mom was shocked. She saw Lily and Ben in the car. She hugged them and kissed them. She was glad they were safe. "What happened?" she asked. "We'll tell you later," dad said. He drove them home. He explained what had happened. He was not happy. He said they had to be more careful and listen to mom. He said they had to share their toys and play nicely. He said they had to be fair. Lily and Ben nodded. They agreed. They learned their lesson. They did not want to mail themselves again. Africa's ancient historic period includes the rise of Egyptian civilization, the further development of societies outside the Nile River Valley and the interaction between them and civilizations outside of Africa. In the late 7th century North and East Africa were heavily influenced by the spread of Islam. That led to the appearance of new cultures such as those of the Swahili people, and the Mali Empire, whose king, Musa Keita I, became one of the richest and most influential people of the early 14th century. This also led to an increase in the slave trade that had a very bad influence for the development of the whole continent until the 19th century. And they can hear and see if we stay around here. In the 16th and 17th centuries (the 1500s and 1600s), doctors started to be allowed to dissect bodies (or cut them open after death) in order to figure out the cause of death. Around this time, there were many different ideas about what caused cancer. The German professor Wilhelm Fabry believed that breast cancer was caused by a clot of milk in the part of a woman's breast that produces milk. The Dutch professor Francois de la Boe Sylvius believed that all disease was caused by chemical processes. He thought that cancer, in particular, was caused by acidic lymph. Nicolaes Tulp, who lived at the same time as Sylvius, believed that cancer was a poison that slowly spreads and was contagious. And I'm Doug Johnson with the Special English program, PEOPLE IN AMERICA. Every week we tell about a person important in the history of the United States. Today, we tell about Babe Ruth, America's greatest baseball player. Some say he was the greatest sports hero of all time. George Herman Ruth was born in Baltimore, Maryland, in eighteen ninety-five. George's parents owned a bar where people came to drink alcohol. His mother died when he was very young. His father was killed in a street fight. Young George was forced to live on the streets of Baltimore. He stole things. He fought with other children. He got into trouble. At the age of eight, he was sent to live at Saint Mary's industrial school for boys. Catholic religious workers operated the school. The religious workers helped George to act better. And they taught him how to play baseball. By the age of eighteen, George was an excellent baseball player. In nineteen fourteen, a teacher at the school wrote to a friend of his, Jack Dunn. Dunn was the manager of the Baltimore Orioles minor league baseball team. He was the one who decided who would play for the team. The teacher invited Dunn to see the young player. Dunn watched George pitch the baseball. He offered the young left-handed pitcher a job playing baseball for six months. He said the Baltimore Orioles team would pay George six hundred dollars. Jack Dunn had to take responsibility for the boy or George could not leave the school. Dunn decided to become George's legal parent. Jack Dunn and his new player arrived at the Orioles' baseball park. The older Orioles' players joked about the new young player. They called him, "Dunn's babe. " The young baseball player became known forever as Babe Ruth. That year, the Boston Red Sox baseball team bought the right to make Babe Ruth a player for their team. Ruth pitched for the Red Sox teams during the next two years. He became the best pitcher in the American baseball league. Then the Red Sox discovered that he could hit the ball even better than he could throw it. So Ruth became an outfielder instead of a pitcher. In nineteen nineteen, he hit the ball out of the baseball park twenty-nine times. He hit more home runs than any other player that year. In nineteen twenty, the Red Sox sold Babe Ruth's contract to the famous New York Yankees baseball team. That year, Babe Ruth hit fifty-four home runs. This was more home runs than any other American League team hit that season. The next year, he hit fifty-nine home runs. Babe Ruth's baseball skill and friendly nature made him famous across the country and around the world. Many people came to the Yankee games just because they wanted to see Babe Ruth play. He helped the team earn a great deal of money. The Yankees built a new baseball stadium. Even today, Yankee stadium is known as "the house that Ruth built." Baseball fans loved Babe Ruth because he was what some people called "larger than life. " Sports writer Paul Gallico wrote that Babe Ruth played ball in the same intense way that he lived his life. Gallico said that whenever Ruth hit a ball out of the baseball park the fans would become so excited that they were ready to break the seats. It was impossible to watch Ruth swing his bat without experiencing a strong emotion. In fact, in nineteen twenty, a man reportedly died of excitement while watching Babe Ruth hit a home run. The name of Babe Ruth appeared so often IN THE NEWSpapers that sports writers thought up new names for him. They called him "The Sultan of Swat." "The King of Clout." "The Babe. " They called him "Bambino." Sometimes they shortened that name to "Bam." Babe Ruth led the New York Yankees to seven championships, including four World Series titles. He hit more home runs than any other baseball player. In nineteen twenty-seven, he hit sixty home runs. During his lifetime, he hit a total of seven hundred fourteen home runs. Before he became a power hitter, he had been among the best pitchers of his time. All these skills made Babe Ruth the greatest player baseball has ever had. In nineteen thirty, Ruth earned eighty thousand dollars. This was more money than the president of the United States, Herbert Hoover, earned that year. Reporters asked Ruth why he should be paid more than President Hoover. Ruth reportedly said: "Why not? I had a better year than he did." Ruth also earned money by permitting his name to be used on many products. A candy bar was named after him. "Baby Ruth" candy bars still are popular today. Sometimes, Babe Ruth got into trouble on the baseball field. He often arrived late. He got angry often. He hit a baseball umpire. He had many disputes with the chief baseball official. In nineteen twenty-one, the Yankees' manager suspended Ruth from playing. The next year, Ruth did the worst thing a baseball player could do. He left the field during a game to chase a fan who said something he did not like. He had to pay five thousand dollars for violating the rules. Babe Ruth also got into trouble off the baseball field. He was a very large man who liked to have a good time. He ate too much. He drank too much alcohol. He played cards and lost money. He went to nightclubs. He drove his car too fast. Some people were unhappy about the way he acted. In nineteen twenty-two, New York State Senator Jimmy Walker appealed to Babe Ruth at a dinner of the baseball writers association. Mr. Walker asked the great baseball star to be a better example to the children of America. Babe Ruth stood up with tears running down his face. He promised he would be a better person. He kept his promise. He was never in trouble again. Yet Babe Ruth continued to eat too much. In nineteen twenty-five, he was returning on a train from baseball spring training in the South. He became hungry. He stopped at a train station. He reportedly ate twelve hot dog sandwiches. He drank eight bottles of soft drink. Ruth developed severe stomach problems. He was taken to a hospital in New York. Babe Ruth was so sick that doctors had to operate on him. He was in the hospital for seven weeks. Many Americans worried about him until he got well. Babe Ruth loved children. In nineteen twenty-six, a child named Johnny Sylvester lay in a hospital bed. He was very weak after an operation. His doctor thought that a visit from Johnny's hero might help the boy get better. So Babe Ruth came to the hospital. He wrote his name on a baseball and gave it to Johnny. He promised to hit a home run that afternoon for the boy. Babe Ruth kept his promise. In fact, he hit three home runs that day. There are many stories about Babe Ruth and his life. Experts do not agree about which ones are true. The most famous story about him concerns the nineteen thirty-two World Series championship game. The Yankees were playing the Chicago Cubs in Chicago. Ruth was at bat getting ready to hit. The Cubs and their fans were trying to make Ruth angry. They insulted him. Ruth swung his bat and missed the first pitch. The crowd laughed at him. Ruth swung and missed the second pitch. The crowd made more noises. Then Ruth pointed his bat at the seats past the center field of the ball park. He showed the crowd where he would hit the next ball. And that was exactly where he hit the ball out of the park. Ruth stopped playing baseball in nineteen thirty-five. The next year he was one of the first five players to be elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame. After he retired as a player, he wanted to be manager of a baseball team. But no such position was offered to him. Ruth died in nineteen forty-eight of throat cancer. He was fifty-three years old. Babe Ruth is buried near New York City. People still come to visit his burial place. They leave things there: A Yankees baseball hat. A small American flag. A baseball. Americans leave these things to show that they have not forgotten the Babe. This Special English program was written by Shelley Gollust. It was produced by Lawan Davis. I'm Shirley Griffith. The capacity of a DVD-ROM can be visually determined by noting the number of data sides, and looking at the data side(s) of the disc. Double-layered sides are usually gold-colored, while single-layered sides are usually silver-colored, like a CD. One additional way to tell if a DVD contains one or two layers is to look at the center ring on the underside of the disc. If there are two barcodes, it is a dual layer disc. If there is one barcode, there is only one layer. Okay then you better go up there and fix that bed right now. In New York, a town is also a smaller part of the county, but it is less important than in New England. Towns provide almost all municipal services to places, called "hamlets", that are not in villages, and some services to places that are in "villages". In New York, a town usually has some hamlets and villages. But, because villages have power without towns (they are "independent") they can be in two towns or even two counties. Everyone in New York State who does not live in an Indian reservation (a special place for American Indians) or a city lives in a town, and perhaps in one of the town's hamlets or villages. A city may be part of a county, but even if it is surrounded by a town that has more people than the city, it is not part of the town. Dear Mom I made a turkey today in Cub Scouts period. The oval-shaped zero and circular letter O came into use together on modern character displays. The zero with a dot in the centre seems to have begun as a choice on IBM 3270 controllers (this has the problem that it looks like the Greek letter theta). The slashed zero, looking like the letter O with a diagonal line drawn inside it, is used in old-style ASCII graphic sets that came from the default typewheel on the well-known ASR-33 Teletype. This format causes problems because it looks like the symbol formula_1, representing the empty set, as well as for certain Scandinavian languages which use as a letter. A vacuum tube, also called a valve in British English, is an electronic device used in many older model radios, television sets, and amplifiers to control electric current flow. The cathode is heated, as in a light bulb, so it will emit electrons. This is called thermionic emission. The anode is the part that accepts the emitted electrons. The device may have other parts. Vacuum tubes must be hot to work. Most are made of glass, thus are fragile and can break. Vacuum tubes were used in the first computers like the ENIAC, which were large and need much work to continue operating. I do want to tell him those story XXX. yeah, she got stung by a bee. It takes 10,759 days, or about 29.8 years, for Saturn to orbit around the Sun. This is known as Saturn's "orbital period". Creating a Carpet Cleaning Routine Clean carpets can significantly improve the appearance and hygiene of your home or office. However, maintaining clean carpets requires consistent effort and attention. This comprehensive guide will walk you through creating an effective carpet cleaning routine that ensures your carpets remain fresh, stain-free, and looking like new. **Step 1: Vacuum Regularly** Vacuuming is arguably the most critical part of any carpet cleaning regimen. It removes dirt, dust mites, allergens, and other debris from deep within the fibers of your carpet. Aim to vacuum at least once or twice a week depending on foot traffic. For high-traffic areas, consider increasing this frequency. Key Tips: * Overlap your strokes to ensure thorough coverage. * Empty the vacuum bag when half full to maintain suction power. * Use appropriate attachments for corners, edges, and crevices. **Step 2: Spot Clean Immediately** Promptly addressing spills and stains prevents them from setting into the carpet permanently. Blot (don't rub) the area with a clean cloth damped with warm water. If necessary, add a small amount of mild dish soap. Afterward, blot again with another damp cloth until all residue is removed. Key Guidelines: * Always test any cleaning solution on an inconspicuous area first. * Avoid using harsh chemicals as they may damage the carpet or fade its color. * Never soak the carpet; excessive moisture can lead to mold growth. **Step 3: Deep Clean Annually** Even if you vacuum regularly and tackle spots right away, annual deep cleans are essential for removing embedded grime and bacteria. You have two options here - hiring professionals or renting equipment yourself. Both methods involve applying shampoo, agitating the carpet with a machine, then extracting the dirty liquid. If choosing DIY rental route: * Follow manufacturer instructions carefully. * Pre-treat heavily soiled areas before starting. * Allow ample time for drying to prevent mildew odor. When opting for professional services: * Check reviews and ratings. * Request proof of insurance. * Ask about their process and products used. **Step 4: Deodorize Periodically** Despite regular maintenance, carpets can still absorb unpleasant smells over time. To keep yours smelling fresh, sprinkle baking soda evenly across the surface, let sit for several hours (ideally overnight), then thoroughly vacuum up. Baking soda neutralizes odors naturally without leaving behind harmful residues. Additional Tips: * Consider combining deodorizing sessions with deep cleans for optimal results. * Don't forget to ventilate rooms during and after these treatments. **Step 5: Establish Rules & Habits** Finally, establish rules and habits to minimize future wear and tear. These could include no eating or drinking on certain parts of the carpet, promptly wiping shoes upon entry, or placing mats near doors to trap outdoor dirt. By doing so, you extend the lifespan of your carpets and reduce the intensity of your cleaning routines. In Conclusion: Creating a comprehensive carpet cleaning routine involves regular vacuuming, immediate spot treatment, annual deep cleans, periodic deodorization, and establishing smart household rules. While seemingly extensive, investing time and resources into such a schedule pays off by preserving the longevity and aesthetic appeal of your carpets. [runs and plops herself down on the floor in front of MOT] [Nina points to her teddy bear sitting on the back of a truck.] Title: How to Dance to Cotton-Eyed Joe Introduction: Cotton-Eyed Joe is a popular line dance that originated in the United States. It has become a staple at country music events and parties due to its high energy and fun nature. This tutorial will break down the steps involved in dancing to this lively tune so you too can join in on the fun! Step 1: Familiarize Yourself with the Song Before attempting any choreography, make sure you are familiar with the song. The tempo of Cotton-Eyed Joe typically ranges from 120 to 130 beats per minute (BPM). Listening to the song multiple times beforehand will help you understand where each move should fall within the rhythm. Step 2: Learn the Basic Step The basic step of Cotton-Eyed Joe consists of two parts - kick-ball-change and hop-skip. Here's how to do them: a) Kick-ball-change: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart. For the first beat, kick one foot forward while pointing the toes downward. Bring your knee up as if marching on the second beat. On the third beat, place the ball of your foot back down next to your stationary foot without putting full weight on it yet. Finally, shift your weight completely onto the newly placed foot during the fourth beat. Repeat this process starting with the opposite foot. b) Hop-Skip: Stand straight again after completing the kick-ball-changes. Jump slightly off both feet on the first count, then skip forward with your right foot while swinging your left arm forward on the second count. Skip backward with your left foot while swinging your right arm backward on the third count. Key Tip: Practice these basic steps until they feel natural and comfortable. Once mastered, transitioning into more complex movements becomes easier. Step 3: Add Turns Turns add complexity and excitement to your dance routine. To turn while doing the kick-ball-change, simply pivot on the ball of your supporting foot when placing it down. Keep turning until facing the direction you want to go. Remember to maintain balance throughout. Step 4: Master Advanced Moves Advanced moves include grapevines, jazz squares, and shuffles. These require coordination but significantly enhance the overall performance. There are numerous online resources available demonstrating these advanced techniques. Be patient with yourself as learning these takes time and consistent practice. Step 5: Follow Along With Others If possible, try following along with others who already know the dance. Watch their footwork closely and mimic their actions. Dancing alongside experienced dancers provides visual cues about timing and sequencing which aid in understanding the flow of the dance. Step 6: Put Everything Together Once you have learned all the individual components, start piecing them together according to the structure of the song. Usually, there's a repetitive pattern involving the basic step, turns, and occasionally an advanced move thrown in between. Don't worry about getting everything perfect immediately; just keep practicing and refining your skills over time. Conclusion: Remember, dancing is supposed to be enjoyable. While mastering Cotton-Eyed Joe might take some effort, don't lose sight of having fun amidst the hard work. As you continue practicing, you'll find yourself improving rapidly. Soon enough, you'll be able to confidently strut your stuff whenever Cotton-Eyed Joe starts playing! But that doesn't s necessarily mean it's bad. Title: How to Help a Horse with Choke Choke is a common aliment in horses that occurs when a horse has difficulty swallowing due to a blockage in its esophagus. This can be caused by various factors such as eating too quickly, consuming hard food or objects, or having dental issues. If you suspect your horse has choke, do not panic. Follow these steps carefully to help alleviate the condition and seek veterinary attention if necessary. **Step 1: Observe the Horse's Behavior** The first sign of choke in a horse is usually coughing, gagging, or excessive salivation. The horse may also extend its neck and make repeated attempts to swallow. These symptoms are often mistaken for colic; however, unlike colic, choked horses typically remain bright and alert. Carefully observe your horse's behavior and note any unusual actions or sounds. *Key Tip:* Always approach a distressed horse slowly and calmly to avoid causing further stress or injury. **Step 2: Examine the Horse's Mouth** Gently hold the horse's head and open its mouth using a speculum or gently pulling down the lower jaw. Look inside the mouth for any signs of obstruction in the throat or esophagus. You might see hay, feed, or other foreign material. *Guideline:* Never stick your hand into a horse's mouth without proper restraint or assistance, as this could result in serious bites. **Step 3: Remove Visible Obstructions** If you can see an obvious obstruction, try removing it with your fingers or blunt-ended tweezers. Be cautious not to push the blockage deeper into the esophagus. If you cannot reach the obstruction or if the horse continues showing signs of distress, stop trying to remove it yourself. Further intervention may cause more harm than good. **Step 4: Encourage the Horse to Drink Water** Offer the horse water to drink. Sometimes, the simple act of drinking can help dislodge the obstruction. However, if the horse refuses to drink or seems unable to swallow, refrain from forcing water, which could exacerbate the situation. **Step 5: Contact a Veterinarian** Even if you successfully removed the visible obstruction, contact a veterinarian immediately. Horses suffering from choke are at risk of developing complications like aspiration pneumonia, dehydration, or damage to the esophagus. A vet will perform a thorough examination and provide appropriate treatment, including fluid therapy, sedatives, or anti-inflammatory medication. *Key Tip:* Keep your veterinarian's phone number handy and readily available in case of emergencies. **Step 6: Provide Aftercare** After the veterinarian has treated the choke, monitor the horse closely for several days. Offer small amounts of soft feed, such as soaked hay cubes or mashed bran mash, until the horse returns to normal eating habits. Gradually reintroduce regular feed over time. *Guideline:* Prevent future episodes of choke by ensuring your horse eats slowly, has access to plenty of fresh water, and receives routine dental care. Additionally, consider feeding smaller meals throughout the day rather than large portions once or twice daily. Remember, helping a horse with choke requires patience, caution, and prompt medical attention. By following these steps and seeking professional advice, you can increase the likelihood of a successful recovery for your beloved equine companion. Jim Henson said the name "muppet" came from combining the words "puppet" and "marionette," but he also said that he just liked the way the word sounded. There is no defined difference between a muppet and a puppet other than the link to Jim Henson. Many of Jim Henson's muppets have large eyes, round noses, and large mouths that go from one end of the face to the other (almost ear to ear). Muppets are often made out of foam with felt on top. Puppeteers usually control the muppet with one hand in the head and the other hand controls the muppets arms. Sometimes the arms have a stick that is used to move the hand and arm - this is called the "arm rod." Puppeteers hold the arm rod at one end and the other end touches the muppet hand. Some muppets have hands that are gloves. Puppeteers put their hands in the gloves of these muppets so the muppet can move its fingers. For this reason, most muppets appear to favor their left hands. A good example is one of your best teaching tools. People will see that you are healthy, and they will want to know why. Cavities are made by the infection called tooth decay. If you have a black spot on your tooth, it might be a cavity. If that tooth hurts some of the lime, such as when you eat, drink, or breathe cold air, it probably has a cavity in it.<br><br> You will get cavities in your teeth if you eat sweet food and then do not clean your teeth. If you see a cavity starting in your mouth or feel a tooth hurting you, get help right away. If you do not Bll a cavity, it grows bigger and deeper. A dental worker knows how to Bll the cavity so you can keep that tooth. Do this before the pain gets worse. A tooth with an abscess needs treatment at once, before the infection can go into the bone (page 93). In most cases the tooth must be taken out. If it is not possible to do this right away, you can stop the problem from getting any worse if you follow these steps: Wash the inside of your mouth with warm 1.<br><br> water. Gums are infected if they are loose, sore, and red, and if they bleed when the teeth are cleaned. Infection in the gums is called gum disease. Gum disease, like tooth decay, happens when acid touches the teeth and gums. This acid is made when sweet and soft foods mix with germs (see page 50).<br><br> Infection from gum disease can spread into the root Bbers and bone (see page 42). But you can stop gum disease and prevent it from coming back. There are two things to do: clean your teeth better and strengthen your gums. Even if your gums are sore and they bleed, you must still clean the 1.<br><br> teeth beside them. If more food collects on the teeth, the gum infection will get even worse. Get a soft brush (see p. 4) and use it gently. This way you will not hurt the gums when you clean. To make your gums stronger and more able to Bght the infection: 2. Eat more fresh fruits and green leafy vegetables, and fewer soft " sticky foods from the store.<br><br> Rinse your mouth with warm salt water. Do this every day, even " after your gums feel better.
