It is a big book with a shiny cover. She opens it and sees many pictures. She sees a picture of a chain. A chain is a long thing with many round parts. She reads the words under the picture. The words say: "A chain can be used to pull, to lock, or to make noise." Sara wants to try the chain. She looks around her room. She sees her toy box. She sees a lock on her toy box. She thinks: "Maybe I can use the chain to open the lock." She takes the chain and puts it around the lock. She pulls the chain hard. The lock does not open. She pulls harder. The lock still does not open. She pulls harder and harder. The chain breaks. The lock falls to the floor. Sara is sad. She says: "Oh no, I broke the chain. Now I can't read the book anymore." Sara hears a noise. She turns around. She sees her mom. Her mom is smiling. She says: "Sara, what are you doing? I brought you a new book. It is a book about chains. I thought you would like it." Sara is surprised. She says: "Mom, this is the book I was reading. I found it in my room. I tried to use the chain to open the lock, but I broke the chain. I'm sorry, mom." Her mom hugs her. She says: "It's okay, Sara. The chain was old and weak. It was not a good chain. I have a new chain for you. It is a strong chain. You can use it to play, but not to open locks. And you can still read the book. The book is not broken. It is a good book. I love you, Sara." Sara smiles. She says: "I love you too, mom. Thank you for the book and the chain. Dark circles under the eyes can make you look tired, aged, or unhealthy. While getting enough sleep and staying hydrated are essential for reducing dark circles, concealer is an effective way to cover them up instantly. However, applying concealer the right way can be tricky. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process step-by-step, ensuring that you achieve natural-looking coverage. **Step 1: Prep Your Skin** Before applying any makeup, cleanse your face and apply a moisturizer suitable for your skin type. Wait a few minutes for the moisturizer to absorb completely. Properly prepped skin ensures a smooth canvas for concealer application and enhances its longevity. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Choose a gentle, fragrance-free cleanser and a lightweight, non-greasy moisturizer. * If you have oily skin, opt for an oil-free moisturizer; if you have dry skin, choose a richer formula. **Step 2: Color Correct** Color correction is crucial when covering dark circles because they often have purple, blue, or green undertones. A color corrector neutralizes these tones before concealer application. For dark circles, select a peach or salmon-colored corrector. Apply it sparingly using a small synthetic brush, focusing on the inner corner of the eye, extending toward the outer corner along the orbital bone. Blend well using a beauty blender or a clean finger. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Opt for a cream or liquid corrector instead of a powder formulation, as powders tend to settle into fine lines and wrinkles. * Test different shades of correctors to find one that matches your undertone best. Fair skin usually benefits from lighter shades, while medium to deep complexions require deeper colors. **Step 3: Choose the Right Concealer** Selecting the proper concealer shade is vital for achieving natural results. Ideally, your concealer should be one to two shades lighter than your foundation. Opt for a liquid or cream formula rather than a stick concealer, which tends to crease more easily. Look for a buildable, medium-coverage product with a slightly dewy finish to avoid emphasizing fine lines. Key Tips & Guidelines: Why don't we try to use the tools anyway? All animals are consumers: they survive by eating other organic material. Almost all animals have neurons, a signalling system. They usually have muscles, which make the body move. Many animals have a head and legs. Most animals are either male or female. They need a mate of the opposite sex to make offspring. Sex cells from the male and female can meet inside or outside the body. Does he know this one, I think maybe he just didn't notice it? Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun with an average distance to the Sun of 2.28 108 km or 1.52 AU. It is a superior planet in that its orbit is larger than the Earths. It has two satellites, Phobos and Deimos. Due to its larger orbit there are times when Mars is very close to Earth and the angular diameter can be as large as 25 arc seconds. The best viewing of Mars occurs when it is at opposition (when the Sun and Mars are at opposite sides of the sky) and when it is near perehelion of its elliptical orbit. At this time the Earth-Mars distance is only 0.37 AU. This happens about once every 15 years. Mars makes one rotation on its axis every 24 hours and 37 minutes, much like Earth. As well, it's rotation axis is tilted at 25 degrees (very similar to Earth with 23.5 degrees tilt), so that Mars experiences seasons, just like Earth. It has temperature extremes of 20 C and -140 C. The orbital period of Mars is 687 Earth days or 1.88 Earth years. Hence the Martian seasons last nearly twice as long as those on Earth. Mars has a diameter of 6794 km or 0.53 Earth diameter and has a mass of 10% that of Earths. The crust of Mars is a single thick (varying between 40 - 70 km thick) tectonic plate. The southern hemisphere is predominantly ancient cratered highlands. The majority of the northern hemisphere has plains which are much younger and lower in elevation. An abrupt elevation change of several kilometers seems to occur at the boundary. Whilst there is no tectonic plate motion on Mars, surface features suggest motion in the mantle. Valles Marineris, a rift valley some 4000 km long and 600 km wide, exists as well as the Tharsis rise, a dome-shaped bulge 5 - 6 km above the average elevation. The Martian surface shows shield volcanoes - the largest of which, Olympus Mons, is 24 km above the surrounding plains. Since there is no tectonic activity, these volcanoes form via hot-spot volcanism where magma rises through a hot spot in the mantle. Lava flows on Mars have impact craters which suggests that the flows are very old, and that the volcanoes are not active. Mars has a thin, cold atmosphere compared to Earth and Venus. The surface pressure, 0.006 atmosphere, is the same as that at an altitude of 35 km on Earth. It is dominated by CO2 (95%), with 2.7% being nitrogen. Water vapour makes up about 0.03% and this can form clouds of ice crystals. Liquid water cant exist in the atmosphere or on the surface because the average temperature (-23 C) combined with extremely low pressure allows water to only exist as a solid or a gas. CO2 also forms crystals at very high altitudes and this form of CO2 also exists on the surface at the poles during their winters. Water in ice form also exists mixed with the frozen CO2 at the poles. Fine dust exists on Mars which is easily carried into the atmosphere and during large, high wind storms, can obscure the whole planet for many months. Not only is the dust spread by such global storms but also by more local phenomena. Warm air rising during Martian afternoons allows whirlwinds, or dust devils to form. He likes to put stuff in his mouth. Title: How to Heal a Blister Blisters are small pockets of fluid that form under the skin due to friction or burns. They can be painful and uncomfortable, but with proper care and attention, they will eventually heal on their own. This article will provide you with an in-depth guide on how to heal a blister while minimizing discomfort and preventing infection. **Step 1: Leave it Alone (if Possible)** The best way for a blister to heal is by leaving it alone as much as possible. The top layer of the blister acts as a natural protective barrier against bacteria and infection. Avoid popping or breaking the blister unless absolutely necessary. If the blister is large, causing significant pain, or located in an area where continued friction is unavoidable, proceed to Step 2. **Step 2: Cleanse the Area Thoroughly** Before attempting to drain the blister, cleanse the affected area thoroughly using mild soap and warm water. Pat dry gently; do not rub. Apply a thin coat of antibiotic ointment over the entire surface of the blister to prevent infection. Key tip: Make sure your hands are completely clean before touching the blister to avoid introducing any germs. **Step 3: Sterilize a Needle** Using a needle sterilized with rubbing alcohol or held over a flame until red hot, carefully pierce the edge of the blister. Allow several holes around the perimeter so the fluid can escape slowly. Press gently on the sides of the blister to help release all the liquid inside. Once drained, apply more antibiotic ointment. **Step 4: Cover the Drained Blister** Cover the drained blister loosely with a non-stick bandage or gauze pad. Secure it with medical tape, ensuring it's not too tight to restrict blood flow. Change the dressing daily or whenever it becomes wet or dirty. Keeping the wound covered helps protect it from dirt and bacteria, promoting faster healing. **Step 5: Protect the Affected Area** Avoid further irritation to the healed blister site. Reduce friction by wearing comfortable shoes and socks made of breathable materials. For hand blisters, use gloves when engaging in activities likely to cause friction. **Step 6: Monitor for Signs of Infection** Watch for signs of infection such as increased pain, redness, warmth, pus formation, swelling, or fever. Seek immediate medical attention if these symptoms occur. Untreated infected blisters may lead to serious complications like cellulitis or sepsis. **Step 7: Stay Hydrated and Eat Nutritious Foods** Maintaining good overall health supports quicker recovery time. Consume plenty of fluids and eat nutrient-rich foods containing vitamins C, A, and B complex which boost immunity and aid tissue repair. **Step 8: Rest and Relax** Rest allows the body to focus its energy on healing. Limit strenuous activity involving the affected limb until the blister has fully healed. Remember, patience is essential when dealing with blisters. While they might take some time to disappear entirely, following these steps should ensure a speedier recovery process without compromising your health. Extracting files from an archive online is a useful skill that can save you time and hassle when dealing with compressed files. This tutorial will guide you through the process using various online tools, ensuring that you have a clear understanding of each step along the way. **Step 1: Identify the Archive Format** Before extracting files, determine the format of your archive. Common formats include ZIP, RAR, 7z, and TAR. Gaining this knowledge allows you to choose the appropriate online extraction tool and ensures compatibility. **Key Tips:** * Different archiving programs may create slightly different file types (e.g., WinZip creates ZIP files while 7-zip creates 7z files). However, most compression algorithms are compatible across platforms. * If unsure about the format, try opening the archive with popular decompression software like WinRAR or 7-zip; they often display the format during the opening procedure. Alternatively, look at the filename extension - common ones being .zip, .rar, .7z, or .tar.gz. **Step 2: Choose an Online File Extraction Tool** Select a reliable and secure online tool based on your identified archive type. Some popular options include ezyZip, Extract.me, and Online Unarchiver. These websites allow users to upload their archives and extract them without needing any special software installed on their local devices. **Key Guidelines:** * Be cautious when choosing an online service as some might contain malware or steal personal information. Stick to reputable sites listed here or recommended by trusted sources. * Avoid services requiring sensitive data such as email addresses unless absolutely necessary. Look for anonymous alternatives if privacy is essential. **Step 3: Upload Your Archive** On the chosen website, click 'Upload' or its equivalent button and select your archive file from your device storage. Wait for the file to finish uploading before proceeding. **Key Tips:** * Keep track of your upload progress bar. Larger files take longer to transfer over the internet. * Make sure you have permission to share this file publicly since it's going through third-party servers. **Step 4: Initiate the Extraction Process** Once your archive has been successfully uploaded, initiate the extraction process according to the specific site's interface. Generally, this involves clicking a 'Extract,' 'Unpack,' or similar button. **Key Guidelines:** * Double-check whether the extracted folder will be saved directly onto your computer or downloaded manually after completion. Adjust settings accordingly. * Remember where you plan to store the unpacked files so you can access them quickly later. **Step 5: Download the Extracted Folder** After successful extraction, download the new folder containing all uncompressed files. Save these documents to your preferred location on your computer. **Key Tips:** * Use descriptive names for folders and files during the saving process to avoid confusion. * Check the size of the extracted folder against the original archive to ensure everything was correctly transferred. **Step 6: Verify the Contents** Finally, verify that all files were properly extracted by checking inside the newly created directory. Open individual files to confirm functionality and integrity. **Key Points:** * Pay attention to error messages or warnings displayed throughout the process. Address issues promptly to prevent potential data loss. * Consider backing up important archives locally instead of relying solely on cloud-based solutions. The nothing will go on that star. Title: How to Add a Business to Yelp Introduction: Yelp is an online platform that connects people with local businesses. It's a great tool for business owners to reach potential customers and increase their visibility. This tutorial will guide you through the process of adding your business to Yelp, providing detailed explanations for each step along with key tips and guidelines. Step 1: Visit Biz.Yelp.com Go to <https://biz.yelp.com/> using your preferred web browser. This is the official page for business owners to manage their presence on Yelp. Step 2: Click on "Sign Up" or "Log In" If you don't have a Yelp account yet, click on "Sign Up". Fill out the required fields (first name, last name, email address, and password) and then click on "Sign up". If you already have a Yelp account, simply log in using your credentials. Step 3: Search for Your Business After logging in, you'll be directed to a page where you can search for your business. Enter your business's name and location, then click on "Search". If your business appears in the list, claim it by clicking on "Claim this Business". If it doesn't appear, proceed to Step 4. Step 4: Add Your Business Manually If your business isn't listed, click on "Add your business to Yelp". You'll need to provide some basic information about your business such as its name, address, category, phone number, and website (if any). Make sure all the details are accurate and consistent with other online platforms to avoid confusion. Step 5: Verify Your Email Address Once you've submitted the necessary info, Yelp will send a verification link to the email address provided. Check your inbox and click on the link to verify your email. This confirms that you're associated with the business. Step 6: Complete Your Business Profile Now that your business is live on Yelp, complete your profile by adding more details like hours of operation, types of payments accepted, parking options, etc. The more comprehensive your profile, the better chances of attracting customers. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Use high-quality photos: Photos play a crucial role in attracting customers. Make sure they're clear, well-lit, and representative of what your business offers. - Encourage customer reviews: Positive reviews can significantly boost your business's reputation. Politely ask satisfied customers to leave a review on Yelp. However, never offer incentives for positive reviews as this violates Yelp's policies. - Respond to reviews: Whether good or bad, respond to every review professionally and respectfully. This shows that you value customer feedback and care about your clients' experiences. - Monitor your listing regularly: Regularly update your business information as needed. Also, keep an eye on new reviews and engage with your audience. Conclusion: By following these steps and keeping our key tips in mind, you should now have successfully added your business to Yelp! Remember, maintaining an active presence on Yelp can help drive traffic to your physical store and improve your overall digital footprint. that's one of his favorite books. oh, no we want to play with stuff. Title: How to Get Your Mum to Buy You a New Phone Introduction: Persuading your mum to buy you a new phone can be challenging, but with careful planning and consideration, you can increase your chances of success. This guide will walk you through the steps needed to convince your mum that purchasing a new phone is both necessary and beneficial. Remember, this process requires patience, respect, and understanding towards your mother's perspective and concerns. Step 1: Identify Your Needs Understand why you want or need a new phone. Is your current device damaged? Are its features outdated, making everyday tasks difficult? Clearly defining your reasons will help build a strong case when discussing the issue with your mum. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Be honest about your needs; don't exaggerate issues or create false problems. - Consider whether repairing your existing phone could be an alternative solution. Step 2: Research Potential Options Familiarize yourself with different types of phones within your budget range. Compare their specifications, prices, brands, and reviews. Select three potential options to present to your mum later. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Choose models from reputable manufacturers known for quality products. - Focus on devices that offer good value for money rather than just high-end flagships. - Take note of any unique selling points (e.g., better cameras, longer battery life) which may appeal to your mum. Step 3: Prepare a Convincing Argument Organize your thoughts into a clear and concise argument detailing why you require a new phone. Use factual information gathered during research while addressing your current struggles with your old device. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Keep emotions under control; remain calm and rational throughout the discussion. - Highlight benefits such as improved productivity, easier communication, or enhanced entertainment experiences. - Address potential objections like cost by explaining financing options, trade-in programs, or suggesting shared payment responsibility. Step 4: Initiate The Discussion Choose an appropriate time and place to approach your mum with your request. Make sure she has enough mental space and energy to engage in a serious conversation without feeling rushed or distracted. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Start off by expressing gratitude for her support thus far. - Present your well-researched arguments and address any questions or concerns raised. - Listen carefully to her feedback and respond thoughtfully instead of becoming defensive. Step 5: Negotiate Terms If your mum agrees to consider buying you a new phone, discuss important aspects like budget, timeline, and expectations. Reach a mutually agreeable arrangement based on compromise and understanding. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Respect her financial limitations and collaboratively find solutions (e.g., saving allowance, part-time job). - Propose a reasonable repayment plan if applicable, showing maturity and commitment. - Agree on conditions such as responsible usage, regular maintenance checks, and sharing positive outcomes resulting from the new device. Conclusion: Following these steps increases the likelihood of successfully persuading your mum to purchase a new phone for you. However, remember that ultimately, the decision lies with her, so accept her response graciously regardless of the final outcome. By demonstrating maturity, respect, and appreciation throughout this process, you show your mum that you are growing more responsible and deserving of trust. By Jacque Mills Rationale: The first step in learning to read is letter recognition. Most children learn all or most of the letters of the alphabet by rote, but many enter kindergarten or first grade still unable to recognize them in print. This lesson will help children recognize and write the letter m by learning a letter symbol and associating it with the humps in Sally's Camel. They will also learn the phoneme m=/m/ by listening for and saying /m/ in words. Materials: Paper with a camel outline drawing minus the humps and lined like primary writing paper on the bottom half (one copy for each child), overhead copy of the same, pencils, set of m cards for the class, Good Morning, Muffin Mouse, by Lawrence DiFiori. 1. Introduce the lesson by explaining that we can not only sing and say the letters in the alphabet but we can also see and write them! Today we will learn about the letter m. First I'll show you what m looks like. (Teacher makes the letter m on the blackboard.) We're also going to learn what m sounds like. Then we'll learn to write the letter m. 2. Tell students: Pretend you just finished eating a bowl of your favorite ice cream. Rub your tummy and say "mmmmm, that was good." Now just say "mmmmmm." Can you guess what alphabet letter says "mmmmm"? That's right! "M"! Say "M". Now say "emmmmmm." 3. Now we'll do a cheer for the letter m. First I'll show you the cheer, and then you'll do it with me. This is M. M says /m/. Marshmallow, marshmallow /m/ /m/ /m/. Let's do it three times. (Everybody does the cheer three times.) What does M say? Mmmmmm. Good work! 4. Who can tell me what our mouths are doing when we say /m/? Call on students. What are our lips doing? That's right, our lips are touching each other. Teacher models mmmm. 5. Now I will say two words and you tell me which word has the sound /m/. You might hear it at the beginning of a word or in the middle of a word or at the end of a word so listen closely. Call on students to answer. Do you hear /m/ in mouse or house? Juice or milk? Bacon or ham? Camel or horse? 6. ( Hand out camel pictures.) Get a pencil out of your desk. Now we will learn to write the letter m. Look at the picture of Sally's camel. (Show on overhead.) What's missing in the picture? That's right! The camel is missing its humps! When we write the letter m, we make two humps, just like Sally's camel! Watch me write the letter m where the camel's humps should be. Teacher models. Now you do it! Good work! You've written the letter m. Now look at the bottom of the page at the part with the lines. That's where we'll practice writing the letter m. Watch me first. Start at the fence line. Draw straight down to the sidewalk. Come straight back up to the fence line and make one hump, two humps, just like Sally's camel. I'll come and look at your m and after I've looked at yours I want you to make five more the same way. Remember, m has how many humps? Two humps and what does m say? M says mmmm. 7. Hand out m cards. Read Good Morning, Muffin Mouse to the class two times. On the second reading, have students listen for the sound /m/ and have them raise their m cards when they hear /m/. References: Punch in the Stomach, A Model Lesson by Dr. Bruce Murray, Auburn University, www.auburn.edu/~murraba Alphabet Cheers, observed in the classroom of kindergarten teacher Alice Smith, Navarro Elementary, Bryan, TX., Fall, 1996 Click here to return to Insights Here throw them both at the same time. This thing is innocent as baby of explosion. It is cordite not yet finish. Creating an idea may seem like a daunting task, but with the right mindset and approach, anyone can do it. This guide will take you through the process of creating an idea, from start to finish. Step 1: Define Your Goal or Problem Before you can create an idea, you need to know what you're trying to accomplish or what problem you're trying to solve. Take some time to clearly define your goal or problem. Be specific and concrete. For example, instead of saying "I want to come up with a new product," say "I want to create a new type of reusable water bottle that is more durable and sustainable than current options." Key tip: Make sure your goal or problem is something you are passionate about. It will be much easier to come up with ideas if you care deeply about the subject matter. Step 2: Research Once you have defined your goal or problem, it's time to do some research. Look into existing solutions, trends, and any relevant data or statistics. The more information you gather, the better equipped you will be to generate creative and informed ideas. Key guideline: Don't limit yourself to just one source of information. Seek out diverse perspectives and sources to get a well-rounded understanding of the topic. Step 3: Brainstorm Now it's time to start generating ideas. Set aside some dedicated time and space to brainstorm. Turn off distractions, grab a pen and paper (or a whiteboard), and let your creativity flow. Don't judge or censor any ideas at this stage - just write down everything that comes to mind. Key tip: Try using different brainstorming techniques to keep things fresh. Some examples include mind mapping, word association, and SCAMPER (Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to another use, Eliminate, Reverse). Step 4: Evaluate and Refine After your brainstorming session, review all of the ideas you generated. Identify which ones align best with your goal or problem, and which ones show the most promise. Then, refine those ideas by adding details, considering potential challenges, and making improvements. Key guideline: Use objective criteria to evaluate your ideas. Ask questions such as "Does this solution address the problem?" "Is it feasible?" "What are the potential benefits and drawbacks?" Step 5: Test and Iterate Finally, test your top idea(s) to see how they hold up in real-world scenarios. Gather feedback from others, make adjustments as needed, and continue iterating until you have a solid solution. Key tip: Remember that creating an idea is often an iterative process. Don't be afraid to go back to previous steps and make changes as you learn more and gain new insights. By following these steps and incorporating the key tips and guidelines provided, you'll be well on your way to creating impactful and innovative ideas. Happy ideating! Today, we're going to explore a fun math problem involving powers and modulus (also known as remainders), similar to what mathematicians work on. Don't worry; it won't involve complex college-level concepts like electromagnetism or integration. Instead, let's dive into an interesting puzzle using simple ideas you already know! Imagine you have two numbers, called 'x' and 'y'. Our goal is to find a special number 't', so that when we raise 'x' to the power of 't' and take the remainder when dividing by 13, we get the same result as raising 'y' to the power of 't' and taking the remainder when dividing by 13. In mathematical notation, we want to solve these equations simultaneously: * y = x^t (mod 13) * x = y^t (mod 13) Let me give you some easy insights before diving deeper: 1. One obvious answer is 't' equals 1 because no matter which numbers 'x' and 'y' are, raising them to the power of 1 doesn't change their value. 2. Another simple observation is when both 'x' and 'y' are multiples of 13, then ANY choice of 't' would make our equations true since anything divided by 13 has zero remainder. Now, here comes the trickier part. What happens if 'x' and 'y' leave the SAME remainder when divided by 13? Well, one pattern emerges: choose 't' as 12 times any whole number plus 1, meaning t = 12k + 1 (where k represents any non-negative integer). This works due to something called "Fermat's Little Theorem," but don't worry about understanding that now-the essential idea is that choosing 't' based on this rule makes the equations hold true. Finally, what occurs if 'x' and 'y' leave DIFFERENT remainders when divided by 13? It turns out finding a solution gets more complicated. However, we still have a clue: focus on looking for values of 't' within a specific group containing numbers like 5, 7, 11, etc., namely those whose squares produce a remainder of 1 upon division by 12. Unfortunately, proving whether there exists an actual solution in this scenario requires more advanced knowledge beyond elementary school curriculum. Nonetheless, isn't it fascinating to ponder possibilities even without having every single answer yet? Keep exploring, questioning, and growing your mathematical mindset! Downloading Fruit Ninja on an iOS Device Fruit Ninja is a popular and addictive game that can be played on various devices, including those running on iOS operating system. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to download Fruit Ninja on your iPhone or iPad. By following these steps, you'll soon be slicing fruit like a true ninja! Step 1: Unlock Your Device Ensure your device is unlocked before starting this process. If your screen is locked with a passcode or Touch ID, you won't be able to access the App Store and download any apps until it's unlocked. Step 2: Open the App Store Look for the App Store icon which resembles a capital 'A' within a blue square. Tap on it to open the application where all applications, games, music, movies, etc., are stored. It comes pre-installed when purchasing an Apple product. Step 3: Navigate to the Search Tab At the bottom part of the screen, there are several tabs such as Today, Games, Apps, Updates, and Search. Click on the 'Search' tab (the one represented by a magnifying glass) located at the far right side of the toolbar. Step 4: Type "Fruit Ninja" in the Search Bar Once you tap on the search bar, a virtual keyboard should appear. Using this keyboard, type "Fruit Ninja". As you start typing, related searches may show up below; however, continue spelling out "Fruit Ninja" fully for accurate results. Step 5: Select the Official Game from Results From the list of suggested applications shown after searching, look for the official version of Fruit Ninja published by Halfbrick Studios. The correct app has a distinct red background with white ninja stars surrounding the title. Make sure other similar looking apps aren't mistakenly selected. Step 6: Installation Process Initiation After selecting the correct app, click the 'Get' button beside the title. A popup window might ask for authentication via Face ID, Touch ID, or password depending upon your settings. After successful verification, the 'Get' button changes into a circular progress wheel indicating download initiation. Key Tip: While downloading, do not close the App Store or turn off your phone unless necessary. Interrupting the download could cause issues installing the complete application. Step 7: Wait for Completion Depending on your internet speed, the size of the app, and server traffic, the installation time may vary. Larger files take longer periods especially over slower connections. Keep patient until the progress circle fills completely. Once filled, the word 'Installed' replaces the circle along with an 'Open' button appearing next to it. Guideline: During installation, avoid using heavy data consuming tasks on your device. Streaming videos, uploading large files, or playing online games can slow down the process significantly. Step 8: Launch the Installed Application Press the 'Open' button to launch Fruit Ninja immediately post-installation. Alternatively, locate its icon on one of your home screens and press it. Enjoy hours of fun slashing fruits while avoiding bombs! Remember, always ensure you have enough storage space available prior to downloading new apps. Regularly updating installed apps also ensures optimal performance and security benefits. Happy gaming! Are we going to have some oatmeal with me? Title: How to Comment in Google Documents Google Docs is a powerful tool that allows users to collaborate on documents in real-time. One essential feature of Google Docs is its commenting system, which enables users to leave feedback, ask questions, or suggest changes without altering the original text. This tutorial will provide an in-depth guide on how to effectively utilize comments within Google Docs. **Step 1:** Open your document To begin, open your Google Drive account and select the desired document you wish to add comments to. If you don't have any existing documents, create a new one by clicking "+ New" > "Google Docs." **Key tip**: Make sure all collaborators are granted editing access so they can view and respond to your comments. **Step 2:** Navigate to the location where you want to insert a comment Scroll through your document until you reach the specific section where you would like to place a comment. It could be a sentence, paragraph, table, image, etc. **Step 3:** Highlight the relevant content (optional) While highlighting isn't mandatory, doing so may help draw attention to the specific part of the document related to your comment. To highlight, click and drag over the text or object. Then choose a color from the highlighted text options at the top of the screen. **Step 4:** Insert a comment Click anywhere within the highlighted area or right next to the line of text that requires a comment. Next, click on the "Insert comment" button located in the upper-right corner of the page, represented by a speech bubble icon. Alternatively, press 'Ctrl + Alt + M' (Windows) or 'Cmd + Option + M' (Mac). **Step 5:** Type your comment A small box with a blinking cursor should appear adjacent to the portion of the document associated with your comment. Begin typing your message into this field. Keep it concise yet informative; elaborate enough to convey your point but brief enough to maintain readability. **Guideline**: Address specific individuals when necessary using '@mentions.' For example, if you need clarification from John regarding his input, type "@John," and he'll receive a notification about your request. **Step 6:** Choose resolution settings (optional) Before posting your comment, consider setting a resolution status. You can do this by clicking on the three vertical dots in the top-right corner of the comment window and selecting either "Resolve" or "Request resolution." These options allow you to mark completed tasks or seek closure on outstanding issues. **Step 7:** Post your comment Once satisfied with your wording and resolution settings, click the "Comment" button (or hit Enter), which will save and post your remark directly onto the document. The person mentioned (@mentioned) in the comment will immediately receive a notification via email. **Key tip**: Encourage active participation among team members by replying to their comments promptly and resolving them once addressed. **Step 8:** Review and manage comments As more people engage with your document, additional comments might accumulate throughout. Access these easily by clicking on the number badge displayed near the comment icon ('Ctrl + Shift + C') in the upper-right corner of the screen. Here, you can filter comments based on unresolved ones, mentioning you, or sorted chronologically. Additionally, hovering over a comment provides management tools such as edit, delete, resolve, or reply. By mastering the art of commenting within Google Docs, collaboration becomes smoother, efficient, and organized - ultimately fostering productive workflows amongst teams. Happy commenting! Due: Thursday 23 September. The reading for this assignment is Chapters 7-8 of How to think.... - Download filters.py by typing: Read the file and get an idea of what it does. You should be able to get a sense of how it works even though it uses some features we haven't worked with yet. The function is_palindrome is an example of a filter. It checks words and indicates whether or not they are palindromes. Or, at least, it is supposed to. At the moment it indicates whether or not a word is equal to the string 'palindrome', which is probably not what we had in mind. If you run the program as is, it will run through a list of words (in /usr/share/dict/words) and print palindrome. Your job is to change is_palindrome so that it does what it is supposed to do. A palindrome, since you asked, is a word that reads the same forward and backward, like `reviver' and `noon.' How many palindromes are there in our little dictionary? Hint: use a pipe. - Write a filter function named is_word that always returns True. Now you can run filters.py again with the new python filters.py is_word | wc So now we know how many words there are in the dictionary. - Read the handout regarding lipograms (attached), and then write a filter called has_no_e that returns True if the given word doesn't have the letter `e' in it. What percentage of the words in the dictionary have no `e'? - Write a function named avoids that takes a word and a string of forbidden letters, and that returns true if the word doesn't use any of the forbidden letters. You can call your function from the command line like this: python filters.py avoids xyz | wc Can you find a combination of 5 forbidden letters that excludes the smallest fraction of the dictionary? - Write a function that takes a word and a list of required letters, and that returns True if the word uses all the required letters at least once. How many words are there that use all the vowels aeiou? How about aeiouy? Hint: You can convert a string to a list using the built-in function list. - Write a function that takes a word and a list of letters, and that returns True if the word contains only letters in the list. How many words can be formed using only the letters acefhlo? Other than `hoe alfalfa'. - Write a function called is_abecedarian that returns True if the letters in a word appear in alphabetical order. How many abecedarian words are there? - Download Palindrome.py from the class web page. Read through the program and then run it. If the version of is_palindrome in your filters.py is correct, then Palindrome.py should provide a working graphical palindrome-checker. - In filters.py, write a function called rotate_word that takes a string and an integer as parameters, and that returns a new string that contains the letters from the original string `rotated' by the given amount. To rotate a letter means to shift it through the alphabet, wrapping around to the beginning if necessary. For example, Y shifted by 1 is Z, and Z shifted by 1 is A. A shifted by 5 is F. For you hard-drinking electrical engineers, what do you get if you rotate 'ohms' by 1? For you entrepreneurial bakers, what do you get if you rotate 'hawk' by 8? And for you environmentalists, what's 'terra' rotated by 13? Hint: check out the ord and chr functions documented A good solution to this problem should be appropriately general and encapsulated. It should work for upper- and lower-case letters, and for positive and negative shifts. - As a JFFE, how many rotate-pairs can you find in the - As a second JFFE, go back to Palindrome.py and make a copy of check_palindrome called rotate_entry. Instead of checking for palindromes, it should take the contents of the text entry, rotate it by 1, and display the result in the label. - As a third JFFE, add a second text entry to the GUI, and modify rotate_entry so that it gets the amount of the shift from the new entry. Betsy agreed and they went to grab some snacks. They enjoyed their snacks and the rest of the day together. Hello! Today, let's talk about a concept called the "p-value." You might have heard your parents or teachers mention it before when discussing science experiments or research studies. The p-value helps us understand whether something we observe in a study is really important or just happened by chance. Imagine that our friend Helen owns a chocolate factory. She makes delicious chocolate bars with lots of yummy peanuts inside them. She promises her customers that each bar has at least 70 grams of peanuts. But one day, some customers complain that their chocolate bars don't seem to have enough peanuts. Now, Helen wants to know if these complaints are because her machines are making bars with less than 70 grams of peanuts, or if people are just mistaken. To do this, Helen sets up a little experiment. She takes many chocolate bars off the assembly line and weighs the amount of peanuts in each one. After all the calculations, she finds that on average, the chocolate bars contain 68.7 grams of peanuts. That's pretty close to 70 grams, but could it be possible that her machine is producing bars with too few peanuts? This is where the p-value comes into play. The p-value will tell Helen how likely it would be to see results like hers (the average weight being 68.7 grams) if her machine were actually putting exactly 70 grams of peanuts in every single bar - meaning if everything was working perfectly according to her promise. In other words, the p-value shows the probability of getting a result as extreme as (or even more extreme than!) the one she got, assuming the Null Hypothesis is correct. The Null Hypothesis simply states that nothing unusual is happening; in Helen's case, it means that her machine puts exactly 70 grams of peanuts per bar. In Helen's situation, the p-value turned out to be 18%. That's quite high compared to the commonly chosen significance level of 5%. A significance level is a threshold set by researchers like Helen to decide if their observations are meaningful or due to randomness. Since the p-value is greater than the significance level, Helen cannot say with confidence that her machine is causing the low peanut weights. Instead, she should keep investigating or accept that sometimes, small differences happen randomly without any particular cause. So remember, the p-value is a way to check if a surprising observation is truly significant or just occurred by chance! I'll just have you put this on. So um but normally he really likes those. Title: How to Make Black Hair Grow Faster Introduction: Hair growth can be influenced by various factors such as genetics, diet, health conditions, and hair care practices. While there is no quick fix to make your hair grow faster overnight, you can adopt certain habits that promote healthy hair growth over time. This tutorial focuses on black hair specifically, but many of these tips may also apply to other types of hair. Please note that results will vary from person to person. **Step 1: Maintain a Balanced Diet** Key Tip: Eat foods rich in protein, vitamins A, C, D, and E, minerals like zinc and iron, and omega-3 fatty acids. These nutrients are essential for hair growth. Explanation: Your diet plays a significant role in promoting hair growth. Consuming a balanced diet filled with necessary nutrients ensures that your body has all it needs to support strong and healthy hair follicles. Foods high in protein (meats, eggs), vitamin A (carrots, sweet potatoes), vitamin C (citrus fruits, strawberries), vitamin D (salmon, mushrooms), vitamin E (almonds, spinach), zinc (oysters, beef), iron (spinach, lentils) and omega-3 fatty acids (flaxseeds, walnuts) should form part of your daily intake. --- **Step 2: Stay Hydrated** Key Guideline: Drink at least eight glasses of water per day. Explanation: Proper hydration supports overall health, including hair growth. Water carries essential nutrients to your scalp and hair roots, keeping them nourished and moisturized, which contributes to faster hair growth. --- **Step 3: Regular Scalp Massages** Key Tip: Use natural oils during massages for added benefits. Explanation: Regularly massaging your scalp increases blood flow to the area, delivering more oxygen and nutrients to stimulate hair growth. Adding natural oils such as coconut, olive, or jojoba oil enhances this process by providing additional moisture and nutrients directly to the scalp. --- **Step 4: Avoid Heat Styling Tools** Key Guideline: Limit usage of blow dryers, flat irons, and curlers. Explanation: Excessive heat styling damages the hair cuticle leading to breakage and split ends, hindering length retention. Air drying and embracing natural textures whenever possible reduces damage and promotes healthier hair growth. --- **Step 5: Minimize Chemical Treatments** Key Guidelines: Reduce frequency of relaxers, texturizers, and color treatments. Title: How to Determine if Bonsai Trees Are Poisonous to Cats Introduction: Bonsai trees are popular indoor plants that can add a touch of nature and tranquility to any home. However, if you own a cat, it's essential to ensure that your bonsai tree doesn't pose a threat to your feline friend. This comprehensive guide will help you understand whether various types of bonsai trees are poisonous to cats and provide steps to safeguard your pet's health. Step 1: Identify Your Bonsai Tree Species To determine if your bonsai tree is toxic to cats, first identify its species. Common bonsai varieties include juniper, azalea, jade, boxwood, pine, and ficus. Each species has unique characteristics and potential risks. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Consult reputable sources like botanical gardens or horticultural societies for assistance with identification. - Keep labels or tags provided by nurseries or suppliers as they often contain relevant information about plant species. Step 2: Research Specific Plant Varieties Once you know the type(s) of bonsai trees in question, research their toxicity levels. Several online resources list common household plants and their safety profiles regarding pets. Some reliable websites include the ASPCA (American Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals), the Humane Society, and PetMD. Key Points: - Be aware that some sites may contradict one another due to differing opinions among experts. - Focus on multiple credible sources to gather consistent data. Step 3: Understand Potential Symptoms Familiarize yourself with symptoms associated with ingestion of potentially harmful bonsai species. These could range from mild irritation to severe medical emergencies. Common Signs of Poisoning: - Vomiting - Diarrhea - Drooling - Loss of appetite - Lethargy - Difficulty breathing Step 4: Observe Cat Behavior Around Bonsai Trees Monitor your cat closely when introducing a new bonsai into your home. Note any changes in behavior such as increased interest, sniffing, pawing, biting, or chewing on the tree. Important Considerations: - Even non-toxic plants can cause gastrointestinal upset if consumed excessively. - Certain cats might be more prone to exploring and nibbling on plants than others. Step 5: Implement Safety Measures If your chosen bonsai variety poses a risk to your cat, take necessary precautions to minimize exposure. Here are some strategies: - Relocate the bonsai tree to an area inaccessible to your cat. - Use barriers like gates or screens to block off spaces containing toxic plants. - Train your cat to avoid certain areas using deterrent methods (citrus scents, loud noises). Additional Suggestions: - Regularly trim leaves and branches within reach of your cat. - Clean up fallen foliage promptly. - Opt for less hazardous alternatives if possible. Conclusion: By following these steps, you can accurately assess whether your bonsai tree is safe for your cat. Remember, prevention is always better than cure; therefore, taking proactive measures ensures both your beloved pet's wellbeing and your enjoyment of beautiful bonsai artistry. [the checkerboard magnet fell off the black base magnet] Find Saved Passwords on iPhone ============================== Follow these steps to view saved passwords on your iPhone: 1. Open the **Settings** app on your iPhone's home screen. The Settings app is typically represented by gray gears. This application allows you to customize settings and features for your device. 2. Scroll down and tap on **Passwords & Accounts**. This option will take you to a list of accounts and their associated passwords stored on your iPhone. These are synced with your iCloud Keychain if you have that feature enabled. 3. Tap on **Website & App Passwords**. You may need to authenticate using Face ID, Touch ID, or passcode to access this section due to its sensitive nature. Here, you can see all the usernames and passwords saved for various websites and apps on your device. 4. Locate the website or service whose password you want to find. By default, entries are sorted alphabetically based on the site name. If necessary, you can search for a specific entry using the search bar at the top of the page. 5. Once you locate the desired entry, tap on it to reveal more details. A new window will appear displaying the username, password, and other information related to the account. 6. To copy the password, press and hold the black circle icon next to the password field until the 'Copy' option appears. Alternatively, double-tap the password field to highlight the text automatically, then choose 'Copy'. Now, you can paste the copied password into another application as needed. 7. (Optional) Enable AutoFill Passwords for even easier login. Go back to **Settings**, scroll down and select **Passwords & Accounts**, and ensure that the toggle switch next to **AutoFill Passwords** is turned ON. With this setting activated, when you visit a supported website or app, iOS will suggest auto-filling your credentials from the iCloud Keychain. 8. For improved security, consider enabling Two-Factor Authentication whenever available. It adds an extra layer of protection against unauthorized access. While performing these actions, always be cautious about sharing any sensitive information, especially over public networks. Key Tips & Guidelines --------------------- * Keep track of your Apple ID and corresponding password securely. They control access to many services linked to your iPhone. * Regularly review and manage saved passwords in your iCloud Keychain to maintain up-to-date records and remove unnecessary ones. * Utilizing strong unique passwords reduces the risk of multiple accounts being compromised simultaneously. Consider using a reputable password manager if remembering complex passwords becomes challenging. * Remember, never share confidential data such as banking PIN numbers or credit card CVV codes through email, messaging apps, or phone calls, even under duress. Legitimate institutions would never request such info via these channels. [watching the ball roll across the floor] A cow jumping over the moon that's right. When an object floats in any liquid, the weight of liquid displaced = the upthrust on the object. example. ice is less dense than water. The RD is about 0.91 An ice cube of 1 cubic centimetre is put in water. It's weight = volume x RD = 0.91 g So 0.91 g of water is displaced (pushed out of the way as the cube settles down in the water). So 91% is below the waterline and 9% above. But the RD is relative. Because it depends what liquid you use as the reference that you measure other substances by. If we lived on a liquid methane planet, we'd probably use that as the reference! On this planet we usually use water as the reference and we have rigged the units so that 1 cc of water weighs 1 g; giving water itself a density of 1. Water will float on a heavier liquid, such as mercury. 1/5 above the surface of the water so it's RD (= density = specific gravity) is 0.8 Let's say we have 1 cubic centimetre of the substance. Then its weight is 0.8 g. Now it is placed in another liquid and it floats with just 1/20 above the surface. That means it displaces 0.95 cc of that liquid and that amount of liquid must weigh 0.8 g (in order to hold the substance up). So the density (specific gravity) of that substance (relative to water) must be = weight / volume = 0.8 / 0.95 = 0.84 Is that the right one that goes on? Since a living thing has two copies of each gene, it can have two different alleles of it at the same time. Often, one allele will be "dominant", meaning that the living thing looks and acts as if it had only that one allele. The unexpressed allele is called "recessive". In other cases, you end up with something in between the two possibilities. In that case, the two alleles are called "co-dominant". Reminder: Definition of Metaphor A metaphor is a writing strategy In a metaphor, something represents something it would not usually represent Metaphors DO NOT use the words "like" or "as." Similes DO use those words. 3. Metaphor skills check Which of these is a metaphor? (Please raise your hand when you know the answer, do not blurt it out.) The goalkeeper was steady as a rock. All the world's a stage. The phone rang as a dog barked outside. It was as easy as taking candy from a baby. 4. A New Kind of Metaphor We have been talking about metaphors at the sentence level. Now we are going to look at metaphors at a story level. Fictional objects or characters are used to represent real-life things. These representations usually have a specific message or lesson to convey. 5. For Example My brother is a busy beaver. Once upon a time, there was a beaver who had no home for the winter. He worked very, very hard to build a dam. That winter he was warm and cozy while other animals were outside in the cold. 6. Metaphors in The Three Little Pigs House of sticks/straw House of brick 7. Most people use "gay" only when talking about someone's sexuality. Sometimes "gay" is used in an insulting way to mean that something is weak, stupid, or less important. For example, "That is so gay!" is used by bigots to say something looks or acts stupid. They may also use the word "queer" in the same way. This inappropriate way of using the words "gay" and "queer" is considered very offensive, as it can be an attack on someone's identity and can assume things about people they don't know. Organs are made up of more than one kind of tissue. The heart is an organ. It is made up of different tissues. It has muscle tissue called myocardium. It has connective tissue on the inside (endocardium), and on the outside (pericardium). The heart has valves that make sure the blood goes the right way through the heart. So the heart is an organ made from several tissues. Momma I know how to do dancing now. Samoa used to be east of the international date line. Pink Floyd were an English rock band that became famous in the 1960s and 1970s. They performed for more than 30 years, and have sold more than 200 million (200,000,000) albums. They are famous for their live shows and deep music. Most of their albums are concept albums, which means every song is linked through a theme. Their first album was "The Piper at the Gates of Dawn", which was released in 1967. Their latest album is "The Endless River", which was released in November 2014. The band were named after the blues artists "Pink Anderson" and "Floyd Council" by Syd Barrett, one of the band's founder members. In 2005, they played at a concert called Live Aid after 24 years of not playing together. The line up was David Gilmour, Roger Waters, Nick Mason and Richard Wright. They have made some very popular albums including "The Dark Side of the Moon," which was released in 1973 and "The Wall," which was released in 1979. After Richard Wright died in 2008, David Gilmour has said that he does not want to reunite, despite Roger saying he might want to. It is now believed that David Gilmour and Roger Waters are friends after becoming enemies for a long time. - draw a circle for a face and tell your child that you are drawing a circle to make a head. - draw 2 eyes, color them, and point them out and tell your child these are eyes. - draw 2 ears, nose, mouth, and hair describing as you go. - ask your child to point to each body part. - take your child's finger and trace the circle with it. - have your child point to his own eyes, nose, ears, mouth, and hair. - let your child color the picture. Many kids have difficulty saying the word "yellow". They often use an "l" sound at the beginning because it is easier to say that way. Young children find it easier to say short words that start with "y" such as "ya" or "yes". Practise the y sound by saying "ya ya ya yeeeellow." Song, Rhyme or Story time For this story you will need five crayons (red, yellow, green, blue, and purple) and a white piece of paper. As you tell the story, draw a rainbow. Note: Change the word Joey to your baby's name. For a girl use she and her instead of he and his. Once there was a baby named Joey (insert your baby's name). On Sunday, Joey found some colourful sticks. He picked them up and put them in his pocket. On Monday, Joey took out the first stick. It was red."I love red,"Joey said. And he drew a red line. (Start rainbow). On Tuesday, Joey took out the second stick. It was yellow. "I love yellow," Joey said. And he drew a yellow line. On Wednesday, Joey took out the third stick. It was green. "I love green," Joey said. And he drew a green line. On Thursday, Joey took out the fourth stick. It was blue. "I love blue," Joey, said. And he drew a blue line. On Friday, Joey took out the last stick. It was purple."I love purple," Joey said. And he drew a purple line.(Finish rainbow). On Saturday, Joey showed his picture to Mama. "Joey," Mama said, "What a beautiful rainbow!" I'm a Learning Rainbow (Sing to traditional "I'm a Little Teapot" tune.) I'm a Learning Rainbow in the sky. With so many colours for you to try. English Checker for Parents rainbow:a colourful curve that appears in the sky after a rainstorm pocket:a small storage place (usually in a pair of pants or a shirt) beautiful:very nice to look at fussy:crying or unhappy nurse:drink milk from a mother's body relax:rest collect:bring many items into one group compare:describe the differences between two or more things laundry:clean clothes pairs:groups of two More fun for Baby: The following books are available new or used online. [mother puts a bright-colored circle made out of a plastic tube around mother's neck.] Were these items um that she played with before? Why do we use adverbials? We use adverbs to give more information about the verb. We use adverbials of manner to say how something happens or how something is done: The children were playing happily. He was driving as fast as possible. We use adverbials of place to say where something happens: I saw him there. We met in London. We use adverbials of time to say when or how often something happens: They start work at six thirty. They usually go to work by bus. We use adverbials of probability to show how certain we are about something. - Perhaps the weather will be fine. - He is certainly coming to the party. Tags for teachers A - Z of Content - 1 de 6 A funeral is a ceremony that is held because someone has died. A funeral is a gathering of friends and family of the dead person. They will mourn the dead person, meaning that they will feel and show sadness that the person has died, and also say thanks for the life the person had. A funeral is usually held with the dead body present but hidden in a coffin, but there are also open casket funerals. During the funeral, or soon after it, the dead body will be buried, cremated (burned) or made to disappear in some other way. A memorial service or celebration of life is a funeral in which the dead body is not present. I don't like to do it because I like to do the t. Access prior knowledge and build background about what birds look like. Explore and apply concepts of what birds look like to how birds are different from other animals. Children will: Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of what birds are. Orally use words that name and describe features that birds have. Extend oral vocabulary by speaking about how different kinds of birds look the same and how they look different. Use key concept words [bird, feathers, beak, wings, group, behavior]. Explain Use the slideshow to review the key concept words. Explain that children are going to learn about: What a bird is. What a group is. How birds look the same and different. Some behaviors that birds have. Model After the host introduces the slide show, point to the birds on screen. Ask children: What do you see in this picture? (a bird sitting on a tree). How do you know this animal is a bird? (it has wings, a beak, feathers, etc.) Ask children: How do birds look different from other animals? (They have wings, feathers, and two legs. Some birds can fly.) Proceed to the next slide. Say: These two different kinds of birds may look different, but they both belong to the same group of animals. How are the places that these birds live different? (A pond is full of water and in nature. A farm is taken care of by people) Guided Practice Guide children through the next two slides, showing them the different kinds of birds and their behaviors. Always have children describe how the birds are similar and how they are different. Apply Play the games that follow. Have them discuss with their partner the different topics that appear during the Talk About It feature. After the first game, ask children to discuss how they have seen birds behave in nature. It is fragile (easily broken) and crystalline. It burns easily, releasing toxic fumes of sulfur dioxide. It has a very faint odor. If it is melted and cooled very quickly, it makes a rubbery form of sulfur that is called "plastic sulfur". It gradually turns back into the yellow brittle form. It does not dissolve in water. The smell normally known as "sulfur" comes from hydrogen sulfide and similar chemicals. These sulfides are produced when things decay without air. Anyway you'd height find a rocket first. Title: How to Arrange Flowers in a Vase Introduction: Flower arrangement is an art form that can bring life and beauty into any space. It's also a great way to express your creativity and personal style. This tutorial will guide you through the process of arranging flowers in a vase, from selecting the right flowers to final placement. Materials Needed: 1. Fresh flowers 2. A clean vase 3. Sharp scissors or floral shears 4. Floral foam (optional) 5. Water Step 1: Choose Your Flowers Selecting the right flowers is the first crucial step. Consider the occasion, room decor, and your personal preference when choosing colors and types of flowers. A mix of textures and sizes often creates visual interest. Remember, odd numbers usually look more natural than even ones. Key Tip: Try to choose flowers with different stages of bloom for added depth and dimension. Step 2: Prepare Your Vase Thoroughly clean your vase with soap and warm water before starting. Any residue could shorten the lifespan of your flowers. Fill the vase about halfway with lukewarm water. If using floral foam, soak it completely in water before placing it inside the vase. Step 3: Condition Your Flowers Trim about an inch off each stem at a diagonal under running water. This exposes fresh flower tissue which absorbs water better. Remove any leaves that would be submerged in water to prevent bacterial growth. Step 4: Create a Grid Pattern (for narrow-necked vases) If you're using a tall, narrow-necked vase, create a grid pattern across the top using tape. This holds the stems in place and provides stability for your arrangement. Step 5: Start Building Your Arrangement Begin by adding larger foliage or greenery to establish height and fullness. Place these elements around the perimeter of the vase, creating a 'foundation' for your design. Step 6: Add Focal Flowers These are typically the largest and showiest flowers in your selection. Position them slightly above eye level, spacing them evenly throughout the arrangement. They should face outward to ensure visibility from all angles. Step 7: Incorporate Secondary Flowers Secondary flowers add detail and color variation. Place them between the focal flowers, filling gaps and complementing the overall shape of the arrangement. Step 8: Finish With Filler Flowers Filler flowers serve to soften the arrangement, blending everything together. Insert these where there are still visible gaps, ensuring no single type of flower dominates the view. Step 9: Check Symmetry & Balance Stand back occasionally to assess symmetry and balance. Adjust as necessary until satisfied with the overall appearance. Step 10: Final Touches Once happy with the layout, recut the stems if needed and reimmerse them fully in water. Make sure there's enough water in the vase, changing it every two days to keep flowers fresh. Conclusion: Remember, practice makes perfect. Don't be discouraged if your first few attempts don't turn out exactly as planned. Over time, you'll develop your own unique style and technique. Happy arranging! These are similar to the British imperial units and also based on the units used in the United Kingdom from before American Independence. Some of the units are different to the British ones. For example, there are 20 imperial fluid ounces in an imperial pint, but 16 US fluid ounces in a US pint. Additionally, the US fluid ounce is slightly bigger than the imperial fluid ounce. The result is that US pints and gallons are smaller than imperial pints and gallons. In the United States, the metric system has been legal for trade since 1866 but other measurements such as the gallon, inch, and the pound are still widely used. Title: How to Allow Facebook Messenger to Access Your Photos Facebook Messenger is a popular messaging app that allows you to send messages, share photos, and make video calls with your friends on Facebook. To share pictures through this platform, you need to grant Facebook Messenger access to your photo library. This tutorial will guide you through the process of enabling photo permissions for Facebook Messenger on both iOS and Android devices. **For iOS Users:** 1. **Open Settings:** Find and tap on the gray gear icon labeled 'Settings' on your home screen or within the app library. The settings app grants you control over various aspects of your device, including its privacy options. 2. **Tap Privacy:** Scroll down until you find the 'Privacy' option (it has a blue face-icon) and select it. Here, you can manage who can see your information and what apps have access to specific features. 3. **Select Photos:** Under the Privacy menu, choose 'Photos.' You will be presented with a list of all installed applications requesting permission to access your photo library. 4. **Find Facebook Messenger:** Locate 'Facebook Messenger' from the list; if it appears under 'Allowed Apps,' then it already has permission to view and share your photos. If it isn't listed here or shows up as 'Never', proceed to the next step. 5. **Grant Permission:** Toggle the switch beside 'Facebook Messenger' to allow it to access your photos. After toggling, you may receive a prompt asking for confirmation about changing the photo access setting. Confirm by tapping 'OK'. Once enabled, the toggle should turn green indicating active permission. 6. **Test the Functionality:** Open Facebook Messenger and start a conversation. Try sending a picture using the paperclip icon next to the text input field. If successful, you should now see an image thumbnail appear within the chat window confirming that Facebook Messenger can indeed access and attach photos from your library. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Only approve trusted apps like Facebook Messenger to ensure your data remains secure. * Regularly review which apps have access to your photos since these permissions could change when updating the operating system or after installing new apps. * Remember, denying photo access won't prevent you from receiving shared images but will stop you from sharing them directly from your phone's gallery. **For Android Users:** All the time I was allowed to look into her eyes, hold her hands, listen to what she said, accompany her wherever she went. Title: How to Ask Your Mum to Go Somewhere Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of asking your mum to go somewhere with you. Going out with family members can be an enjoyable experience that strengthens relationships and creates lasting memories. However, approaching this topic may seem intimidating or nerve-wracking for some individuals. By following these steps, you'll learn how to effectively communicate your desires while being considerate of your mother's feelings and schedule. Step 1: Choose a Suitable Destination Before initiating the conversation, decide where you would like to invite your mum. Consider her interests, preferences, and accessibility needs when selecting a location. For instance, if she enjoys art, visiting an exhibition might be appealing; if she has mobility issues, opt for wheelchair-friendly venues. This thoughtful planning demonstrates respect for her comfort and shows genuine interest in spending quality time together. Key Tips: * Avoid suggesting places associated with conflict or negative emotions (e.g., locations related to past arguments). * Make sure the destination is within your budget to avoid financial stress during the outing. Step 2: Find an Appropriate Time Approach your mum at a convenient moment when she isn't preoccupied with other tasks or commitments. Engaging in meaningful conversations becomes challenging amidst distractions. Look for opportunities such as meal times, car rides, or leisurely walks when both of you can engage in undivided attention. Guidelines: * Refrain from requesting something major right before bedtime or during hectic mornings. * If possible, give her advance notice so she can prepare herself mentally and physically for the excursion. Step 3: Initiate the Conversation Politely Begin by expressing gratitude for her presence in your life and emphasize your desire to spend more time together. Use positive language and maintain eye contact throughout the discussion to convey sincerity and respect. Example Script: "Mum, I really appreciate having you in my life. Lately, I've been thinking about how much we used to enjoy going out together, just the two of us. Would you be open to doing that again soon?" Step 4: Present the Proposed Plan Share the details of your chosen destination, including what it offers and why you believe she would find it interesting. Explain any logistics involved, such as transportation arrangements, admission fees, and estimated duration of stay. Be prepared to answer questions or address concerns she may have regarding the proposed plan. Step 5: Respect Her Response Listen attentively to her reply, whether it's affirmative, hesitant, or negative. Acknowledge her thoughts and feelings without becoming defensive or dismissive. If she declines, ask if there are alternative activities she would prefer instead. Maintaining open communication fosters understanding and mutual respect between both parties. Potential Scenarios: * If she agrees immediately, proceed by setting a date and discussing further preparations. * If she seems uncertain, offer additional information or reassurance to help alleviate any apprehensions. * If she declines, kindly probe for reasons behind her decision and explore potential alternatives. Conclusion: By following these steps, you increase the likelihood of successfully inviting your mum to join you on an outing while maintaining a healthy relationship built on trust and consideration. Remember that effective communication involves active listening, empathy, patience, and respect - qualities essential for creating memorable experiences with loved ones. Title: How to Activate the Dark Theme on TweetDeck TweetDeck is a popular social media management tool that allows users to monitor multiple Twitter accounts simultaneously. The platform offers several customization options, including the ability to enable a dark theme for improved visibility and reduced eye strain during low-light conditions. This tutorial will guide you through the process of activating the dark theme on TweetDeck. **Step 1:** Open your preferred web browser and navigate to <https://tweetdeck.twitter.com/> This action takes you to the TweetDeck login page. By accessing this URL directly, you ensure that you're using the official website rather than any potential phishing or malicious sites mimicking the service. **Step 2:** Log into your Twitter account by entering your username (or email address) and password. Click 'Log in'. By signing in with your legitimate Twitter credentials, you grant TweetDeck permission to manage your connected Twitter profiles securely. You can trust that all data transmitted between your device and TweetDeck servers are encrypted via HTTPS protocols, protecting sensitive information from unauthorized interception. **Key Tip**: If you have two-factor authentication enabled on your Twitter account, remember to enter both your password and the verification code sent to your trusted device when prompted. This security feature adds an extra layer of protection against cybercriminals attempting to gain unauthorized access to your online assets. **Step 3:** Once logged in, look at the top right corner of the screen where you should see three horizontal lines representing the menu icon. Click on them. Accessing the main menu reveals various configuration settings and features available within TweetDeck. Presenting these functions as concise icons conserves valuable screen space while maintaining ease of navigation throughout the interface. **Step 4:** From the dropdown list, locate and click on 'Settings'. The 'Settings' option enables you to modify general preferences related to functionality, appearance, alerts, and other aspects of TweetDeck tailored to suit individual user needs. **Step 5:** Within the left sidebar under the 'Accounts' section, select the profile(s) whose default column view you wish to change. Ensure you choose the correct account since each one might have different display settings based on personal preference or necessity. Changing themes only affects columns belonging to the selected profile(s). **Step 6:** After choosing the appropriate profile(s), find and click on 'Theme' located near the bottom of the left sidebar. Selecting 'Theme' presents you with two choices - Light and Dark. Choosing the latter sets the background color of all associated columns to black, text to white, and highlights to grayish blue hues, making it easier on the eyes during nighttime usage or extended sessions in dim environments. **Step 7:** To save changes, simply close the Settings tab. Your chosen theme will automatically be applied across all open TweetDeck windows. Confirming modifications ensures consistency across devices if you utilize TweetDeck on multiple platforms such as desktops, laptops, tablets, or smartphones. In conclusion, following these seven steps provides you with the knowledge necessary to activate the dark theme on TweetDeck. Not only does this enhance visual comfort but also contributes to reducing overall digital eyestrain often experienced during prolonged computer usage. Enjoy managing your Twitter presence more efficiently and ergonomically! = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Peters/011000a.cha = = = [Maisie throws back the ball overhand. [BRO makes several unintelligible sounds as he plays] Title: How to Change the Computer Language Changing the computer language can be helpful if you are using a device that is set up in a different language than your own or if you want to learn a new language. This guide will walk you through changing the computer language on various devices and operating systems. **Windows 10:** Step 1: Click the Windows Start button (the one with the Windows logo) at the bottom left corner of your screen. This opens the Start Menu. Explanation: The Start Button provides access to all installed applications, settings, and power options. By clicking it, you begin the process of changing the display language. Key tip: If you have a touchscreen, simply tap the Windows icon instead. Step 2: Select Settings from the list of apps displayed in the Start Menu. Explanation: Accessing Settings allows you to customize many aspects of your Windows experience, including the display language. Step 3: Choose Time & Language within Settings. Explanation: Time & Language contains several configuration categories related to time format, regional formats, and languages used by the system. Step 4: Navigate to the Language tab on the left sidebar. Explanation: Here, you'll see an option called 'Windows Display Language'. It shows the current interface language. Step 5: Under 'Windows Display Language', click on the current language and then select the preferred language from the dropdown menu. Explanation: Changing this setting changes the primary language used throughout the operating system such as menus, dialogs, and built-in apps. Step 6: After selecting your preferred language, restart your PC. Explanation: To ensure the change takes effect, save any open work beforehand, close all running programs, and finally hit Restart Now when prompted. Guideline: Keep in mind some software may require manual updates to reflect these changes. Consult their respective documentation for guidance. **MacOS:** Step 1: Open System Preferences which is located in the Dock or Apple Menu. Explanation: System Preferences allow users to modify numerous features and behaviors of macOS, among them being the ability to alter the user interface language. Step 2: Find and click on Language & Region. Explanation: This section manages geographical location data, calendar preferences, measurement units, etc., along with supported languages. Step 3: Within the newly opened pane, find the 'Preferred Languages' box. Explanation: Inside this area resides a ranked listing of all available languages on your Mac. Step 4: Drag your chosen language to the top position. Explanation: Reordering here determines priority given to selected languages; moving your choice above others guarantees its usage whenever possible. Step 5: Log out and log back into your account. Explanation: Upon logging out, the OS applies the updated linguistic configurations during re-authentication. Guideline: Be patient while system components transition to the new language. Some elements might take longer due to caching mechanisms. By following these steps, you should now successfully changed the computer language on either a Windows 10 or MacOS machine. Remember, certain third-party applications might need manual intervention to fully support the new locale, so don't forget to check those individually too! See this is a kind of magnet? Very often the result of a scientific experiment is difficult or impossible to replicate later, either by independent researchers or by the original researchers themselves.
