Title: How to Master Various Cuddling Positions Introduction: Cuddling is an intimate act that can strengthen bonds between people, be it romantic partners, friends, or family members. It provides comfort, security, and promotes relaxation. However, finding the perfect cuddling position may require some experimentation. This tutorial will guide you through various cuddling positions, explaining their benefits and offering tips for optimal enjoyment. Step 1: The Classic Spoon The spooning position involves two individuals lying side by side with one person (the big spoon) wrapping their arms around the other (the little spoon). This pose fosters feelings of protection, warmth, and closeness. For maximum comfort, ensure both parties' hips and legs are aligned; this prevents strain on backs and necks. Adjust your pillows as needed to maintain spinal alignment. Key Tip: If you find yourself getting hot quickly, consider using thinner blankets or leaving part of your body uncovered. Overheating could disrupt the peaceful atmosphere. Step 2: Face-to-Face Embrace This position requires both individuals to lie down facing each other. You then wrap your arms around each other creating an intimate connection. Eye contact can enhance emotional bonding, making this ideal for couples looking to deepen their relationship. To avoid discomfort in the neck region, prop up your head using a pillow. Guideline: Remember to keep communication open about what feels good and what doesn't during any form of physical intimacy including cuddling. Everyone has different preferences so respect those boundaries. Step 3: The Cherish Hold In this position, the 'cherisher' holds the other person closely while they rest their head on the cherisher's chest. It's particularly soothing due to the heartbeat rhythm and tends to create a sense of safety and calmness. Make sure the cherished individual is comfortable by providing enough support under their back and neck. Important Note: Always listen to your body. If something starts feeling uncomfortable or painful, don't hesitate to adjust your position or take a break from cuddling. Step 4: The Lap Pillow One person sits upright with their legs crossed or stretched out straight whereas the other uses their lap as a pillow. Often seen in public settings like parks or cafes, this position allows for easy conversation and maintains a level of privacy despite being in public spaces. Just remember to ask permission before turning someone into your personal chair! Fun Fact: Research shows that touch, such as cuddling, releases oxytocin which reduces stress and increases happiness levels. Step 5: The Back Hug Here, one person lies flat on their stomach while the other hugs them from behind, resting their chin on the first person's shoulder. This position offers reassurance and love without feeling overly intrusive, making it great for friends or newer relationships. Be mindful of your arm placement to prevent putting too much weight on the receiver. Bonus Step: Mix & Match Feel free to mix these basic positions to discover unique combinations that suit you best. Maybe try a hybrid where you start off face-to-face but end up switching to spoons midway through the night. Exploration keeps things interesting and fun! Conclusion: Remember, there's no right or wrong way to cuddle as long as it's consensual and enjoyable for all involved. Take time to explore these positions, communicate openly, and most importantly, relax and enjoy the moment together. Happy cuddling! Of course, you can find where it lands, and--oh, wait a minute! Maybe it opens in the next door building. [toys banging] you knocked mommy's tower over! Tell Gail and Melissa where you're goin'. Now roll over and get some XXX. A computer has several main parts. When comparing a computer to a human body, the CPU is like a brain. It does most of the thinking and tells the rest of the computer how to work. The CPU is on the Motherboard, which is like the skeleton. It provides the basis for where the other parts go, and carries the nerves that connect them to each other and the CPU. The motherboard is connected to a power supply, which provides electricity to the entire computer. The various drives (CD drive, floppy drive, and on many newer computers, USB flash drive) act like eyes, ears, and fingers, and allow the computer to read different types of storage, in the same way that a human can read different types of books. The hard drive is like a human's memory, and keeps track of all the data stored on the computer. Most computers have a sound card or another method of making sound, which is like vocal cords, or a voice box. Connected to the sound card are speakers, which are like a mouth, and are where the sound comes out. Computers might also have a graphics card, which helps the computer to create visual effects, such as 3D environments, or more realistic colors, and more powerful graphics cards can make more realistic or more advanced images, in the same way a well trained artist can. Blue decided to practice every day. He would sing in the morning, afternoon, and evening. His friends noticed that Blue was working hard and started to encourage him. Blue was very happy that he could help his friend turn better from being dead. SEASONS Find out about alignments --> People often say that the sun rises in the east. That's not exactly true. If you watch the sun rise each morning over the course of an entire year, you'll see that the sun doesn't always rise in the same place. the summer, the sun rises somewhat north of due east. The date on which it rises the farthest to the north is roughly June 21, the summer solstice and the longest day of the year in the Northern Hemisphere. Why does the sun's path across the sky change throughout the year? The reason is that the earth is tilted-by 23.5 degrees, to be exact-with respect to its orbit. When it's summer in the Northern hemisphere, the North Pole is tilted toward the sun and the sun shines on a greater area of the Northern Hemisphere. As the earth spins, places in the Northern Hemisphere stay in the sunlit area longer, and that means the days are longer. it's winter in the Northern Hemisphere, the North Pole is tilted away from the sun, the sun shines on a smaller area of the Northern Hemisphere, and the days are shorter. Children under the age of four are at risk for choking on food. Here are a few tips on how to reduce the risk of choking. 1. Check fish to make sure there are no bones. 2. Slice hot dogs lengthwise into quarters. 3. Hard raw vegetables and fruits can be shredded. 4. Remove the pits from apples, cherries, peaches, plums and other fruits. 5. Do not serve large chunks of food. 6. Cut cherry tomatoes and grapes into quarters. 7. Spread the peanut butter in thin layers. 8. Chop nuts and seeds fine. 9. Avoid popcorn and hard candies. In other words, use this to talk about an obligation that started in the past and has continued until the present. When complaining about stairs, people often use the phrase "walk up and down". "Stairs" can't easily be counted, but here are ways that you can specifically count them: - One whole set of stairs in a building is called a "staircase". Most buildings only have one staircase, but some large buildings might have 2 or 3 separate staircases. - One "flight of stairs" connects two floors. For example, between the ground floor and the 2nd floor is one flight of stairs; between the 2nd and 3rd floor is another flight, and so on. There can be several flights of stairs in one staircase. - Sometimes there will be a "landing" halfway up a flight of stairs. This is a wider area where the stairs turn around and go in another direction. - A staircase or a flight of stairs is made up of lots of "steps". You usually walk up one step at a time. I can hear her now--the way, one cold, black Sunday morning when, on account of an extraordinary fog, we had not gone to church, she broke it to me by the school-room fire. I was reading a history-book by the lamp--when we didn't go to church we had to read history-books--and I suddenly heard her say, out of the fog, which was in the room, and _apropos_ of nothing: 'Papa has done something wicked.' And the curious thing was that I believed it on the spot and have believed it ever since, though she could tell me nothing more--neither what was the wickedness, nor how she knew, nor what would happen to him, nor anything else about it. We had our sense, always, that all sorts of things _had_ happened, were all the while happening, to him; so that when Marian only said she was sure, tremendously sure, that she had made it out for herself, but that that was enough, I took her word for it--it seemed somehow so natural. We were not, however, to ask mother--which made it more natural still, and I said never a word. Can you squeeze it with your hand? So Lisa couldn't decide what she wants. Once upon a time there were two best friends, Bob and Nancy. Every day they would go together to the forest nearby to play. One day, Bob wanted to cut down a tree with an axe. Nancy looked scared and said, "Let's not chop down a tree. That's wrong." But Bob just rolled his eyes and grasped the axe. Nancy looked more and more miserable as she watched Bob chopping down the tree. Finally, she shouted, "Stop!" With a sigh, Bob put down the axe and they sat together on the ground. To cheer Nancy up, Bob suggested, "Let's go skipping around the pond." Nancy smiled and skipped around the pond with Bob. The sun shone on their faces. Nancy forgot about the axe and she laughed as she skipped around the pond with Bob. Soon, the pond looked like a swirling of colours and Nancy was no longer miserable. They were back to being the best of friends. Bell, with his assistant, devised a receiver, consisting of a stretched film or drum with a bit of magnetised iron attached to its middle, and free to vibrate in front of the pole of an electromagnet in circuit with the line. This apparatus was completed on June 2, 1875. On July 7, he instructed his assistant to make a second receiver which could be used with the first, and a few days later they were tried together, at each end of the line, which ran from a room in the inventor's house at Boston to the cellar underneath. Bell, in the room, held one instrument in his hands, while Watson in the cellar listened at the other. The inventor spoke into his instrument, ""Do you understand what I say?"" and Mr. Watson rushed back into the upstairs and answered ""Yes."" The first successful two-way telephone call was not made until March 10, 1876 when Bell spoke into his device, "Mr. Watson, come here, I want to see you." and Watson answered back and came into the room to see Bell. The first long distance telephone call was made on August 10, 1876 by Bell from the family home in Brantford, Ontario to his assistant in Paris, Ontario, some 16 km (10 mi.) away. These people enjoyed a high social status. In English-speaking countries, the traditional dessert is Christmas plum pudding. Nowadays these puddings are often bought from bakers, but many people make their own to a family recipe. The tradition came from the Middle Ages when the pudding was used to preserve some of the fruit from the autumn until the mid-winter. A traditional pudding is baked six weeks before Christmas and is left tied up in a cloth or basin with a cloth covering, in a cool place. Stirring the pudding is sometimes a family tradition, with everyone making a wish as they stir. Traditionally a silver coin would be stirred into the pudding, to bring luck to the person who found it. Nowadays most coins cannot be used because they are not made of silver so taste horrible and may be poisonous. Some families use old coins or silver charms. On Christmas Day the pudding must be boiled in a pot for several hours. When it is served, the cloth is cut off, brandy is poured onto the pudding, and it is set on fire before it is carried to the table. Christmas crackers are used to decorate and are often opened prior to serving dish or starter course. It is usually served with hard sauce. If we shoot single photons (single units of light) from a laser at a sheet of photographic film, we will see a single spot of light on the developed film. If we put a sheet of metal in between, and make two very narrow slits in the sheet, when we fire many photons at the metal sheet, and they have to go through the slits, then we will see something remarkable. All the way across the sheet of developed film we will see a series of bright and dark bands. We can use mathematics to tell exactly where the bright bands will be and how bright the light was that made them, that is, we can tell ahead of time how many photons will fall on each band. But if we slow the process down and see where each photon lands on the screen we can never tell ahead of time where the next one will show up. We can know for sure that it is most likely that a photon will hit the center bright band, and that it gets less and less likely that a photon will show up at bands farther and farther from the center. So we know for sure that the bands will be brightest at the center and get dimmer and dimmer farther away. But we never know for sure which photon will go into which band. Use this menu to view and help create subtitles for this video in many different languages. You'll probably want to hide YouTube's captions if using these subtitles. - Welcome to the presentation on BASIC ADDITION. - I know what you're thinking: - "Sal, addition doesn't seem so basic to me." - Well, I apologize. - I hope I -- - Hopefully, by the end of this presentation, - or in a couple of weeks, it will seem basic. - So lets get started with, - I guess we could say, some problems. - Well, let's say I start with an old classic. - 1 + 1 - And I think you already know how to do this. - But, I'll kind of show you a way of doing this, - in case you don't have this memorized, - or you haven't already mastered this. - You say, well, if I have - (Let's call that an avocado.) - If I have 1 avocado, - and then you were to give me another avocado, - how many avocados do I now have? - Well, let's see. I have 1 ... 2 avocados. - So 1 + 1 is equal to 2. - Now, I know what you are thinking: - "That was too easy." - So, let me give you something a little bit more difficult. - I like the avocados. I might stick with that theme. - What is 3 + 4? - Hmm. This is, I think, a more difficult problem. - Well, let's stick with the avocados. - And in case you don't know what an avocado is, - it's, it's actually a very delicious fruit. - It's actually the fattiest of all the fruits. - You probably didn't even think it was a fruit -- - even if you [have eaten] one. - So, let's say I have 3 avocados. - 1, 2, 3. Right? 1, 2, 3. - And let's say you were to give me 4 more avocados. - So let me put this 4 in yellow, - so you know that these are the ones you're giving me. - So how many total avocados do I have now? - That's 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 avocados. - So 3 + 4 is equal to 7. - And now I am going to introduce you - to another way of thinking about this. - It's called the number line. - And, actually, I think this is how I do it in my head, - when I forget -- if I don't have it memorized. - So [on the] number line, I just write all the numbers in order, - and I go high enough just so I can -- - [so that] all the numbers I am using are, kind of, in it. - So, you know the first number is 0, - which is nothing. - Maybe you don't know; but now you know. - And then you go to - 1 (one) - 2 (two) - 3 (three) - 4 (four) - 5 (five) - 6 (six) - 7 (seven) - 8 (eight) - 9 (nine) - 10 (ten) - It keeps going, - 11 (eleven) - So, we're sayng 3 + 4. His eyes were also changed back to their black dot appearance. His chin was also further enlarged with only a few minor changes due to Sagendorf's own unique art style. This design would continue to be used by Sagendorf's successors in Popeye comics, such as George Wildman, Bobby London and several others throughout the 70s, 80s and future newspaper comic strips depending on the artist, with Segar and Sagendorf's designs usually being interchanged. Adding a mobile signature to your Gmail account can be a great way to ensure that all outgoing emails from your phone include a professional or personalized touch. This guide will walk you through the process of adding a mobile signature to your Gmail account, with clear explanations and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1: Open the Gmail App on Your Mobile Device** To begin, open the Gmail app on your mobile device. If you are not already logged into your Gmail account, enter your email address and password to log in. **Step 2: Tap the Three Horizontal Lines in the Top Left Corner** Once you are logged into your Gmail account, tap the three horizontal lines (also known as the hamburger menu) located in the top left corner of the screen. This will reveal a sidebar with various options. **Step 3: Scroll Down and Select "Settings"** From the sidebar, scroll down until you see the "Settings" option. Tap this to access your Gmail settings. **Step 4: Choose the Email Address You Want to Edit** If you have multiple email addresses associated with your Gmail account, select the one you want to add a mobile signature to by tapping on its name. **Step 5: Locate the "Signature" Option** After selecting the appropriate email address, look for the "Signature" option within the settings. It should be near the bottom of the list under the "General" section. **Step 6: Enable the Signature Feature** Before creating your mobile signature, make sure the signature feature is enabled. To do so, toggle the switch next to "Signature" to the ON position. The switch should turn blue when activated. **Key Tips:** * Make sure the signature feature is only active for the email address(es) you wish to add signatures to. * Leaving the signature feature disabled for certain accounts can help avoid confusion when sending emails from different addresses. **Step 7: Create Your Mobile Signature** Now it's time to create your mobile signature! Here are some ideas for what to include: * Your full name * Job title / company name (if using this email for business purposes) * Contact information such as phone number or alternative email address * A link to your website or social media profiles * An inspirational quote or motto Type your chosen text into the "Signature" box provided. Remember that formatting options like bold, italics, and bullet points may also be available to enhance readability. **Key Guidelines:** * Keep your mobile signature brief yet informative; aim for no more than four lines of text. * Avoid including sensitive information like physical mailing addresses. * Use consistent branding if using the signature for business purposes. **Step 8: Save Your New Mobile Signature** Once you have created your mobile signature, tap the back button at the top left corner of the screen to save your changes. A confirmation message should appear briefly before disappearing. **Step 9: Test Your New Mobile Signature** Finally, test your new mobile signature by composing an email. After typing your message, check the bottom of the draft to ensure that your signature appears correctly. Congratulations! You have successfully added a mobile signature to your Gmail account. With these simple steps, you can now enjoy the convenience and customization of having a unique signature for all outgoing messages sent from your mobile device. If a person or an animal touched a bare wire carrying current, they could get hurt or even die depending on how strong the current was and how much electrical energy the current is transmitting. You should be careful around electrical sockets and bare wires that might be carrying current. Most guitars have six strings, but there are also guitars with four, seven, eight, ten, or twelve strings. More strings make the instrument sound fuller. The neck of a guitar has bars or marks called frets. Frets help a guitarist know where to put his or her fingers to get the right pitch when playing. Standard tuning defines the string pitches as E, A, D, G, B, and E, from lowest (low E2) to highest (high E4). Standard tuning is used by most guitarists, and frequently used tunings can be understood as variations on standard tuning. Around 176,000 inhabitants with secondary residence also live in administrative city limits but they are not calculated in this official census. According to some estimates that population counts around 200,000 people. The fast growing Munich urban area has 2,667,000 inhabitants (2008 estimate). Munich city with all suburbs at 12,000 km has 4,700,000 inhabitants and Munich metropolitan region that covers 27,700 km and includes Augsburg, Ingolstadt, Landshut, Rosenheim and Landsberg has around 6,000,000 inhabitants. Munich is the 12th largest city in EU by population within city limits and the 14th largest urban area in Europe. Its metropolitan area ranks among largest metro areas in Europe. As of December 2008, 47.3% of Munich's residents belong to no religious group, 38.3% are Roman Catholic, 14.0% are Lutheran Protestants and 0.3% are Jewish. There is also a small Old Catholic parish and an Episcopal Church. Title: Discreetly Breastfeed Your Baby on the Go: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Breastfeeding is a natural and beautiful way to nourish your baby. However, new mothers may feel uncomfortable or anxious about breastfeeding in public places. This guide will help you learn how to discreetly breastfeed your baby while on the go, ensuring both your comfort and convenience. Step 1: Choose the Right Clothing Wear comfortable, loose-fitting clothes that allow easy access to your breasts. Nursing bras, tank tops, or button-down shirts are ideal choices. Layering your clothing can also provide an extra layer of discretion. By wearing a camisole under a cardigan, for example, you can lift the top layer without exposing your stomach. * Key Tip: Opt for darker colors or patterns, which can help conceal any potential leaks and make stains less noticeable. Step 2: Select the Ideal Location Look for quiet, secluded areas where you can sit comfortably. Family restrooms, nursing rooms, or corners with chairs work well. If these options aren't available, try facing away from foot traffic or using large bags as makeshift screens. * Guideline: Always prioritize cleanliness when choosing a location to ensure your baby's safety. Step 3: Use a Nursing Cover or Blanket A lightweight nursing cover or blanket can offer additional privacy during feedings. Drape it over your shoulder and chest, creating a tent-like structure that shields your baby from view. Make sure there's enough space for air circulation to prevent overheating. * Key Tip: Practice using the cover at home before attempting to do so in public. This will help you become more adept at positioning it properly and latching your baby while covered. Step 4: Perfect Your Latch Technique Ensure a proper latch by supporting your baby's head with one hand and guiding them towards your breast with the other. Their mouth should be wide open, covering most of the areola. Proper latching reduces discomfort and increases milk flow. * Guideline: Listen for swallowing sounds to confirm they are feeding effectively. Step 5: Maintain Good Posture Sit up straight with your feet flat on the ground. This posture promotes better milk production and minimizes back pain. Additionally, keeping your shoulders relaxed allows for easier maneuverability of your clothing and nursing cover. Step 6: Master Distraction Tactics Engage your baby in eye contact and conversation during feedings. Sing songs, recite nursery rhymes, or talk softly to keep their attention focused on you rather than passersby. * Key Tip: Bring along toys or teething rings to further distract your little one if needed. Step 7: Be Confident and Relaxed Exude confidence and maintain a calm demeanor throughout the process. People are generally respectful and understanding when they see a mother attending to her child's needs. Remember that you have every right to nurse your baby wherever you are legally allowed to be present. Conclusion: With practice and patience, discreetly breastfeeding your baby on the go becomes second nature. Embrace this special bonding time with your infant while confidently navigating public spaces. Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Daisy. While arithmetic and geometric sequences involve a rule that uses a constant number, recursion sequences use the terms themselves in the rule. One term in recursion sequences is determined from using the terms before it. This concept of recursion sequences can be difficult to fully comprehend, but is found often in mathematics. For example, the Fibonacci sequence is a famous recursion sequence. So we have formulas for arithmetic sequences and geometric sequences. Okay? Whenever we are going from one term to the next by addition or subtraction or multiplication division, we know how to fit those 2 a formula. Okay? What we don't know is recursive formulas and what that means is that the term that we actually have depends on more than just the previous term and a set rate or difference, okay? So what I have behind me is the fibonacci sequence. Okay? You may have seen it before but basically you start with 1 and 1 and then to get the next term, you always just to add the 2 terms before it. So to get the third term we add the first 2, 1+1 is 2. To get the next one, the 2 before it, before it. 1+2 is 3, 2+3 is 5, 3+5 the next one will be 8 so and so forth, okay? So we can't just write a term by the term right previous to it, okay? We have to actually have it written in terms of the terms before it and the one before that. So how we would actually write out the fibonacci sequence in sort of notation we're used to with our a sub ones and twos and n's is we define our first 2 terms as a sub 1 and then our ace of n is just going to be ace of n minus 2 which is actually 2 times before plus ace of n minus 1 which is the term before the term that we're looking at. It'a little hard to do from the abstract use so what I'm going to do is try one out, okay? So what we have is 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Let's try to find the sixth term. Ace of 6. To find the sixth term, we want to find ace of 6-2 just ace of 4 plus ace of 6-1, ace of 5. Ace of 4 is our fourth term, so we just have 1, 2, 3, 4. Ace of 5 is our next term which is going to be 5, and so ace of 6 is just going to be equal to 8, okay? So by just adding the 2 terms before it which we can write as ace of n-1 and minus 2 and together we are able to find terms in the fibonacci sequence. This is convenient for going down the row but it's not convenient for finding terms way down the line. If I want to find the hundredth term, I have to have the ninety ninth and the ninety eighth. In order to find the ninety ninth and the ninety eighth, I would need to know the ones before that as well. Okay? So these aren't quite as easy to use as any other. Just ace of n is equal to a set formula, because in order to find any term we need to know the ones previous to it. Okay? But either way it's a good way to find a sequence when you're starting at the beginning and just moving forward down the line. Imagine you have a family with one boy. We'll call this a "one-boy family." Now let's think about the number of sisters this boy might have. There could be zero sisters (making it a one-boy family), one sister, two sisters, three sisters, or more. But remember, we already have one boy in our family. To keep things simple, imagine flipping a coin to decide whether each new baby will be a girl or a boy. When you flip heads, you have a girl; when you flip tails, you have a boy. Since we already have one boy, pretend he was the first flip and came up tails. So now, all future flips will determine the number of sisters in the family. Now, here's where probability comes into play: since your coin doesn't favor either boys or girls (just like real life!), we assume getting heads (girls) and tails (boys) are equally likely. In other words, there's a 50% chance for each outcome-we denote this as = 0.5 because represents the probability of having a boy. Since we want to find out the average or expected number of sisters, not including the initial boy, we turn to our equation E[X] = + (1 - ) * x. To make sense of this equation for our problem, consider these steps: 1. E[X]: This stands for the Expectation or Average of X, which, in our case, refers to the average number of sisters in families with one boy. 2. : As mentioned before, equals the probability of having a boy (in our simplified scenario, 0.5). 3. (1 - ): Represents the complementary probability, meaning the likelihood of NOT having a boy (getting a head instead of tail); thus, also equal to 0.5. 4. The term (1 - ) * x tells us the contribution of multiple girls (x) to the total number of children in the family. Remember, though, we don't include the original boy in this calculation. So, putting everything together, plugging in = 0.5, and remembering that X is the number of sisters, our final equation becomes E[X] = 0.5 + 0.5 * X. If you would like to calculate the expected number of sisters for specific values of , simply replace with those numbers and follow the same process. Just keep in mind that changing means altering the assumed probabilities of having a boy or a girl, making the concept less intuitive than the balanced 50-50 odds presented earlier. Today, we're going to talk about something called "conjugacy classes" and how they relate to something called "Jordan forms." Now, don't worry if those words sound complicated - I promise we'll break it down into easy-to-understand concepts using things you see every day. Imagine you have a big box full of different toy blocks. Each block might be a different color, shape, or size. You could group these blocks based on their shared characteristics - all red blocks together, all square blocks together, etc. These groups would represent what mathematicians call "conjugacy classes." In our context, two matrices belong to the same conjugacy class if they share similar properties after some transformations. Now let's focus on one type of block - cubes. Cubes come in various sizes, so you may decide to stack them by size: small cubes on top, followed by medium ones, and finally large ones. Interestingly, this concept relates to the "Jordan form" of a matrix when dealing with linear algebra. A special kind of cube arrangement emerges when considering Jordan forms; imagine stacking cubes where each layer rests upon the previous one without gaps. We refer to this specific layout as the "Jordan canonical form," which helps us understand more complex arrangements involving multiple stacks of blocks. In the snippet provided earlier, we encountered three types of stacks made up of three layers (or single larger cubes) corresponding to the first, second, and third matrices mentioned. Let's simplify these ideas further using real-life scenarios. Picture yourself organizing your toys or books; notice that sometimes items share common features allowing you to arrange them systematically. That intuition will guide you through understanding abstract mathematical structures once you grow older. Remember, mathematics isn't just numbers and equations; it's also patterns and relationships waiting to be discovered! She's never heard of a beet before. If you only got one thing for Christmas. Title: How to Delete People on Kik Kik is a popular messaging app that allows you to connect with friends and family. However, there may be situations where you want to delete people from your Kik contacts list. This tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting people on Kik in detail, providing clear instructions and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Open the Kik App Locate the Kik app on your device and tap on its icon to open it. Familiarize yourself with the user interface by looking at the main screen, which typically displays your recent conversations and search bar. Step 2: Access Your Chat List Tap on the chat bubble or speech bubble icon, usually located at the bottom center of the screen. This action takes you to your chat list, displaying all your current chats and contact names. Key Tip: If someone has been bothering you or sending unwanted messages, consider blocking them before deleting their conversation from your chat list. Blocking prevents further communication while keeping their previous messages visible in case you need to refer back to them later. To block someone, go to their profile within an active chat, select 'Settings,' then choose 'Block User.' Step 3: Find the Contact You Want to Delete Scroll through your chat list until you find the person you wish to remove. Keep in mind that this only removes them as a contact; any existing conversations between you two will still appear in your chat history unless you decide to delete those separately (as mentioned in Step 2). Step 4: Go to Their Profile Once you have found the contact, tap on their name or picture to access their profile page. Here, you can view their username, profile information, and other settings related to your interaction with them. Step 5: Select 'Forget Person' Option On the top right corner of the profile page, look for three dots arranged vertically ('...' or 'More') - this indicates additional options. Tapping these dots will reveal several choices, one being 'Forget Person.' Confirm that you would like to forget this individual by selecting the option when prompted. Key Guideline: Choosing 'Forget Person' permanently removes the selected contact from your Kik account. It also erases their message history with you and revokes their ability to start new conversations with you without re-adding them first. Make sure you are certain about removing someone before proceeding with this action. Step 6: Verify Deletion After following steps 1-5 correctly, the chosen contact should now be deleted from your Kik account. Double-check your chat list to ensure they no longer appear among your contacts. Additionally, if you attempt to initiate a new conversation with the removed individual, Kik will notify you that they are not currently saved as a contact and must be added again. In conclusion, deleting people on Kik involves navigating through various screens and menus to locate the correct settings. By carefully following our step-by-step instructions, you can successfully manage your Kik contacts list and maintain control over who can communicate with you via the platform. "b3" means "b cubed", which is the same as "b" times "b" times Be careful!! Units count. Use the same units for all measurements. Examples cube = a 3 rectangular prism = a b c irregular prism = b h cylinder = b h = pi r 2 h pyramid = (1/3) b h cone = (1/3) b h = 1/3 pi r 2 h sphere = (4/3) pi r 3 ellipsoid = (4/3) pi r1 r2 r3 Volume is measured in "cubic" units. The volume of a figure is the number of cubes required to fill it completely, like blocks in a box. Volume of a cube = side times side times side. Since each side of a square is the same, it can simply be the length of one If a square has one side of 4 inches, the volume would be 4 inches times 4 inches times 4 inches, or 64 cubic inches. (Cubic inches can also be written in3.) Be sure to use the same units for all measurements. You cannot multiply feet times inches times yards, it doesn't make a perfectly cubed measurement. The volume of a rectangular prism is the length on the side times the width times the height. If the width is 4 inches, the length is 1 foot and the height is 3 feet, what is the volume? NOT CORRECT .... 4 times 1 times 3 = 12 CORRECT.... 4 inches is the same as 1/3 feet. Volume is 1/3 feet times 1 foot times 3 feet = 1 cubic foot (or 1 cu. ft., or 1 ft3). Title: How to Edit Captions on Instagram Instagram is a popular social media platform that allows users to share photos and videos with captions. Sometimes, you may want to edit the caption after posting an image or video. This tutorial will guide you through the process of editing captions on Instagram. ### Step 1: Open the Instagram App Start by opening the Instagram app on your mobile device. If you're not already logged in, enter your username and password to access your account. Key tip: Make sure you have the latest version of the Instagram app installed to ensure optimal functionality. ### Step 2: Navigate to Your Post Tap on the profile icon (a person-shaped silhouette) at the bottom right corner of the screen to go to your profile page. Locate the post whose caption you wish to edit, then tap on the picture to open it. Guideline: Remember that only posts made from your own account can be edited; you cannot edit captions for other people's posts or reposted content. ### Step 3: Access the Caption Editor To begin editing the caption, tap on the three dots ("...") located at the top right corner of the post. A menu will appear; select 'Edit.' Tip: The ellipsis button also contains options like sharing to another app, deleting the post, disabling comments, and more. Be cautious when exploring these additional features. ### Step 4: Edit the Caption Text You are now presented with the text field containing the current caption. Delete any part of the existing caption if needed using the backspace button on your keyboard. Type your new caption as required. You can add hashtags, mentions (@), line breaks, and emojis as necessary. When finished typing, hit 'Done' or the checkmark symbol depending upon your device type. Important note: Line breaks might disappear once published due to Instagram formatting. To maintain them, end each line with a period before hitting return. Also, there is no character limit for captions but keep in mind longer captions could deter some viewers. ### Step 5: Save Changes After successfully modifying the caption, press 'Share' or the airplane icon to save the changes. The updated caption will immediately become visible under the selected photo or video. Good practice: Double-check spelling and grammar before saving edits. Consider using online tools or built-in phone apps for spell checking if needed. ### Troubleshooting Common Issues * **Captions aren't changing:** Make sure you've saved the changes by pressing 'Share' or the airplane icon. Check network connectivity too - sometimes slow internet connection causes delays in updating posts. * **Can't find the 'Edit' option:** Only posts created directly via the Instagram app can be edited. Reposted content or stories converted into posts don't support caption editing. By following this comprehensive tutorial, you should feel confident about editing captions on your Instagram posts whenever necessary. Happy Gramming! Merriam Webster officially recognized the pronoun "they" as a singular use pronoun in 2019. According to the dictionary, the word had actually been used as a non-gender binary pronoun since the 1300s. Around the time Merriam Webster made this announcement, artist Sam Smith announced that their correct pronouns were actually they/them. Typically, for those who identify as female, the correct pronouns are she/her, and for males, he/him. Title: How to Call New Zealand Introduction: Making an international call can be a daunting task, especially if you've never done it before or if you're calling a country with a different dialing format like New Zealand. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to make a successful call to New Zealand, including essential tips and guidelines to ensure clear communication. Step 1: Determine the Time Difference Before making your call, find out the current time difference between your location and New Zealand. The country is 19 hours ahead of UTC (Coordinated Universal Time) during standard time and 12 hours ahead during daylight saving time. Keep this information in mind when scheduling your call, as waking someone up in the middle of the night might not yield favorable results. Key tip: Check online resources such as <https://time.is/> to confirm the exact time difference. Step 2: Identify Your Phone Carrier's International Dialing Rules Different phone carriers may have unique rules and restrictions regarding international calls. Familiarize yourself with these regulations by visiting your carrier's website or contacting their customer support line. Some providers require specific access codes or offer special rates for certain countries. Understanding these details can save you money and hassle. Guideline: Make sure to enable any necessary features or add-ons required for placing international calls through your provider. Step 3: Obtain the Recipient's Full Contact Information Ensure that you possess all relevant data about the person you want to call, including their full name, area code, and telephone number. If possible, get alternative numbers just in case you encounter issues reaching them at the primary one. Double-check every digit to avoid errors. Essential tip: When receiving a phone number from another source, verify its accuracy before attempting to call. Miscommunication could lead to incorrect digits being shared. Step 4: Format the Number Correctly To properly format the recipient's New Zealand phone number, follow this structure: +64 [Area Code] [Phone Number]. Replace "[Area Code]" with the appropriate two-digit region code and "[Phone Number]" with their seven-digit local number. For example, if the recipient lives in Auckland, the formatted number would look like this: +64 9 XXXX XXXX. Important note: Remove any leading zeros within the area code or phone number when adding the '+' sign and country code. Step 5: Place the Call Using Your Device Now that everything is set, initiate the call using your device. Depending on whether you are using a landline or mobile phone, consult your manufacturer's user manual for guidance on inputting international numbers correctly. Once connected, wait for the other party to answer and proceed accordingly. Crucial guideline: Be patient while waiting for the connection since international calls often take longer than domestic ones due to routing delays. Troubleshooting Tips: * If you experience difficulties connecting, double-check the formatting of the phone number and try again. * Consider utilizing Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) services like Skype or Google Hangouts if traditional methods fail or prove too expensive. * Remember that some public phones do not support international calls; check with the service provider beforehand. Conclusion: By following this thorough guide, you should now feel confident in making an international call to New Zealand without facing major obstacles. Always remember to respect cultural differences, observe proper etiquette, and enjoy meaningful conversations! Title: How to Do the Weirdest Things with Friends in Public Introduction: This guide is intended to help you and your friends have fun, be silly, and create memorable experiences together by doing weird things in public. These activities can help strengthen friendships, relieve stress, and add excitement to everyday life. Always remember to respect others' comfort levels and public space rules while engaging in these activities. Step 1: Brainstorm Ideas Gather your friends and spend some time brainstorming ideas for weird activities that appeal to everyone. Encourage creativity and open-mindedness during this process. Keep in mind that safety should always be a priority when planning these activities. Key Tips: * Consider each person's preferences and limitations. * Avoid activities that could cause harm or damage property. * Make sure all participants are comfortable with the chosen activities. Step 2: Choose an Appropriate Venue Select a suitable location for your activity. Parks, malls, and other public spaces often provide ample opportunities for strange behavior without causing disturbances. Be aware of any local regulations governing public conduct and ensure compliance throughout your outing. Key Guidelines: * Respect private property. * Adhere to posted rules and guidelines. * Stay within designated areas. Step 3: Dress for Success Wear clothing that allows freedom of movement and expression appropriate for the planned activities. Comfortable shoes are essential if there will be walking involved. Coordinate outfits or accessories for added humor and camaraderie. Key Tips: * Prioritize comfort and functionality. * Embrace themed attire related to the activity. * Remember weather considerations (e.g., layers for cold temperatures). Step 4: Prepare Props or Materials Some activities may require specific items or materials. Gather these beforehand to streamline the experience and avoid potential distractions or complications during execution. Clearly label shared props to prevent confusion and maintain order among participants. Key Guidelines: * Only bring necessary items. * Clearly communicate ownership of personal belongings. * Dispose of waste properly and leave no trace behind. Step 5: Practice Good Sportsmanship Maintain a positive attitude throughout the entire experience. Celebrate successes, learn from failures, and support one another as you engage in unusual antics. This will foster teamwork and contribute to lasting memories. Key Tips: * Focus on having fun rather than winning or losing. * Offer constructive feedback and encouragement. * Treat fellow participants with kindness and respect. Step 6: Document Your Adventure Capture moments of your weird adventures using cameras, smartphones, or other recording devices. Share these memories with absent friends or keep them as reminders of your unique bonding experiences. When sharing photos or videos online, exercise caution and protect privacy as needed. Key Guidelines: * Obtain consent from all parties before photographing or filming. * Use discretion when posting content online. * Preserve the authenticity of the moment by minimizing staging or editing. Conclusion: Engaging in weird activities with friends in public spaces can lead to unforgettable experiences and stronger bonds between companions. By following this guide's steps and guidelines, you can plan safe, enjoyable, and memorable encounters that showcase your group's individuality and sense of adventure. Most of the words are not distinguishable.] Title: How to Connect Your Android Device to Microsoft Outlook Microsoft Outlook is a popular email client used by many individuals and businesses worldwide. If you have an Android device, you can synchronize your emails, contacts, and calendar with Outlook using various methods. This comprehensive guide will walk you through connecting your Android to Outlook, ensuring that all your essential data stays up-to-date across both platforms. ### Method 1: Using the Built-in Email App (Recommended) Step 1: Accessing Settings On your Android device, open the 'Settings' app. It typically has a gear icon and can be found in the app drawer or on the home screen. Key Tip: Make sure your device is connected to Wi-Fi or mobile data before proceeding as this method requires internet access. Why do this? By configuring settings correctly, you ensure seamless communication between your Android device and Outlook account. Step 2: Navigate to Accounts In the Settings menu, scroll down until you find the 'Accounts' option. Tap on it. Guideline: Depending upon your device model, the location of options may vary slightly; however, they are generally easy to locate due to intuitive design principles. Step 3: Add Account Tap 'Add account' at the bottom of the page. You should now see a list of common email providers like Gmail, Yahoo Mail, etc., along with an 'Other' option. Since we want to connect our Outlook account, select 'Other'. Step 4: Configure Exchange Settings Choose 'Exchange' under Manual setup. Enter your full Outlook email address and password when prompted. Then tap 'Next.' Important Note: The term 'Exchange' refers to Microsoft's mail server protocol known as Exchange ActiveSync. Many organizations use this service for their corporate email systems. Step 5: Set Server Details For the domain field, leave it blank if unsure. Input 'outlook.office365.com' without quotes into the server field. Enable SSL (usually checked by default), then hit 'Next.' Step 6: Customizing Sync Options You will be presented with several sync options such as Contacts, Calendar, and Emails. Choose what you wish to sync, set the appropriate time frame, then press 'Done.' Congratulations! You've successfully linked your Android device to your Outlook account via the built-in Email application. All selected items - whether it be emails, contacts, or calendars - will automatically update according to your specified intervals. ### Method 2: Utilizing Microsoft's Official Outlook App Alternatively, you could download Microsoft's official Outlook app from the Google Play Store to manage your accounts more efficiently. Here's how: Step 1: Download & Installation Search for 'Outlook' in the Google Play Store, find the correct app developed by Microsoft Corporation, and install it. Step 2: Launch Application Open the newly installed Outlook app. Sign in with your Microsoft account credentials associated with your Outlook email. Step 3: Grant Permissions Grant necessary permissions so the app can function properly, including access to files, photos, media, and other data. Step 4: Configuration Follow any additional prompts within the app to configure your preferences regarding notification sounds, vibrations, etc. By following these steps, you can leverage Microsoft's dedicated Android app for managing multiple email accounts, making it easier than ever to keep track of your digital communications. Title: How to Charge Your iPhone Without a Charger Introduction: While using an original charger is always the recommended way to charge your iPhone, there might be situations where you don't have access to one. This guide will explore alternative methods to charge your iPhone when a traditional charger isn't available. Keep in mind that these are emergency solutions; they may not be as efficient or fast as using an Apple-approved charging method. Method 1: Using a USB Port Things You'll Need: 1. A computer with an active USB port 2. An authentic Lightning-to-USB cable (preferably an Apple product) Step 1: Connect Your iPhone to Computer Plug one end of the Lightning-to-USB cable into your iPhone and plug the other end into an open USB port on your computer. If possible, avoid connecting through hubs or docks, which can slow down the charging process due to shared power supply. Step 2: Check Power Settings Ensure your computer is powered on and set not to go into sleep mode while connected. On Windows, this can be adjusted under Control Panel > Hardware and Sound > Power Options. For Mac users, change settings via System Preferences > Energy Saver. Key Tips & Guidelines: - The charging speed depends on the power output of the USB port. Most standard ports provide around 0.5A, significantly slower than even older iPhones' 1A wall adapters. - Avoid disconnecting frequently during the charging cycle, as it increases conversion losses and reduces overall efficiency. Method 2: Battery Cases or External Batteries Things You'll Need: 1. iPhone compatible battery case or external battery pack 2. Authentic Lightning-to-USB cable Step 1: Attach Battery Case or Connect External Pack For battery cases, simply slide your iPhone into the case until it clicks into place. For external batteries, connect them to your phone using the provided cable. Step 2: Start Charging Press the button on the battery case/pack to initiate charging. Some models automatically start charging once attached. Monitor the status indicator lights to confirm proper connection and charging. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Make sure the accessories carry MFi certification from Apple, ensuring safety and compatibility. - Remember that some battery cases add bulk and weight to your device. Consider whether this tradeoff fits your lifestyle before purchasing. Method 3: Wireless Chargers (for Qi-enabled devices only) Things You'll Need: 1. A wireless charging pad supporting Qi technology 2. An iPhone model that supports wireless charging (iPhone 8 or later) Step 1: Place Phone onto Pad Place your iPhone face up on the center of the charging pad. Confirm alignment by checking if the screen displays 'Charging wirelessly.' Step 2: Plugin the Pad Connect the charging pad to a power source using its included AC adapter or USB cable. Note that some pads require specific adapters for optimal performance. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Not all iPhones support wireless charging natively. Only iPhone 8 and newer versions do. - Although convenient, wireless charging tends to be slower compared to wired alternatives. Conclusion: These three methods should help you out in times when you need to juice up your iPhone but don't have immediate access to a regular charger. However, remember that none match the convenience, reliability, and speed of official Apple chargers. Always prioritize using approved equipment whenever feasible. I'm not going to boss you ever again. **Understanding Logarithms: An Easy Explanation** Have you ever heard of logarithms before? They might sound intimidating, but don't worry! Once you understand what they are, they become quite straightforward. So, let's break them down together! Imagine you have a base number, say, 2. Now think about finding out how many times you need to multiply the base by itself to reach another number, like 8. In this case, you would find that you need to multiply 2 three times because 2 x 2 x 2 equals 8. So, when you see something like log\_2(8), it's asking, "What power do I raise 2 to, so that I get 8?" The answer here is 3, since 2 = 8. That's all there is to understanding basic logarithms! Now, let me show you a cool property of logarithms called the product rule. It says that when you want to find the logarithm of two numbers multiplied together, you add up their individual logarithms instead of figuring out the whole multiplication problem directly! Confusing? Let's make it clearer with an example using our base 2 again. Suppose we want to calculate log\_2(16 * 4). Instead of manually computing 16 * 4 = 64, and then trying to figure out the logarithm, we simply add the individual logarithmic values: * First, determine the logarithm of each separate number: + log\_2(16) = 4 (because 2^4 = 16) + log\_2(4) = 2 (because 2^2 = 4) * Then, just add those results: + log\_2(16 * 4) = log\_2(16) + log\_2(4) + = 4 + 2 + = 6 As it turns out, the actual value of log\_2(16 * 4) is indeed equal to 6, which agrees with our quick calculation! How convenient is that? Learning these properties will save you time while solving complex problems later on! That's enough logarithm fun for now! Keep practicing, and soon enough, you'll feel comfortable working through more involved logarithmic expressions! Do we have teeth comin' through yet? It sounded like someone was playing with something. Two friends, Mel and June, were curious and excited to find out what was making the noise. "What's that noise?" asked June. "I don't know," said Mel, "Let's go find out!" The two went searching around the room until they found the source of the noise: a pin, zipping up and down the wall! "How unusual," said June, "How does it zip up and down the wall like that? Mel put her finger to her lips and said, "Shhh, maybe it's magic?" June's wide eyes lit up as she said, "That's it! "Woo-hoo!" cried Mel, "We did it! How exciting! I think this pin has a magical zipping power!" June and Mel enjoyed their newfound discovery and magic pin. Title: How to Fold Bath Towels Like a Pro Introduction: Learning how to fold bath towels properly can help you save space in your linen closet or bathroom cabinet, while also keeping them neatly organized and easy to access when needed. This guide will walk you through the process step-by-step with clear explanations, helpful tips, and guidelines along the way. By following these simple instructions, you'll be able to master this essential life skill! Step 1: Lay out the towel flat Place the bath towel on a table, bed, or other clean surface. Make sure that there are no wrinkles or creases in the fabric as they may affect the final result. Smooth out any bumps by gently patting down the material using both hands. A well-prepared base layer ensures an easier folding experience and contributes to crisp edges once folded. Key Tip: It is best to start with a freshly laundered towel since dampness might cause mildew growth over time if improperly stored. Step 2: Fold the towel horizontally into thirds Bring one end of the towel towards the middle so that its edge aligns with the centerline of the towel. Repeat this action with the opposite end, covering about two-thirds of the width. The goal here is to create three equal sections lengthwise. Neatly aligned folds make for visually appealing stacks and facilitate efficient storage solutions. Guideline: Keep your movements steady and precise; swift motions could lead to misaligned edges or uneven folds which would compromise the overall appearance of your stacked towels. Step 3: Fold the towel vertically into halves Now take hold of one of the longer ends (the open side) and bring it up to meet the folded edge. Your towel should now resemble a rectangle shape. Performing this step correctly allows for optimal usage of available space during storage, making it easier to organize multiple towels within confined areas like shelves or cabinets. Step 4: Adjust the alignment of corners and edges Inspect the towel for any discrepancies in corner placement or edge alignment. If necessary, lightly tug at the fabric to ensure everything lines up evenly. Pay particular attention to the top and bottom edges - ensuring they run parallel enhances the professional look of your folded towels. Step 5: Finalize the fold Perform a quick once-over to check for any remaining wrinkles or imperfections before giving the towel one last pat down. Once satisfied, set aside the completed fold and repeat the process with additional towels until all are accounted for. Organizing similar items together based on size or color further promotes orderliness and ease of retrieval. Key Tips: - For added flair, consider ironing your folded towels to give them a polished hotel-like finish. However, exercise caution when doing so around delicate trims such as lace or embroidery to avoid potential damage. - To maintain folded shapes, store towels upright instead of laying them flat. Standing towels upright reduces the likelihood of creases forming due to pressure from surrounding objects. Conclusion: Congratulations! You have successfully learned how to fold bath towels professionally. With practice, this task becomes second nature, ultimately saving you time and effort while providing a neater living environment. Don't forget to share your newfound skills with friends and family members who may appreciate some organizational inspiration too! Definition of Tongue Tongue: A strong muscle that is anchored to the floor of the mouth. The tongue is covered by the lingual membrane, which has special areas to detect different types of tastes. The tongue muscles are attached to the lower jaw and to the hyoid bone, a small, U-shaped bone that lies deep in the muscles at the back of the tongue and above the larynx. On the top surface of the tongue are small nodules, called papillae, that give the tongue its rough texture. Between the papillae, at the sides and base of the tongue, are the taste buds, which are small bulb-like structures. The muscle fibers of the tongue are heavily supplied with nerves. Babies have more taste buds than adults, and they have them almost everywhere in the mouth, including the cheeks. The tongue aids in the formation of the sounds of speech and aids in swallowing.Source: MedTerms Medical Dictionary Last Editorial Review: 3/19/2012 Find out what women really need. Moai are stone statues on Easter Island. Each moai is made out of one large stone but some have an extra stone on top of the head. Most were made from the volcanic rock in the Rano Raraku area of the island. Moai are sometimes called "heads" but they do have shoulders, arms, and a body but these are usually too small for the head, also some of the best known ones had been partly buried over the years and only the heads were showing. Most moai are between 2.5 and 10 metres high and, usually, weigh 14 tonnes. There are about 1000 moai placed mainly around the coast of the island plus nearly 400 more which were left not yet finished at Rano Raraku. How many people think they really live happily ever after? Sexual reproduction is a process that can only be found in eukaryotes. These cells have a nucleus and mitochondria. There are other kinds of organisms (other than animals, plants and fungi), the other eukaryotes - such as the malaria parasite - that also engage in sexual reproduction. Some bacteria use conjugation to transfer genetic material between bacteria. This is not the same as sexual reproduction, but it results in a similar mixture of genetic traits. Title: How to Be Friends with Two People Who Hate Each Other Introduction: Maintaining friendships with two people who hate each other can be a challenging task. It requires diplomacy, tact, and excellent communication skills. This guide will provide you with steps and insights on how to navigate this tricky situation while maintaining healthy relationships with both parties involved. Step 1: Understand the Situation Analyze the nature of the conflict between the two individuals. Try to determine if their issues are temporary or deeply rooted. Consider whether your involvement could escalate tensions or contribute positively to resolving them. Remember that mediating conflicts should ideally be left to those directly involved unless they request assistance from others. * Key Tip: Respect the feelings and perspectives of all parties involved. Avoid taking sides as it may compromise your relationship with one or both individuals. Step 2: Set Boundaries Make sure each person understands that you value your friendship with them but also want to maintain a good relationship with the other party. Explain that you won't tolerate disrespectful behavior towards either of them in your presence. * Guideline: Be firm yet kind when setting these boundaries. Make sure both friends know where you stand to prevent any misunderstandings. Step 3: Keep Communication Open Ensure that conversations about each friend remain separate. Don't compare them favorably or unfavorably against each other. When discussing personal matters, ensure you're doing so privately and confidentially. * Key Tip: Always listen attentively when either friend talks about the other. Show empathy and understanding without judging or contributing negatively to the existing tension. Step 4: Encourage Healthy Communication If appropriate and safe, encourage open dialogue between the two friends. Suggest meeting up in a neutral location where they can talk things out calmly. Offer to act as a moderator if asked, but only if you believe you can handle such a responsibility objectively. * Guideline: Never force confrontation. Let the decision come from them and assure them of your support regardless of the outcome. Step 5: Manage Get-Togethers Carefully When planning social events including both friends, consider activities that don't exacerbate tensions. Opt for group settings rather than one-on-one meetings initially. If necessary, have different meetups with each individual. * Key Tip: Be honest about your intentions. Let both understand that while you enjoy spending time with each individually, seeing them together might not always be feasible due to their strained relationship. Step 6: Remind Yourself Why You Value Both Friendships It's easy to get caught up in the drama surrounding feuds. Regularly remind yourself why you appreciate these individuals as friends. Focusing on their positive qualities can help keep things in perspective. * Guideline: Maintain balance. Neither friend should feel like they're getting less attention because of the other. Conclusion: Navigating friendships amidst animosity is delicate work. By following these steps, you demonstrate respect for both parties, foster healthier communication dynamics, and protect your own wellbeing. Ultimately, remember that everyone deserves kindness and understanding, even during difficult times. Is it right to play the tape recorder? Come on peanut be my big boy. Title: How to Detect Flaky People Introduction: Flakiness is a common trait found in people who are unreliable, indecisive, or prone to making last-minute changes or cancellations. Dealing with flaky individuals can be frustrating and time-consuming. By learning how to detect flaky people early on, you can manage your expectations, protect yourself from disappointment, and maintain healthy relationships. This wiki-style guide will provide in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines to help you identify flaky behavior patterns effectively. Step 1: Observe their Communication Patterns Explanation: Pay attention to how they communicate with you. A flaky person may exhibit inconsistent communication habits such as delayed responses, frequent rescheduling, or sudden disappearance from conversations without giving proper closure. Key Tips: * Note any significant delays between their messages or calls. * Keep track of broken promises regarding response times. * Be aware if they often cancel plans at the last minute via text message instead of calling. Step 2: Analyze Reliability Explanation: Determine whether they consistently fulfill commitments made to you. Their reliability (or lack thereof) could indicate whether they're likely to be flakey in other areas of life. Key Guidelines: * Document instances where they failed to meet deadlines or keep appointments. * Reflect on past experiences - have they ever let you down unexpectedly? * Assess their commitment level towards shared goals or tasks. Step 3: Evaluate Decision-Making Abilities Explanation: Indecisiveness is another characteristic associated with flaky people. If someone frequently changes their mind about decisions, especially after agreeing upon something initially, this might signal potential flakiness. Key Tips: * Identify situations where they struggle to make choices. * Notice if they change their minds multiple times during discussions. * Monitor whether their final decision aligns with previous statements or actions. Step 4: Recognize Emotional Instability Explanation: Mood swings or emotional instability can contribute to flaky behavior. When dealing with unpredictable emotions, an individual may find it challenging to stick to agreements or maintain consistency. Key Guidelines: * Observe shifts in their mood and temperament over time. * Consider whether these fluctuations impact their ability to follow through on obligations. * Seek professional advice if necessary, particularly when concerning mental health issues. Step 5: Understand External Factors Explanation: Sometimes, external factors beyond a person's control can lead to flaky behavior. Stress, personal crises, or demanding schedules can affect one's capacity to remain consistent and reliable. Key Tips: * Learn about ongoing challenges or difficulties they face. * Offer support when possible but set boundaries to avoid being negatively affected by their circumstances. * Reassess your relationship based on new information while maintaining empathy and understanding. Conclusion: By carefully observing and analyzing various aspects of an individual's behavior, communication patterns, and decision-making abilities, you can develop a clearer picture of whether they display flaky tendencies. Armed with this knowledge, you can choose appropriate strategies for managing interactions and setting realistic expectations when engaging with them. Ultimately, understanding how to detect flaky people enables you to safeguard your own time, energy, and wellbeing in both personal and professional relationships. Solving Another Equation with Two Variables Date: 1/30/96 at 20:28:1 From: McKellar Clan Subject: how to do algebra Dear Dr. Math, I am in the 7th grade and our math class is learning algebra. I do not understand it and my teacher is no help in trying to explain it. My mother doesn't understand algebra and my father is too busy to help me. I hope you can. I have included a sample problem from my book; 6y = 3D = 54. I am supposed to solve the equation, but I don't even understand it! PLEASE HELP!!!! I really need to get a handle on this stuff. My name is Brandon. Date: 2/1/96 at 14:44:17 From: Doctor Elise Subject: Re: how to do algebra Hi! You sound really frustrated. Algebra makes a lot of people confused, especially at the beginning, but there's no magic to it. You really can learn it! I'll try to help by talking about algebra in general, and your sample problem in particular. Algebra is the next step in math after you can pretty much add, subtract, multiply, divide, and do anything else you want with actual numbers. In Algebra, instead of using a specific number, like '5', we start using a letter, like 'y', to represent a number we don't know yet. At this point, we also start leaving out the "times" sign when we write equations if we're multiplying a number "times" a letter. What '6y' really means is "six times y, which is a number I don't know yet." The goal of almost every algebra problem is to find out what number (or numbers) the letter could equal. The mathbooks usually call it "solving for y", and you've done it when your equation finally looks like "y = some number". Your example is: 6y = 3D = 54 This looks like a pretty funny equation, doesn't it? That's because...Surprise! It's really three entirely different problems. They are: 6y = 3D 3D = 54 6y = 54 Does that make a little more sense? Let's start with the third one. 6y = 54 In words, that's "six times y equals 54". The goal is to figure out what number we can plug in for 'y' that works. If you just think about it for a minute, you know the answer already, 6 * 9 = 54, right? Here's how you get there using algebra. The way you solve any algebra problem is by putting all the letters on one side of the equals sign, and all the numbers on the other. The big rule is that you can do anything you want to the equation as long as you do the same thing to both sides of the equals sign. Think about it. If I start with 2 = 2, I know this is true. What if I add 4 to both sides: 2 + 4 = 2 + 4 I get: 6 = 6 This is also true. What if I multiply both sides by 3: 6 * 3 = 6 * 3 I get: 18 = 18 Still works. Okay, what if I multiply both sides by some unknown number 'x': 18 * x = 18 * x This is still always true. Remember, in algebra we write this as: 18x = 18x Okay, what if I add 4 * x to both sides? Can I do that? Sure! 18x + 4x = 18x + 4x Works just fine. Now. Remember how you used to factor your plain old vanilla numbers? As in, 6 = 3 * 2? And if you wanted to add 6 + 4 you could write it like 6 + 4 3 * 2 + 2 * 2 2 * (3 + 2) 2 * 5 10 See, you get 10 this way, too. Well, you can do the same thing with this silly 'x' number. 18x + 4x 18 * x + 4 * x write it out the long way x * (18 + 4) pull out the 'x' x * 22 add the numbers 22x here's the answer. Of course, without an "equals" sign, we can't solve it any further. Anyhow, if you have "6y = 54", and what you want is "y = something", you just have to divide both sides by 6, right? 6y = 54 6y / 6 = 54 / 6 and we can write 6y/6 in a bunch of different ways, but it basically boils down to the exact same thing you used to do with fractions. The same way you can reduce 10/15 by factoring it into 5 * 2 / 5 * 3 and then cross out both 5's, we can write 6y/6 as 6 * y / 6 * 1 and cross out the 6's to get plain old y/1, which is 'y'. And, of course, 54/6 = 9, so we have y = 9 Yay! So, for 3D = 54 we do the same approach: 3D /3 = 54/3 D = 18 We can even use 6y = 3D to check our work. If you substitute 9 for y and 18 for D, is the equation true? I hope this helps. Good luck! -Doctor Elise, The Math Forum Search the Dr. Math Library: Ask Dr. MathTM 1994-2013 The Math Forum There it is here we'll put zebra over here. You put the doggy on the grass? Disputing a chargeback can be a complex process, but with careful attention to detail and adherence to your credit card company's policies, you can increase your chances of having the chargeback reversed. Here is an in-depth guide on how to dispute a chargeback: Step 1: Gather Documentation The first step in disputing a chargeback is gathering all relevant documentation related to the transaction. This may include receipts, invoices, emails exchanges between you and the merchant, shipping confirmations, and any other evidence that supports your claim to the validity of the charge. Having this information organized and readily available will make the rest of the process go more smoothly. Key tip: Make sure to keep copies of everything! You never know what piece of information might end up being crucial to your case. Step 2: Contact the Merchant Before filing a formal dispute with your credit card company, try reaching out to the merchant directly. Explain the situation and provide them with any necessary documents or proof of purchase. The merchant may be able to resolve the issue without involving the credit card company. Additionally, some credit card companies require customers to attempt resolution with the merchant before they will consider a dispute. Guideline: Be respectful and patient when communicating with the merchant. Remember, their goal is likely to satisfy their customer (you) while also protecting themselves from fraudulent claims. Step 3: Review Your Credit Card Company's Policies Familiarize yourself with your credit card company's specific procedures for disputes. Each bank has its own set of rules regarding time limits, required documentation, and communication methods. Understanding these guidelines will help ensure that your dispute is handled efficiently and effectively. Key tip: Many credit card companies allow you to initiate a dispute online through their website or mobile app. This can often expedite the process compared to mailing or faxing physical forms. Step 4: File a Formal Dispute If contacting the merchant does not result in a satisfactory resolution, it's time to file a formal dispute with your credit card company. Provide all requested information, including your account number, date of the disputed transaction, and reason for the dispute. Attach any supporting documentation gathered during Step 1. Guideline: Submit your dispute as soon as possible. Most credit card companies have strict deadlines for submitting disputes - typically within 60 days of receiving the initial chargeback notification. Step 5: Follow Up With Your Credit Card Company After filing a dispute, expect to wait several weeks for a decision. During this time, stay proactive by following up with your credit card company periodically to check on the status of your case. If additional information is needed, promptly provide it to prevent further delays. Key tip: Keep notes on every interaction with your credit card company, including dates, names of representatives, and details discussed. This will help maintain clear communication and demonstrate your commitment to resolving the matter. Step 6: Accept the Decision Once a decision has been made, accept the outcome graciously. If the dispute is resolved in your favor, great! However, if the decision goes against you, remember that there are still options for recourse (such as small claims court). Regardless of the outcome, maintaining a positive relationship with both the merchant and your credit card company can only benefit you in the future. Guideline: Always treat others with respect and professionalism throughout the dispute process. This approach increases the likelihood of successful resolutions and fosters goodwill for potential issues down the road. And he seems very interested in them. For a given data formula_5, this is sometimes written as formula_6. To find the median, we sort the data from the smallest number to the largest number, and then choose the number in the middle. If there is an even number of data, there will not be a number right in the middle, so we choose the two middle ones and calculate their mean. In our example above, there are 10 items of data, the two middle ones are "57" and "64", so the median is (57+64)/2 = 60.5. Once there was a girl named Liz. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in her backyard. One day, she went outside to play, but it was very quiet. She looked around and saw that all the birds had flown away. Suddenly, she heard a loud noise. It was her neighbor, Mr. Brown, who was cutting down a tree. Lily didn't like that because she loved the trees in her backyard. She went to Mr. Brown and said, "Please stop cutting the tree. I don't want it to go away." Mr. Brown said, "I'm sorry, Lily, but I have to cut it down. It's old and it might fall down." Lily didn't want to give up. She resisted and said, "But I love this tree. Can't you just leave it?" Mr. Brown thought for a moment and said, "Okay, I won't cut it down. But you have to promise to take care of it." Lily was so happy that she hugged the tree and promised to take good care of it. Each of the hearts four chambers is a blood-filled space surrounded by a wall of heart muscle. These four chambers work as four separate pumps, pushing blood around your body. Most people are born with a small, but perfectly shaped heart that grows in proportion to the rest of the body. Once you stop growing, your heart stops growing. But different conditions can cause the heart to get larger after the rest of your body stops growing. This can happen if: - The muscular wall of one or more of the chambers thickens. When this happens, its usually the wall of the left ventricle, the main chamber of the heart. - The inside space of the heart chambers expand. Sometimes both of these things happen to make the heart get bigger. Conditions that cause the heart to get larger include: - High blood pressure. The left ventricle must work harder because its trying to push blood against a higher pressure. Over time, the extra workload thickens the left ventricle wall. - A leaky heart valve. When a valve leaks, some of the blood that has just been pumped out slips back into the chamber. The backflow can cause the inside space of the chamber to get larger because its forced to hold more blood than it was designed to. - Inflammation of the heart muscle (myocarditis). A viral infection or problem with the immune system can inflame the heart muscle cells. The walls can get thicker and weaker. Weaker heart walls allow the inside space of the heart chambers to get wider. Your doctor will review your medical history, examine you and run some tests to figure out the cause. With treatment, theres a good chance your heart will stop growing. oh, you want to throw that away for me? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV1/TP/anntp1.cha = = = I wonder what kind of thing that is? John was very organized. Every morning he would bring his carpet to school. On the carpet were many special toys. They were kept in a special way so they were easy to find.
